translations.hypothesis: While *Taenia solium* eggs can infect humans, *Taenia saginata* eggs can also cause illness (by being ingested, for example, from undercooked beef). *Taenia solium* larvae penetrate the muscles, skin, eyes, and central nervous system (the human nervous system), causing a serious infection. This can have devastating effects on health. When cysts form in the brain, the condition is called neurocysticercosis. Symptoms: Taenia solium infections are typically associated with mild and non-specific symptoms, such as abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. These symptoms can persist until the larvae die. Otherwise, they can last for years.

translations.source: ለሳይሲሴርኮሲስ የመራቢያ ጊዜው ተለዋዋጭ ነው። እና በበሽታው የተያዙ ሰዎች ለዓመታት ያለ ምልክቶች ሊቆዩ ይችላሉ። በአንዳንድ የተለመዱ አካባቢዎች በበሽታው የተጠቁ ሰዎች በቆዳው ሥር የሚታዩ ወይም ተጨባጭ የሆኑ ኖዱሎች( በቆዳው ሥር ሊታዩ የሚችሉ ትንሽ ጠንካራ ጉብታ ወይም ኖድ) ሊፈጥሩ ይችላሉ። ኒውሮሲስቲካርኮሲስ በፓቶሎጂካል ለውጦች ብዛት፣ መጠን፣ ደረጃ እና ቦታ እንዲሁም በአስተናጋጁ የበሽታ መከላከያ ምላሽ እና በፓራሳይቱ ጂኖታይፕ ላይ በመመርኮዝ ከተለያዩ ምልክቶች እና ህመሞች ጋር የተቆራኘ ነው። ግን ክሊኒካዊ ምልክቶችም ሊሆኑ ይችላሉ። ምልክቶቹ ሥር የሰደደ ራስ ምታት፣ ዓይነ ስውርነት፣ የሚጥል በሽታ (የሚጥል በሽታ ተደጋጋሚ ከሆኑ)፣ ሃይድሮፋፋለስ፣ ማጅራት ገትር፣ የመርሳት በሽታ እና የማዕከላዊውን የነርቭ ሥርዓት ቦታዎችን በሚይዙ ቁስሎች ምክንያት የሚመጡ ምልክቶችን ሊያካትቱ ይችላሉ። ህክምና: የታይኒያሲስ ህክምና በታይኒያ ሶሊየም የኒውሮሲስቲካርኮሲስን ለመከላከል እና የፓራሳይት ስርጭት ዑደትን ለመቈጣጠር ወይም ለማቆም እንደ መሣሪያ አስፈላጊ ነው።
translations.reference: For cysticercosis, the incubation period is variable, and infected people may remain asymptomatic for years. In some endemic regions, infected people may develop visible or palpable nodules (a small solid bump or node which is solid that can be detected by touch) beneath the skin. Neurocysticercosis is associated with a variety of signs and symptoms depending on the number, size, stage and location of the pathological changes as well as the host’s immune response and the parasite’s genotype, but it can also be clinically asymptomatic. Symptoms may include chronic headaches, blindness, seizures (epilepsy if they are recurrent), hydrocephalus, meningitis, dementia and symptoms caused by lesions occupying spaces of the central nervous system. Treatment: The treatment of taeniasis by Taenia solium is important to prevent neurocysticercosis and as a tool to assist in controlling or stopping the parasite transmission cycle.
translations.hypothesis: The duration of neurocysticercosis varies. People with the disease may experience symptoms for years. In some cases, individuals may develop visible or palpable nodules (small, firm bumps or lumps) under the skin. Neurocysticercosis is characterized by a variety of signs and symptoms, depending on the number, size, location, and severity of pathological changes, as well as the host's immune response and the parasite's genotype. However, clinical signs can also occur. These may include headache, blurred vision, seizures, hydrocephalus, chiasmal edema, memory impairment, and other neurological symptoms. Treatment: Treatment for neurocysticercosis typically involves antihelminthic medications to prevent or control the spread of Taenia solium larvae.

translations.source: ህክምናው በግለሰብ ደረጃ ሊከናወን ይችላል። ወይም በአከባቢው ሁኔታ እና በተተገበሩ የቁጥጥር አቀራረቦች ላይ በመመርኮዝ እንደ የጅምላ መድኃኒት አስተዳደር ሊከናወን ይችላል። ታኒያሲስ አንቲልሚንቲክ( ፕራዚኳንቴል፣ ኒኮሎሳሚድ፣ አልቤንዳዞል) በመጠቀም ሊታከም ይችላል። በኒውሮሲስቲካርኮሲስ ውስጥ፣ የሲስትዎች መጥፋት ወደ እብጠት ምላሽ ሊመራ ስለሚችል፣ ንቁ በሽታ ልዩ ህክምና ያስፈልጋል እና ከፍተኛ መጠን ያለው የፀረ-ተባይ መድኃኒት ረጅም ጊዜዎችን እንዲሁም በኮርቲኮስቴሮይድ እና/ ወይም በፀረ- ነቀርሳ መድኃኒቶች እና ምናልባትም በቀዶ ጥገና የሚደረግ የድጋፍ ህክምና ሊያካትት ይችላል የመድኃኒቱ መጠን እና የህክምናው ጊዜ በከፍተኛ ሁኔታ ሊለያይ ይችላል እናም በዋነኝነት የሚወሰነው በሲስትዎች ብዛት፣ መጠን፣ ቦታ እና የዕድገት ደረጃ፣ በአከባቢው ባለው ያለው ብግነት እብጠት፣ ከባድነት እና ክሊኒካዊ ምልክቶች ወይም ምልክቶች ላይ ነው።
translations.reference: The treatment can be done on an individual bases, or as Mass Drug Administration depending on the local circumstances and the control approaches being implemented. Taeniasis can be treated using anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). In neurocysticercosis, since the destruction of cysts may lead to an inflammatory response, specialised treatment of active disease is required and may include long courses with high doses of anthelmintic, as well as supporting therapy with corticosteroids and/or anti-epileptic drugs, and possibly surgery. The dosage and the duration of treatment can vary greatly and depend mainly on the number, size, location and developmental stage of the cysts, their surrounding inflammatory edema, acuteness and severity of clinical symptoms or signs.
translations.hypothesis: The treatment can be administered individually or as a mass medication program, depending on the severity of the infection and the available resources. Taniaomycin (pyrazinamide, niclosamide, albendazole) is an effective treatment option. In neurocysticercosis, the cysts can cause inflammation, so specific treatment is needed to eliminate the cysts. This may include a combination of anti-parasitic medications, corticosteroids, and/or anti-inflammatory drugs, as well as surgical intervention. The dosage and duration of treatment can vary widely, depending on the number, size, location, and stage of the cysts, as well as the severity of the inflammation and the presence of clinical symptoms.

translations.source: ቴታነስ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ ቴታነስ በአፈር፣ ምራቅ፣ አቧራ እና ፋንድያ ውስጥ ለሚኖሩ የባክቴሪያ፣ ክሎስትሪዲየም ቴታኒ ስፖሮች በመጋለጥ የሚመጣ ከባድ በሽታ ነው። ባክቴሪያው የነርቭ ሥርዓትን በሚጎዳ ጥልቅ መቍረጥ፣ ቁስሎች ወይም ቃጠሎዎች ወደ ሰውነት ውስጥ ሊገባ ይችላል። ኢንፌክሽኑ በተለይ የጉንጭ እና የአንገት ጡንቻዎች ሕመም የሚያስከትሉ የጡንቻ መጨናነቆችን ያስከትላል እና በተለምዶ "መንጋጋ ቆልፍ" በመባል ይታወቃል። በሁሉም የዕድሜ ክልል ውስጥ ያሉ ሰዎች ቴታኑስ ሊይዙ ይችላሉ ነገር ግን በሽታው በተለይ በተወለዱ ሕፃናት እና እናቶቻቸው ላይ በጣም የተለመደ እና ከባድ ነው እናት በክትባት፣ በቴታኑስ ቶክሶይድ ከቴታኑስ ካልተጠበቀች።
translations.reference: Tetanus Overview: Tetanus is a serious illness contracted through exposure to the spores of the bacterium, Clostridium tetani, which live in soil, saliva, dust and manure. The bacteria can enter the body through a deep cuts, wounds or burns affecting the nervous system. The infection leads to painful muscle contractions, particularly of the jaw and neck muscle, and is commonly known as “lockjaw”. People of all ages can get tetanus but the disease is particularly common and serious in newborn babies and their mothers when the mother is unprotected from tetanus by the vaccine, tetanus toxoid.
translations.hypothesis:
Tetanus: A Comprehensive Overview

Tetanus is a serious bacterial infection that occurs when spores of the bacterium *Clostridium tetani* enter the body through a break in the skin, such as a cut, wound, abrasion, or puncture. The bacteria produce a potent toxin that attacks the nervous system, leading to muscle spasms, stiffness, and pain. The infection typically affects the muscles of the neck and jaw, causing the characteristic "lockjaw."

While tetanus can occur in people of all ages, it is most common in infants and young children, as well as in adults who have not been vaccinated. Vaccination is the most effective way to prevent tetanus.

translations.source: በእርግዝና ወቅት ወይም እርግዝናው ካለቀ በ6 ሳምንታት ውስጥ የሚከሰተው ቴታነስ የእናቶች ቴታነስ ተብሎ የሚጠራ ሲሆን በመጀመሪያዎቹ 28 ቀናት ውስጥ የሚከሰተው ቴታነስ የአራስ ቴታነስ ይባላል። በሽታው በብዙ የዓለም ክፍሎች ጠቃሚ የህብረተሰብ ጤና ችግር ሆኖ ቆይቷል ነገር ግን በተለይ ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት ወይም ወረዳዎች የክትባት ሽፋን ዝቅተኛ እና ንፁህ ያልሆኑ የወሊድ ልምዶች የተለመዱ ናቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2018 (ግምት የሚገኝበት የመጨረሻ ዓመት) 25 000 አዲስ የተወለዱ ሕፃናት በአራስ ቴታነስ ሞተዋል ፣ በ 2000 ከነበረው ሁኔታ 88% ቀንሷል። ምልክቶች: በአብዛኛዎቹ የቴታነስ በሽታዎች የሚከሰቱት በጭራሽ ክትባት ባልተሰጣቸው ሰዎች ወይም በተከታታይ ክትባት ባልወሰዱ አዋቂዎች ላይ ነው። የቴታነስ ምልክቶች የሚታዩት የቴታነስ ባክቴሪያ በቁስል ወደ ሰውነት ከገባ ከ3-21 ቀናት ባለው ጊዜ ውስጥ ነው።
translations.reference: Tetanus occurring during pregnancy or within 6 weeks of the end of pregnancy is called maternal tetanus, while tetanus occurring within the first 28 days of life is called neonatal tetanus. The disease remains an important public health problem in many parts of the world, but especially in low-income countries or districts, where immunization coverage is low and unclean birth practices are common. WHO estimates that in 2018 (the latest year for which estimates are available), 25 000 newborns died from neonatal tetanus, 88% reduction from the situation in 2000. Symptoms: Nearly all cases of tetanus occur in people who have never been vaccinated or in adults who have not kept up to date on their booster shots. Signs and symptoms of tetanus appear anytime from 3 to 21 days after tetanus bacteria enter the body through a wound.
translations.hypothesis: Puerperal tetanus, also known as maternal tetanus, occurs during or within six weeks after childbirth. It is different from neonatal tetanus, which occurs in the first 28 days of life. This disease has been a significant public health problem in many parts of the world, but it is particularly prevalent in low-income countries or communities with limited access to vaccination and safe delivery practices. According to the World Health Organization, in 2018, an estimated 25,000 newborns died from neonatal tetanus, a significant decrease from 2000 when an estimated 88,000 deaths occurred. Symptoms: Most cases of tetanus occur in people who have not been vaccinated or who have not received booster vaccinations. The symptoms of tetanus typically appear 3-21 days after the tetanus bacteria enters the body through a wound.

translations.source: አብዛኛዎቹ ጉዳዮች በ 14 ቀናት ውስጥ ይከሰታሉ። ምልክቶቹ የሚከተሉትን ሊያካትቱ ይችላሉ- መንጋጋ መኮማተር ወይም አፍን መክፈት አለመቻል ብዙውን ጊዜ በጀርባ፣ በሆድ እና በየሰውነት ክፍሎች ውስጥ የጡንቻዎች እብጠት። ድንገተኛ ህመም የሚሰማቸው የጡንቻ ህመሞች ብዙውን ጊዜ በድንገተኛ ጫጫታ ይነሳል
translations.reference: Most cases occur within 14 days. Symptoms can include: jaw cramping or the inability to open the mouth muscle spasms often in the back, abdomen and extremities sudden painful muscle spasms often triggered by sudden noises
translations.hypothesis: Most cases develop within 14 days. Symptoms may include: difficulty or inability to urinate, muscle aches or spasms in the back, abdomen, and other parts of the body. Sudden muscle pain is often associated with a sudden onset of pain.

translations.source: የመዋጥ ችግር መናድ(እንፍርፍሪት) ራስ ምታት ትኩሳት እና ላብ የደም ግፊት ለውጦች ወይም ፈጣን የልብ ምት።
translations.reference: trouble swallowing seizures headache fever and sweating changes in blood pressure or fast heart rate.
translations.hypothesis: Symptoms of a migraine include severe headache, fever, nausea, vomiting, and changes in vision or heart rate.

translations.source: በአራስ ሕፃናት ቴታኑስ ውስጥ ምልክቶቹ የጡንቻ መጨናነቅን ያካትታሉ፣ ይህም ብዙውን ጊዜ አራስ ሕፃኑ ለመምጠጥ ወይም ጡት ለማጥባት አለመቻል እና ከመጠን በላይ ማልቀስ ነው። ቴታኑስ የሚታወቀው በክሊኒካዊ ባህሪያት ላይ በመመርኮዝ ሲሆን የላብራቶሪ ማረጋገጫ አያስፈልገውም። ቴታነስ በሕክምና ተቋም ውስጥ፣ ብዙ ጊዜ በሪፈራል ሆስፒታል ውስጥ ሕክምናን ይፈልጋል። ከቴታነስ ያገገሙ ሰዎች ተፈጥሯዊ መከላከያ ስለሌላቸው እንደገና ሊበከሉ ስለሚችሉ የበሽታ መከላከያ ያስፈልጋቸዋል። መከላከል፡ ቴታነስን መከላከል የሚቻለው ቴታነስ ቶክሳይድ የያዙ ክትባቶችን (ቲቲሲቪ) በመከተብ ሲሆን ይህም በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ በመደበኛ የክትባት መርሃ ግብሮች ውስጥ የተካተቱ እና በቅድመ ወሊድ እንክብካቤ ወቅት የሚደረጉ ናቸው።
translations.reference: In neonatal tetanus, symptoms include muscle spasms, which are often preceded by the newborn’s inability to suck or breastfeed, and excessive crying. Tetanus is diagnosed on the basis of clinical features and does not require laboratory confirmation. Tetanus requires treatment in a medical facility, often in a referral hospital. People who recover from tetanus do not have natural immunity and can be infected again and therefore need to be immunized. Prevention: Tetanus can be prevented through immunization with tetanus-toxoid-containing vaccines (TTCV), which are included in routine immunization programmes globally and administered during antenatal care contacts.
translations.hypothesis: Symptoms of tetanus in infants include muscle spasms, which often make it difficult or impossible for the infant to suck or feed. Tetanus is typically diagnosed based on clinical signs, and laboratory testing is not usually required. Treatment for tetanus usually takes place in a hospital setting, such as a referral hospital. Because individuals who have recovered from tetanus do not have natural immunity, they are at risk of recurrence and may require booster vaccinations. Prevention: Tetanus can be prevented by administering tetanus toxoid vaccines (Td), which are part of routine immunization programs worldwide and are typically given during prenatal care.

translations.source: በሕይወት ዘመኑ ሁሉ ለመጠበቅ፣ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት አንድ ግለሰብ 6 መጠኖች (3 ዋና እና 3 የማበልጸጊያ መጠኖች) ቲቲሲቪ እንዲወስድ ይመክራል። የ 3 መጠን የመጀመሪያ ተከታታይ ከ 6 ሳምንታት ዕድሜ ጀምሮ መጀመር አለበት፣ ከዚያ በኋላ የሚቀጥሉት መጠኖች ቢያንስ በ 4 ሳምንታት መካከል ባለው ልዩነት ይሰጣሉ። 3ቱ ማበልጸጊያ ክትባቶች በሁለተኛው የህይወት አመት (12-23 ወራት), ከ4-7 አመት እና ከ9-15 አመት እድሜ ውስጥ መሰጠት አለበት። በሐሳብ ደረጃ በመድኃኒቱ ማጠናከሪያ መጠን መካከል ቢያንስ 4 ዓመት መሆን አለበት። ቴታነስን ለመከላከል የሚያገለግሉ ብዙ አይነት ክትባቶች አሉ፡- ዲፍቴሪያ እና ቴታነስ (ዲቲ) ክትባቶች ዲፍቴሪያ፣ ቴታነስ እና ፐርቱሲስ (ትክትክ ሳል) (ዲቲኤፒ) ክትባቶች ስቴታነስ እና ዲፍቴሪያ (ቲዲ) ክትባቶች ስቴታነስ፣ ዲፍቴሪያ እና ፐርቱሲስ (ቲዳፕ) ክትባቶች።
translations.reference: To be protected throughout life, WHO recommends that an individual receives 6 doses (3 primary plus 3 booster doses) of TTCV. The 3-dose primary series should begin as early as 6 weeks of age, with subsequent doses given with a minimum interval of 4 weeks between doses. The 3 booster doses should preferably be given during the second year of life (12–23 months), at 4–7 years of age, and at 9–15 years of age. Ideally, there should be at least 4 years between booster doses. There are many kinds of vaccines used to protect against tetanus: diphtheria and tetanus (DT) vaccinesdiphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis (whooping cough) (DTaP) vaccinestetanus and diphtheria (Td) vaccinestetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis (Tdap) vaccines.
translations.hypothesis: To ensure lifelong protection, the World Health Organization recommends that an individual receive six doses of the tetanus vaccine (three primary doses and three booster doses). The first primary dose should begin at 6 months of age, followed by the remaining doses at least 4 weeks apart. The three booster doses should be administered at 12-23 months, 4-7 years, and 9-15 years of age. There should be at least 4 years between each dose of the vaccine. There are several different types of tetanus vaccines available, including: Diphtheria and tetanus (DT) vaccines, which protect against diphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis (whooping cough); Tetanus and diphtheria (Td) vaccines, which protect against tetanus and diphtheria; and Tetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis (Tdap) vaccines, which protect against tetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis.

translations.source: የአራስ ቴታነስን በእርግዝና ወቅትም ሆነ ከእርግዝና ውጪ በመውለድ እድሜ ላይ ያሉ ሴቶችን በቲቲሲቪ በመከተብ መከላከል ይቻላል። በተጨማሪም ጠንካራ የህክምና ልምዶች የቲታኑስ በሽታን መከላከል ይችላሉ፣ ይህም ንጹሕ መላኪያ እና በወሊድ ወቅት የሽቦውን እንክብካቤ፣ እና የቀዶ ጥገና እና የጥርስ ህክምና ሂደቶች ተገቢ የሆነ የቍስል እንክብካቤን ያካትታል።
translations.reference: Neonatal tetanus can be prevented by immunizing women of reproductive age with TTCV, either during pregnancy or outside of pregnancy. Additionally, robust medical practices can also prevent tetanus disease including clean delivery and cord care during childbirth, and proper wound care for surgical and dental procedures.
translations.hypothesis: Tetanus vaccination, particularly during pregnancy and in women of childbearing age, can help prevent tetanus. Additionally, strong medical practices, including proper wound care, newborn care, and dental and surgical procedures, can also help prevent tetanus.

translations.source: መዥገር-ወለድ ኢንሰፍላይትስ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡- መዥገር-ወለድ ኢንሰፍላይትስ(ቲቢኢ) በማዕከላዊው የነርቭ ሥርዓት በምስራቅ፣ በማዕከላዊ፣ በሰሜናዊ እና ከጊዜ ወደ ጊዜ እየጨመረ በመጣው የምዕራብ አውሮፓ አገሮች እንዲሁም በሰሜን ቻይና፣ ሞንጎሊያ እና የሩሲያ ፌዴሬሽን የማዕከላዊው የነርቭ ሥርዓት የቫይረስ ኢንፌክሽን ምክንያት ነው። መዥገር-ወለድ የኢንሰፍላይትስ ቫይረስ የፍላቪቪሪዳ ቤተሰብ አባል ነው። በየአመቱ በግምት 10 000-12,000 ክሊኒካዊ መዥገር-ወለድ የኢንሰፍላይትስ በሽታዎች ሪፖርት ይደረጋሉ ፣ ግን ይህ አሃዝ ከጠቅላላው የክሊኒካዊ ጉዳዮች ብዛት በእጅጉ ያነሰ ነው ተብሎ ይታመናል። በቫይረሱ የተያዙት አብዛኛዎቹ ኢንፌክሽኖች የሚመነጩት በተለከፉ መዥገሮች ሲሆን ይህም ብዙውን ጊዜ ለቀናት ከቆዳ ጋር ተጣብቆ ይቆያል።
translations.reference: Tick-borne encephalitis Overview: Tick-borne encephalitis (TBE) is an important cause of viral infections of the central nervous system in eastern, central, northern and increasingly western European countries, and in northern China, Mongolia, and the Russian Federation. Tick-borne encephalitis virus is a member of the family Flaviviridae. Approximately 10 000–12 000 clinical cases of tick-borne encephalitis are reported each year, but this figure is believed to be significantly lower than the actual total number of clinical cases. The vast majority of infections with the virus result from infected ticks, which often remain firmly attached to the skin for days.
translations.hypothesis: Overview of Measles-Associated Encephalitis: Measles-associated encephalitis (MAE) is a neurological condition caused by a viral infection of the central nervous system. It is most commonly seen in Eastern Europe, Central Asia, and increasingly in Western Europe, as well as in Northern China, Mongolia, and the Russian Federation. The virus responsible for MAE is a member of the Flaviviridae family. Each year, an estimated 10,000-12,000 cases of measles-associated encephalitis are reported, although this number is significantly lower than the total number of measles cases. Most of the infections are thought to be caused by the virus itself, which can spread from the respiratory tract to the brain.

translations.source: አልፎ አልፎ በበሽታው ከተያዙ ፍየሎች፣ በጐች ወይም ላሞች ባልበሰለ ወተት ምክንያት ኢንፌክሽኑ ሊከሰት ይችላል። ሰዎች ከ 2000 ሜትር ከፍታ በላይ በሚገኙ የደን አካባቢዎች ከቤት ውጭ በሚያደርጉ እንቅስቃሴዎች ከመዥገሮች ጋር ይገናኛሉ። በቀጥታ ከሰው ወደ ሰው የሚተላለፍ የለም። ምልክቶች፡ አብዛኞቹ ኢንፌክሽኖች ምንም ምልክት ሳያሳዩ ይቀራሉ። በክሊኒካዊ ህመም ፣ መዥገር ወለድ የኢንሰፍላይትስ በሽታ የመታቀፉ ጊዜ ከ2-28 ቀናት ይቆያል (በተለምዶ ከ7-14 ቀናት) እና ከ1-8 ቀናት በኋላ አጠቃላይ የጉንፋን ምልክቶች እንደ ድካም ፣ ራስ ምታት እና አጠቃላይ ህመም ፣ ብዙውን ጊዜ ይከተላል። ከ ≥38 ° ሴ ትኩሳት ጋር ተዳምሮ።
translations.reference: On rare occasions, infection can result from consumption of unpasteurized milk from infect goats, sheep or cows. People come in contact with the ticks during outdoor activities in forested areas up to an altitude of about 2000 meters. There is no direct person-to-person transmission. Symptoms: Most infections remain asymptomatic. In case of clinical illness, the incubation period for tick-borne encephalitis lasts 2–28 days (most commonly 7–14 days) and is followed by 1–8 days of general cold symptoms, such as fatigue, headache and general malaise, usually combined with fever of ≥38 °C.
translations.hypothesis: The infection can occur in cattle, sheep, or goats due to contamination of their milk. People are exposed to the virus through contact with infected animals, particularly in areas above 2,000 meters, such as forests. The virus is not transmitted directly from person to person. Symptoms: Most infections are asymptomatic. If symptoms do occur, they typically appear between 2 and 28 days after exposure (usually within 7-14 days), and are followed by a period of fever, headache, and general malaise.

translations.source: ከ1-20 ቀናት ውስጥ ምንም ምልክት ሳይታይበት ከቆየ በኋላ እስከ 15% የሚደርሱ ታካሚዎች የበሽታው ሁለተኛ ደረጃ ያጋጥማቸዋል ትኩሳት በተደጋጋሚ ከ 40 ዲግሪ ሴንቲግሬድ በላይ የሆነ ትኩሳት እና እንደ ማጅራት ገትር (ለምሳሌ ትኩሳት፣ ራስ ምታት እና ደረቅ አንገት)፣ ኤንሰፍላይትስ (ለምሳሌ፣ ድብታ፣ ግራ መጋባት እና የስሜት መረበሽ)፣ ማይላይላይትስ ወይም ራዲኩላላይዝስ። በዚህ በሁለተኛው ደረጃ ላይ የሚከሰት ኢንሴፍላይተስ ሽባነትን፣ ዘላቂ መዘዞችን ወይም ሞትን ሊያስከትል ይችላል። የነርቭ በሽታ ካለባቸው ሰዎች መካከል 1% የሚሆኑት ሊሞቱ ይችላሉ፤ ከፍ ያለ የሞት መጠን ከሩሲያ ፌዴሬሽን ሪፖርት ተደርጓል፣ ይህም ከተለየ የቫይረስ ንዑስ ዓይነት ጋር የተዛመደ ሊሆን ይችላል። የበሽታው ከባድነት ከታካሚው ዕድሜ ጋር ይጨምራል፣ ግን ከሁሉም የዕድሜ ቡድኖች ሞት ሪፖርት ተደርጓል። መዥገር-ወለድ የኢንሰፍላይትስ በሽታ የተለየ ሕክምና የለም።...
translations.reference: After an asymptomatic interval of 1–20 days up to 15% of patients experience a second phase of the disease characterized by fever frequently exceeding 40 °C and signs of central nervous system involvement, such as meningitis (e.g. fever, headache, and a stiff neck), encephalitis (e.g., drowsiness, confusion, and sensory disturbances), myelitis or radiculitis. Encephalitis developing during this second phase may result in paralysis, permanent sequelae or death. About 1% of cases with neurological pathologies may die; higher fatality rates have been reported from the Russian federation, which may be related to a different virus subtype. Severity of illness increases with age of the patient, but fatalities have been reported from all age groups. There is no specific treatment for tick-borne encephalitis.
translations.hypothesis: Up to 15% of patients who experience no symptoms for 1-20 days may develop a secondary stage of the disease, characterized by recurrent fever of over 40 degrees Celsius, and symptoms such as headache, dry mouth, nausea, vomiting, dizziness, and confusion. This secondary stage can lead to complications such as encephalitis, which can cause permanent brain damage or death. In individuals with pre-existing neurological conditions, the risk of death is higher, with a reported mortality rate of 1% in Russia, potentially linked to a specific viral strain. The severity of the disease increases with age, but cases have been reported in all age groups. There is no specific treatment for encephalitis.

translations.source: መከላከል፦ ሰዎች ለአደጋ በተጋለጡ አገሮች ወይም አካባቢዎች በእግር በሚጓዙበት ወይም በካምፕ በሚያርፉበት ጊዜ ረዥም ሱሪዎችንና የተዘጋ ጫማዎችን ጨምሮ ተገቢውን ልብስ በመልበስ ራሳቸውን ከመዥገር መከላከል ይችላሉ። መላ ሰውነት በየቀኑ መፈተሽ እና የተጣበቁ መዥገሮች በተቻለ ፍጥነት መወገድ አለባቸው። በእነዚህ አካባቢዎች ፓስተር ያልሆኑ የወተት ተዋጽኦዎችን ከመመገብ መቆጠብ ያስፈልጋል። የበሽታ መከላከያ ክትባት በጣም ውጤታማውን መከላከያ ያቀርባል። በአሁኑ ጊዜ፣ የተረጋገጠ ጥራት ያላቸው 4 በስፋት ጥቅም ላይ የዋሉ ክትባቶች አሉ።
translations.reference: Prevention: People can protect themselves from ticks by wearing appropriate clothing, including long trousers and closed footwear, when hiking or camping in countries or areas at risk. The whole body should be inspected daily and attached ticks removed as soon as possible. The consumption of unpasteurized dairy products should also be avoided in those areas. Immunization offers the most effective protection. Currently, there are 4 widely used vaccines of assured quality:
translations.hypothesis: Prevention: People living in or traveling through areas with outbreaks of cholera can protect themselves by wearing appropriate clothing, including long pants and closed-toe shoes, while walking or staying in camps. It is important to wash the entire body daily and remove any visible ticks as quickly as possible. Avoid consuming unpasteurized milk and dairy products in these areas. Vaccination is the most effective form of protection. Currently, there are four widely used and effective vaccines available.

translations.source: ኤፍኤስኤምኢ_ኢምዩን እና ኢንስፑር፣ በቅደም ተከተል በኦስትሪያ እና በጀርመን የተመረተ እና በአውሮፓ የቫይረሱ ዝርያዎች ላይ የተመሰረተ፤ እና ቲቢኢ-ሞስኮ እና ኢንሴቨር, በሩሲያ ፌዴሬሽን ውስጥ የተመረተ እና በሩቅ-ምስራቅ ዝርያዎች ላይ የተመሰረተ. 4ቱ ክትባቶች አስተማማኝ እና ውጤታማ እንደሆኑ ይቆጠራሉ። በሽታው ሥር በሰደደባቸው አካባቢዎች፣ ሕፃናትን ጨምሮ በሁሉም የዕድሜ ክልሎች ውስጥ ክትባት እንዲሰጥ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ይመክራል። በተጨማሪም መዥገሮች የባክቴሪያ በሽታ የሆነውን ቦሬሊዮሲስ( የላይም በሽታ) ያስተላልፋሉ።
translations.reference: FSME-Immun and Encepur, manufactured in Austria and Germany respectively, and based on European strains of the virus; and TBE-Moscow and EnceVir, manufactured in the Russian Federation and based on Far-Eastern strains. The 4 vaccines are considered to be safe and effective. In areas where the disease is highly endemic, WHO recommends that vaccination be offered to all age groups, including children. Ticks also transmit Borreliosis (Lyme disease), which is a bacterial infection.
translations.hypothesis: The four vaccines, namely, Fluenz, Imvanex, and Innovax (all manufactured in Austria and Germany and based on European viral strains), and TBE-Mosco and Enseve (manufactured in the Russian Federation and based on Far-Eastern strains), are considered to be reliable and effective. The World Health Organization recommends that the vaccines be administered to people in areas where the disease is prevalent, including children of all ages. Furthermore, the ticks that transmit the disease can also transmit Borreliosis, a bacterial infection.

translations.source: የቲቢኢ ክትባት በዚህ በሽታ ላይ ውጤታማ አይደለም, ነገር ግን በፀረ-ተባይ መድሃኒቶች ሊታከም ይችላል.
translations.reference: TBE vaccination is not effective against this disease, which however is treatable with antimicrobials.
translations.hypothesis: While TBI (traumatic brain injury) is not effectively treated with antibiotics, it can be managed with other medications.

translations.source: ትምባሆ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡- በትምባሆ ውስጥ ያለው ኒኮቲን በጣም ሱስ የሚያስይዝ ሲሆን ትንባሆ መጠቀም ለልብና የደም ቧንቧና የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታዎች፣ ከ20 በላይ የተለያዩ የካንሰር አይነቶች ወይም ንዑስ ዓይነቶች እና ሌሎች ብዙ የሚያዳክም የጤና እክሎች ዋነኛ አደጋ ነው። በየዓመቱ ከ8 ሚሊዮን በላይ ሰዎች በትምባሆ ምክንያት ይሞታሉ። አብዛኛው ከትንባሆ ጋር የተያያዘ ሞት የሚከሰቱት ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች ውስጥ ነው፣ እነዚህም ብዙውን ጊዜ ከፍተኛ የትምባሆ ኢንዱስትሪ ጣልቃ ገብነት እና ግብይት ኢላማ ናቸው። ትንባሆ ለማያጨሱ ሰዎች ገዳይ ሊሆን ይችላል።
translations.reference: Tobacco Overview: Nicotine contained in tobacco is highly addictive and tobacco use is a major risk factor for cardiovascular and respiratory diseases, over 20 different types or subtypes of cancer, and many other debilitating health conditions. Every year, more than 8 million people die from tobacco use. Most tobacco-related deaths occur in low- and middle-income countries, which are often targets of intensive tobacco industry interference and marketing. Tobacco can also be deadly for non-smokers.
translations.hypothesis: Overall view of tobacco: Nicotine in tobacco is highly addictive, and using tobacco is a major risk factor for heart and blood vessel diseases, over 20 different types of cancer, and numerous other health problems. More than 8 million people die each year from tobacco-related causes, and the majority of these deaths occur in low- and middle-income countries, where tobacco industries often have a strong influence and target vulnerable populations. Tobacco use can be deadly for smokers.

translations.source: የሁለተኛ እጅ ጭስ መጋለጥ በአሉታዊ የጤና ውጤቶች ላይም ተካትቷል፣ ይህም በየዓመቱ 1.2 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች ይሞታሉ። ከሁሉም ህፃናት ግማሽ ያህሉ በትንባሆ ጭስ የተበከለ አየር ይተነፍሳሉ እና 65,000 ህጻናት ከሁለተኛ እጅ ጭስ ጋር በተያያዙ በሽታዎች ይሞታሉ። በእርግዝና ወቅት ማጨስ በሕፃናት ላይ በርካታ የዕድሜ ልክ የጤና ችግሮች ሊያስከትል ይችላል። የሚሞቁ የትምባሆ ምርቶች( ኤችቲፒስ) ትንባሆ ይይዛሉ እና ተጠቃሚዎችን ለመርዛማ ልቀቶች ያጋልጣሉ፣ ከእነዚህ ውስጥ ብዙዎቹ ካንሰርን ያስከትላሉ እንዲሁም ለጤንነት ጐጂ ናቸው። ኤሌክትሮኒክ የኒኮቲን ማቅረቢያ ሥርዓቶች( ኢኤንዲስ) እና ኤሌክትሮኒክ ኒኮቲን ያልሆኑ ማቅረቢያ ሥርዓቶች( ኢኤንኤንዲስ)፣ በተለምዶ ኢ-ሲጋራዎች በመባል የሚታወቁት፣ ትንባሆ አይይዙም እና ኒኮቲን ሊይዙ ወይም ላይይዙ ይችላሉ፣ ግን ለጤንነት ጐጂ እና ያለ ጥርጥር አደገኛ ናቸው።
translations.reference: Second-hand smoke exposure has also been implicated in adverse health outcomes, causing 1.2 million deaths annually. Nearly half of all children breathe air polluted by tobacco smoke and 65 000 children die each year due to illnesses related to second-hand smoke. Smoking while pregnant can lead to several life-long health conditions for babies. Heated tobacco products (HTPs) contain tobacco and expose users to toxic emissions, many of which cause cancer and are harmful to health. Electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS) and electronic non-nicotine delivery systems (ENNDS), commonly known as e-cigarettes, do not contain tobacco and may or may not contain nicotine, but are harmful to health and undoubtedly unsafe.
translations.hypothesis: Secondhand smoke also has negative health consequences, contributing to 1.2 million deaths each year. Half of all children breathe air contaminated with tobacco smoke, and 65,000 children die each year from diseases linked to secondhand smoke. Smoking during pregnancy can lead to a number of health problems in children. Tobacco products (including e-cigarettes) contain nicotine and other harmful chemicals that can damage the body, including causing cancer. Electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS), commonly known as e-cigarettes, do not contain nicotine, but they can deliver nicotine or other harmful chemicals to the user.

translations.source: ነገር ግን፣ በኤችቲፒ እና/ወይም ኢ-ሲጋራ አጠቃቀም የረዥም ጊዜ ተጽእኖ ላይ ግልጽ የሆነ መልስ ለመስጠት በጣም ገና ነው። ተፅዕኖ፡ በዓለም ዙሪያ 1.3 ቢሊዮን ሰዎች የትምባሆ ምርቶችን ይጠቀማሉ፣ 80% የሚሆኑት ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች ውስጥ ናቸው። ትንባሆ መጠቀም የቤተሰብ ወጪን ከመሰረታዊ ፍላጎቶች ማለትም ከምግብ እና መጠለያ ወደ ትምባሆ በማዞር ለድህነት አስተዋፅኦ ያደርጋል። ትንባሆ ሱስ የሚያስይዝ ስለሆነ ይህን የወጪ ባህሪ ለመቆጣጠር አስቸጋሪ ነው። እንዲሁም በቤተሰቦች ውስጥ በአምራችነት ዕድሜ ላይ የሚገኙ ጎልማሶች ያለጊዜው እንዲሞቱ እና የአካል ጉዳትን ስለሚያስከትል የቤተሰብ ገቢ እንዲቀንስ እና የጤና እንክብካቤ ወጪን ይጨምራል።
translations.reference: However, it is too early to provide a clear answer on the long-term impact of HTPs and/or e-cigarette use. Impact: An estimated 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, 80% of whom are in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by diverting household spending from basic needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This spending behaviour is difficult to curb because tobacco is so addictive. It also causes premature death and disability of productive age adults in households thus leading to reduced household income and increased healthcare costs.
translations.hypothesis: However, there is no clear answer to the long-term effects of e-cigarettes and/or vaping. The impact: Approximately 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, with 80% of them in low- and middle-income countries. The use of tobacco can divert household spending away from basic needs, such as food and shelter, contributing to poverty. Furthermore, the addictive nature of tobacco makes it difficult to control this spending pattern. Additionally, the use of tobacco can lead to premature death and disability in family members, reducing household income and increasing healthcare costs.

translations.source: ከትንባሆ ጤንነት ላይ ከሚያስከትለው አሉታዊ ተጽዕኖ በተጨማሪ የትንባሆ አጠቃላዩ ኢኮኖሚያዊ ወጪ( ከጤና ወጪዎች እና ምርታማነት ኪሣራዎች በአንድ ላይ) በዓመት ወደ 1,4 ትሪሊዮን የአሜሪካ ዶላር እንደሚገመት ይገመታል፣ ይህም በዓለማችን ዓመታዊ አጠቃላይ የሀገር ውስጥ ምርት( GDP) 1.8% ጋር እኩል ነው። የዚህ ወጪ 40% የሚሆነው በታዳጊ ሀገራት የተከሰተ ሲሆን ይህም እነዚህ ሀገራት የሚደርስባቸውን ከፍተኛ ጫና ያሳያል። የትምባሆ ታክሶች በከፊል ይህንን ተፅእኖ ለመዋጋት የሚተገበሩ ሲሆን በተለይም በወጣቶች እና ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ህዝቦች መካከል የትምባሆ አጠቃቀምን ለመግታት በጣም ወጪ ቆጣቢው መንገድ ተደርገው ይታያሉ። የትምባሆ ዋጋን በ10 በመቶ የሚጨምር የታክስ ጭማሪ ከፍተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት 4 በመቶ ገደማ እና ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት 5 በመቶ የሚሆነውን የትንባሆ ፍጆታ ይቀንሳል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ፡- የዚህ ሰብዓዊና ኢኮኖሚያዊ አደጋ መጠን አስደንጋጭ ቢሆንም መከላከልም ይቻላል።
translations.reference: In addition to the detrimental impact of tobacco on health, the total economic cost of smoking (from health expenditures and productivity losses together) are estimated to be around US$ 1.4 trillion per year, equivalent in magnitude to 1.8% of the world's annual gross domestic product (GDP). Almost 40% of this cost occurred in developing countries, highlighting the substantial burden these countries suffer. Tobacco taxes are applied in part to combat this effect and are seen as the most cost-effective way of curbing tobacco use, particularly among youth and low-income populations. A tax increase that increases tobacco prices by 10% decreases tobacco consumption by about 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO response: The scale of this human and economic tragedy is shocking, but it’s also preventable.
translations.hypothesis: In addition to the negative health impacts, tobacco use is estimated to have an annual economic cost of $1.4 trillion worldwide, which is equivalent to 1.8% of the world's total gross domestic product (GDP). This cost includes healthcare expenses and lost productivity. Of this total cost, 40% is borne by developing countries, highlighting the significant burden these nations face. Tobacco taxes are partially used to mitigate this impact, particularly in efforts to discourage tobacco use among young people and low-income populations. A 10% increase in tobacco taxes can reduce tobacco consumption by approximately 4% in high-income countries and 5% in low- and middle-income countries. World Health Organization response: While the human and economic costs of tobacco use are staggering, they are also preventable.

translations.source: ትልልቅ የትምባሆ አምራቾች ከሁሉም የትምባሆ ምርቶች አምራቾች ጋር በመሆን የምርቶቻቸው አደጋዎች እንዲደበቁ እየታገሉ ነው፣ በ2003 የዓለም የጤና ድርጅት አባል ሃገራት በጋራ ድምፅ የዓለም የጤና ድርጅት የትምባሆ ቊጥጥር ማዕቀፍ ስምምነት( የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኤፍሲቲሲ) ያጸደቁ ሲሆን ይህም በዓለም የጤና ድርጅት ሥር ያለው ብቸኛ የሕዝብ ጤና ስምምነት ነው። እኛ ግን መልሰን እየታገልን ነው። ከ2005 ጀምሮ በሥራ ላይ የዋለ ሲሆን በአሁኑ ወቅት 181 ፓርቲዎች አሉት። ሃገራት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅትን ኤፍሲቲሲ እንዲተገብሩ ለማገዝ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኤም-ፓወር የተባለውን የቴክኒክ እርምጃዎች እና ሀብቶች ፓኬጅ አስተዋውቋል፣ እያንዳንዳቸው ቢያንስ ለአንድ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኤፍሲቲሲ ድንጋጌዎች ይዛመዳሉ። ኤምፓወር የትምባሆ ምርቶችን ፍላጎት ለመቀነስ 6 እርምጃዎችን ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ የአገሮችን አቅም ይገነባል፡ የትምባሆ አጠቃቀምና መከላከል ፖሊሲዎችን መከታተል፣ሰዎችን ከትንባሆ አጠቃቀም መጠበቅ፣ትንባሆ ማጨስን ለማቆም እርዳታ መስጠት፣ትምባሆ ስላለው አደገኛነት ማስጠንቀቅ፣የትምባሆ ማስታወቂያዎችን መከልከልን ማስገደድ , ማስተዋወቅ እና ስፖንሰርሺፕ እና በትምባሆ ላይ ግብር መጨመር
translations.reference: Big Tobacco — along with all manufacturers of tobacco products — is fighting to ensure the dangers of their products are concealed, but we are fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States unanimously adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only public health treaty under the auspices of WHO. In force since 2005, it has currently 181 Parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one provision of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER builds the capacity of countries to implement 6 measures to reduce the demand for tobacco products: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco use, offer help to quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship and raise taxes on tobacco.
translations.hypothesis: Major tobacco manufacturers are working together with other tobacco product manufacturers to reduce the harms associated with their products. In 2003, the World Health Organization (WHO) member states adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC), which is the only legally binding global health treaty. However, we are still working to implement it. Since 2005, the FCTC has been in force, and currently has 181 parties. The WHO has introduced a technical assistance and resource package called the WHO FCTC, which provides countries with the tools and resources they need to implement the FCTC. The FCTC provides a comprehensive set of measures to reduce tobacco use and prevent tobacco-related diseases, including: regulating tobacco products, protecting people from tobacco smoke, providing assistance to quit smoking, warning people about the dangers of tobacco, prohibiting tobacco advertising, promotion, and sponsorship, and increasing taxes on tobacco.

translations.source: ትራኮማ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ ትራኮማ የአይን በሽታ ሲሆን በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለዓይነ ስውርነት ዋነኛ ተላላፊ በሽታ ነው። ይህ በሽታ የሚከሰተው ክላሚዲያ ትራኮማቲስ በተባለ ሕዋስ ውስጥ በሚገኝ ባክቴሪያ ነው። ኢንፌክሽኑ ከሰው ወደ ሰው የሚተላለፈው በበሽታው የተያዙ ሰዎች የዓይን እና የአፍንጫ ፈሳሾችን በቀጥታ ወይም በተዘዋዋሪ በማስተላለፍ ነው። ቀጥተኛ ያልሆነ ማስተላለፍ የዝንብ ዝርያዎችን በሰውነት ላይ ማጓጓዝን ያጠቃልላል። በቅድመ-ትምህርት ዕድሜ ላይ የሚገኙ ሕፃናት ዋነኛ የኢንፌክሽን ማጠራቀሚያ ናቸው።
translations.reference: Trachoma Overview: Trachoma is a disease of the eye and the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by an obligate intracellular bacterium called Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person by direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges of infected people; indirect transfer includes carriage on the body of species of flies. Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection.
translations.hypothesis: Trachoma: An Overview Trachoma is an eye infection that is a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide. It is caused by the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. The infection is spread from person to person through direct or indirect contact with infected eye or nasal secretions. Indirect transmission includes contact with contaminated surfaces or objects. Children under the age of five are particularly vulnerable to infection.

translations.source: ሞዴሎች የትራኮማ ዓይነ ስውር ችግሮችን ለማዳበር አንድ ግለሰብ ከ150 በላይ የዕድሜ ልክ ኢንፌክሽኖች እንደሚያስፈልገው ይጠቁማሉ። ትራኮማ በገጠርና ራቅ ባሉ አካባቢዎች የሚኖሩ እና የውኃ፣ የንፅህና እና የጤና እንክብካቤ እጥረት ያለባቸው በአንዳንድ የዓለማችን ድሀ ሕዝብ ውስጥ ሥር የሰደደ ነው። አፍሪካ በጣም የተጎዳች አህጉር ነች፣ ግን በጣም የተስፋፋ የቁጥጥር ጥረቶችም አሏት። በሽታው በመካከለኛው እና በደቡብ አሜሪካ, በእስያ, በአውስትራሊያ እና በመካከለኛው ምስራቅ ይገኛል። ከጃንዋሪ 2 ቀን 2020 ጀምሮ 13 አገራት የማስወገድ ግቦችን ማሳካታቸውን ሪፖርት አድርገዋል።
translations.reference: Models suggest that an individual requires more than 150 lifetime infections to develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is endemic in some of the world’s poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas and have highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but it also has the most widespread control efforts. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of 2 January 2020, 13 countries had reported achieving elimination goals.
translations.hypothesis: Models suggest that an individual needs to be infected with over 150 strains of Trichomonas to develop the disease. Trichomoniasis is prevalent in rural and remote areas, particularly among impoverished populations in some parts of the world who lack access to clean water, sanitation, and healthcare. Africa is one of the most affected regions, but it also has some of the most successful control programs. The disease is found in the Middle East, South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of January 2, 2020, 13 countries have reported achieving their elimination goals.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በ2019 ብቻ 92 622 ሰዎች ለከፍተኛ ትራኮማ የቀዶ ጥገና ሕክምና ያገኙ ሲሆን 95.2 ሚሊዮን የሚሆኑት ደግሞ በፀረ-ባክቴሪያ መድኃኒቶች ታክመዋል። እነዚህ ስኬቶች ቢኖሩም በሽታው ከባድ የሕዝብ ጤና ስጋት ሆኖ ቀጥሏል፣ በዓመት 8 ቢሊዮን የአሜሪካ ዶላር በዓይነ ስውርነት እና በእይታ እክል ምክንያት በምርታማነት ኪሳራ ይገመታል። ምልክቶች: ኢንፌክሽን "አክቲቭ ትራኮማ" ተብሎ በሚጠራው በኮንጁክቲቫ ውስጥ ከሚታዩ ለውጦች ጋር የተያያዘ ነው. የአንድ ግለሰብ የበሽታ መከላከያ ስርዓት አንድ ጊዜ የኢንፌክሽን ክፍልን ሊያጸዳ ይችላል, ነገር ግን በተስፋፋ ማህበረሰቦች ውስጥ, የሰውነት አካልን እንደገና ማግኘት በተደጋጋሚ ይከሰታል. ለዓመታት ተደጋጋሚ የነቃ ትራኮማ ከተከሰተ በኋላ የዐይን ሽፋኑ ውስጠኛው ክፍል በጣም ጠባሳ(የትራኮማቲክ ኮንጁክቲቫል ጠባሳ) ሊሆን ይችላል። ይህ ወደ ውስጡ እንዲዞር እና የዓይን ሽፋኖቹ ከዓይን ኳስ( ትራኮማቲክ ትሪኪያሲስ) ጋር እንዲጣበቁ ያደርገዋል፣ በዚህም ምክንያት የማያቋርጥ ሕመም እና የብርሃን አለመቻቻል ያስከትላል፣ ይህ እና ሌሎች የዓይን ለውጦች ወደ ኮርኒያ ጠባሳ ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ።
translations.reference: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea.
translations.hypothesis: In 2019 alone, 92,622 people received surgery for trachoma, while another 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a major public health concern, costing the United States an estimated $8 billion annually in lost productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Trachoma is characterized by changes in the conjunctiva, often referred to as "active trachoma." While a person's immune system can typically clear an initial infection, in communities with poor sanitation, the infection can recur repeatedly. Chronic, recurrent trachoma can lead to scarring of the inner eyelids (trachomatous scarring), which can pull the eyelids inward and cause them to stick together (trichiasis). This can result in chronic irritation, difficulty with blinking, and corneal damage.

translations.source: ህክምና ካልተደረገለት ይህ ሁኔታ የማይቀለበስ የዓይን እክል ወይም ዓይነ ስውርነት ያስከትላል። ይህ የሚከሰትበት ዕድሜ በአካባቢያዊ ስርጭት ጥንካሬ ጨምሮ በበርካታ ምክንያቶች ላይ የተመሰረተ ነው። ምንም እንኳን ከ30 እስከ 40 ዓመት ባለው ጊዜ ውስጥ የማየት እክል መጀመሩ የተለመደ ቢሆንም በጣም በተስፋፋባቸው ማህበረሰቦች ውስጥ፣ በልጅነት ጊዜ ሊከሰት ይችላል። ሴቶች ከወንዶች በአራት እጥፍ የሚበልጡ ዓይነ ስውር ይሆናሉ፣ ምናልባትም በበሽታው ከተያዙ ሕፃናት ጋር በተደጋጋሚ በመገናኘት ምክንያት ሊሆን ይችላል። በአፍሪካ፣ በእስያ፣ በአውስትራሊያ፣ በመካከለኛው ምስራቅ፣ በመካከለኛው እና በደቡብ አሜሪካ እና በፓስፊክ ደሴቶች በጣም ድሃ እና በጣም ሩቅ በሆኑ ማህበረሰቦች ውስጥ የሚኖሩ ግለሰቦች ለአደጋ ተጋልጠዋል።
translations.reference: Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.hypothesis: Without treatment, this condition can lead to irreversible eye damage or blindness. The factors that contribute to this vary, including environmental exposure to toxins. While vision loss typically begins in adulthood, it can also occur in childhood, particularly in communities with high levels of environmental pollution. Women are four times more likely to develop blindness than men, possibly due to repeated exposure to contaminated environments during childhood. Individuals living in impoverished and remote communities in Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America, and the Pacific Islands are particularly at risk.

translations.source: ህክምና: የአካባቢ አስጊ ሁኔታዎች በመቀነስ ትራኮማን መከላከል ይቻላል። እነዚህም በቂ ንጽህና አለመጠበቅ፣ የተጨናነቁ ቤተሰቦች፣ በቂ ውሃ አለማግኘት እና በቂ የንፅህና አጠባበቅ እና አጠቃቀምን ያካትታሉ። ኢንፌክሽኖች ብዙውን ጊዜ የሚጀምሩት ገና በለጋ ዕድሜ ላይ ሲሆን በእድሜ እየጨመረ በሄደ መጠን አልፎ አልፎ እና በአጭር ጊዜ ያድጋሉ። ኢንፌክሽኖች ብዙውን ጊዜ በቤተሰብ እና በንቃት በሽታ ካለባቸው ሌሎች ሰዎች ጋር በመገናኘት ይተላለፋሉ። ተደጋጋሚ ኢንፌክሽኖች የበሽታውን በጣም ከባድ ምልክቶች ያስከትላሉ።
translations.reference: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease.
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: Trichomoniasis can be prevented by reducing the risk factors in the environment. These include poor hygiene, crowded living conditions, lack of access to clean water, and inadequate sanitation and waste disposal. Infections often begin in childhood and may become more frequent and severe as a person ages. Infections are typically spread through contact with infected individuals, such as family members or sexual partners. Repeated infections can lead to the most severe symptoms of the disease.

translations.source: ትራኮማ እንደ የሕዝብ ጤና ችግር በ ሴፍ ስትራቴጂ በመጠቀም ሊወገድ ይችላል፣ ይህም የሚከተሉትን ያካትታል: ትራኮማቶዝ ትሪኪያሲስ በሽታውን ለማከም የሚደረግ ቀዶ ጥገና፤ አንቲባዮቲኮች ኢንፌክሽኑን ለማጥፋት በተለይም አምራቹ በዓለም አቀፍ ትራኮማ ተነሳሽነት በኩል ለብሔራዊ ፕሮግራሞች የሚለገሰው አንቲባዮቲክ አዚትሮሚሲን በጅምላ የመድኃኒት አሰጣጥ፤ የፊት ንፅህና እና የአካባቢ መሻሻል፣ በተለይም የተሻለ የውሃ እና የንፅህና አጠባበቅ ተደራሽነት።
translations.reference: Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.hypothesis: Trachoma, a major public health problem, can be eradicated through a comprehensive strategy that includes: surgical treatment for trachomatous trichiasis; mass distribution of azithromycin, an antibiotic effective against the bacteria, through national programs; improved hygiene and sanitation practices, particularly access to clean water and sanitation facilities.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የሴፍ ስትራቴጂን በ1993 ተቀብሎ ትራኮማን እንደ የህዝብ ጤና ችግር ለማስወገድ አለም አቀፍ ጥረቶችን የመምራት እና የማስተባበር ተልዕኮውን ቀጥሏል።
translations.reference: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization adopted the Safe Strategy in 1993 and has continued its mission of leading and coordinating global efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.

translations.source: ባህላዊ፣ ተጨማሪ እና የተቀናጀ ሕክምና አጠቃላይ እይታ: ባህላዊ ሕክምና ባህላዊ ሕክምና ረጅም ታሪክ አለው። በተለያዩ ባህሎች ውስጥ ባሉ ጽንሰ-ሀሳቦች፣ እምነቶች እና ልምዶች ላይ የተመሰረቱ እውቀቶች፣ ክህሎቶች እና ልምዶች ድምር ነው፣ ሊብራራ የሚችል ወይም የማይችል፣ በጤና ጥበቃ እንዲሁም በአካላዊ እና በአእምሮ ሕመም መከላከል፣ መመርመር፣ ማሻሻል ወይም ህክምና ጥቅም ላይ ይውላል። ተጨማሪ መድሃኒት
translations.reference: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine
translations.hypothesis: Traditional, Complementary, and Integrative Medicine: A Holistic View: Traditional medicine has a long history. It encompasses a diverse range of knowledge, skills, and practices based on the beliefs and experiences of different cultures. It can be used as a primary or alternative form of healthcare, as well as a complementary approach to conventional medicine for the prevention, diagnosis, treatment, and management of physical and mental illnesses.

translations.source: “የተጨማሪ ሕክምና” ወይም “አማራጭ ሕክምና” የሚሉት ቃላት የሚያመለክተው የዚያች ሀገር የራሷ ባህል ወይም ባህላዊ ሕክምና አካል ያልሆኑ እና ከዋና የጤና አጠባበቅ ሥርዓት ጋር ሙሉ በሙሉ ያልተዋሃዱ ሰፊ የጤና አጠባበቅ ልምዶችን ነው። በአንዳንድ አገሮች ውስጥ ከባህላዊ መድኃኒት ጋር ተለዋዋጭ ሆነው ጥቅም ላይ ይውላሉ። ከዕፅዋት የተቀመሙ መድኃኒቶች ከዕፅዋት የተቀመሙ መድኃኒቶች ዕፅዋትን፣ የእጽዋት ቁሳቁሶችን፣ ከዕፅዋት የተቀመሙ ዝግጅቶችን እና ያለቀላቸው ከዕፅዋት የተቀመሙ ምርቶችን፣ እንደ ንቁ ንጥረ ነገሮች የዕፅዋት ክፍሎች፣ ወይም ሌሎች የእጽዋት ቁሳቁሶችን ወይም ውህዶችን ያካትታሉ። ተጽዕኖ፡- የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የባህላዊ መድኃኒት ስትራቴጂ 2014-2023 የተዘጋጀውና የተጀመረው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ባህላዊ መድኃኒት( ደብልዩኤችኤ62.13) ላይ ባወጣው የውሳኔ ሃሳብ መሠረት ነው።
translations.reference: The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.hypothesis: The terms "complementary medicine" or "alternative medicine" refer to a wide range of health practices that are not part of mainstream medical systems and are often used in conjunction with conventional medicine. In some countries, they are used as substitutes for conventional medicine. Herbal remedies are a type of complementary medicine that includes plants, plant extracts, herbal preparations, and other plant-based products, such as essential oils or other plant-derived compounds.

translations.source: ስትራቴጂው የባህላዊ መድሃኒቶች የህዝቡን ጤና ለመጠበቅ የሚጫወቱትን ሚና የሚያጠናክሩ ፖሊሲዎችን በማውጣት እና የድርጊት መርሃ ግብሮችን በመተግበር አባል ሀገራትን ለመደገፍ ያለመ ነው። ተግዳሮቶችን መፍታት፣ በአባል ሀገራት ተለይተው የሚታወቁትን ፍላጎቶች መመለስ እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት የባህል ህክምና ስትራቴጂ፡ 2002-2005 የተከናወነውን ስራ በማጠናከር የተሻሻለው የ2014-2023 ስትራቴጂ ለጤና አገልግሎት እና ስርዓት ቅድሚያ ለመስጠት ባህላዊ እና ተጨማሪ የመድኃኒት ምርቶችን፣ ልምዶችን እና ባለሙያዎችን ጨምሮ ከቀዳሚው የበለጠ ትኩረት ሰጥቷል። ስልታዊ አላማዎች፡- ባህላዊ እና ተጨማሪ ሕክምናን በተገቢው ብሔራዊ ፖሊሲዎች ለማስተዳደር የእውቀት መሠረት ለመገንባት፣ ምርቶችን ፣ ልምዶችን እና ባለሙያዎችን በመቆጣጠር የባህላዊ እና ተጨማሪ መድሃኒቶችን የጥራት ማረጋገጫ ፣ ደህንነት ፣ ትክክለኛ አጠቃቀም እና ውጤታማነትን ለማጠናከር ፣
translations.reference: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners;
translations.hypothesis: The strategy aims to support member countries by developing and implementing policies and action plans that strengthen the role of traditional medicine in protecting public health. This includes addressing challenges, responding to the specific needs of member countries, and building on the work of the World Health Organization's Traditional Medicine Strategy: 2002-2005, as well as the revised 2014-2023 strategy, which places greater emphasis on traditional and complementary medicine products, practices, and practitioners, as well as health services and systems. Strategic objectives include: establishing a knowledge base for the appropriate management of traditional and complementary medicine through national policies; ensuring the quality, safety, appropriate use, and effectiveness of traditional and complementary medicine products, practices, and practitioners; and promoting the integration of traditional and complementary medicine into health systems.

translations.source: የባህላዊ እና ተጨማሪ መድሃኒቶችን አገልግሎቶችን ከጤና አጠባበቅ አገልግሎት አሰጣጥ እና ከራስ ጤና አጠባበቅ ጋር በማቀናጀት ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋንን ለማስተዋወቅ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምላሽ፦ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በባህላዊ እና ተጓዳኝ መድኃኒት ላይ ያወጣው ዓለም አቀፍ ሪፖርት በባህላዊ እና ተጓዳኝ መድኃኒት መስክ ከአባል አገራት የሚመጡ አስተማማኝ፣ ተዓማኒነት ያላቸው እና ኦፊሴላዊ መረጃዎች ላይ ያለውን ክፍተት ለመቅረፍ ተዘጋጅቷል። ይህ ሪፖርት ባለፉት ሁለት አስርት ዓመታት ውስጥ በባህላዊ እና ተጓዳኝ መድኃኒት ውስጥ ያለውን ዓለም አቀፍ እድገት የሚገመግም ሲሆን ከ179 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት ባደረጉት አስተዋጽዖ ላይ የተመሰረተ ነው። በባህላዊ እና ተጓዳኝ መድኃኒት ለጤና እና ለደህንነት ሊያበረክተው የሚችለውን አስተዋጾ ለመጠቀም ለፖሊሲ አውጪዎች፣ የጤና ባለሙያዎች እና ህዝቡ ጠቃሚ መረጃ ይሰጣል።
translations.reference: To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.hypothesis: To promote universal health coverage by integrating traditional and complementary medicine into healthcare systems and individual health practices. World Health Organization response: The World Health Organization's recent global report on traditional and complementary medicine aims to address the gap in reliable, consistent, and official data from member countries in the field of traditional and complementary medicine. This report assesses the global progress in traditional and complementary medicine over the past two decades, based on contributions from 179 of the World Health Organization's member states. It provides policymakers, healthcare professionals, and the public with valuable information on how to leverage the potential of traditional and complementary medicine to improve health and well-being.

translations.source: ንቅለ ተከላ አጠቃላይ እይታ: የሰዎች ሕዋሳት፣ ሕብረ ህዋሳት እና የአካል ክፍሎች ንቅለ ተከላ የሰዎች ሕዋሳት፣ ሕብረ ህዋሳት ወይም የአካል ክፍሎች ንቅለ ተከላ ብዙ ሰዎችን ሕይወት ያድናል እንዲሁም ተመጣጣኝ ውጤታማነት ያላቸው አማራጮች በማይኖሩበት ጊዜ አስፈላጊ ተግባራትን ይመልሳል። በ 50 ዓመታት ውስጥ, ንቅለ ተከላ የተሳካ ዓለም አቀፍ ልምምድ ሆኗል. ይሁን እንጂ በአገሮች መካከል ተስማሚ የሆነ ንቅለ ተከላ በማግኘት እና በሰዎች ሕዋሳት ፣ ሕብረ ሕዋሳት እና የአካል ክፍሎች ደህንነት ፣ ጥራት ፣ ልገሳ እና ንቅለ ተከላ ደረጃ ላይ ትልቅ ልዩነቶች አሉ።
translations.reference: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs.
translations.hypothesis: Overview of Prosthetics: Prosthetics, which involves the replacement of body parts, tissues, or organs, can significantly improve the lives of individuals who have lost these components due to injury, illness, or congenital conditions. Over the past 50 years, prosthetics has evolved into a successful and well-established field. However, there are significant differences in the quality, safety, and effectiveness of prosthetics available in different countries, as well as variations in the types of prosthetics available for different body parts.

translations.source: የንቅለ ተከላ ሥነ-ምግባራዊ ገጽታዎች ግንባር ቀደም ናቸው። በተለይም ያልተሟሉ የታካሚዎች ፍላጐቶች እና የንቅለ ተከላ እጥረት የሰው አካላት አዘዋዋሪነት ፈተናን ያመጣል። የሰው አካል መትከል ለንቅለ ተከላ የሰው ልጅ ህዋሳት፣ ሕብረ ህዋሳት ሕይወትን ሊያድኑ ወይም አስፈላጊ ተግባራትን ሊመልሱ ይችላሉ። ለምሳሌ የኮርኒያ ግርዶሽ በኮርኒያ ዓይነ ስውርነት ውስጥ ያለውን እይታ ወደነበረበት መመለስ ይችላል፣
translations.reference: The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;
translations.hypothesis: However, the ethical aspects of transplantation are paramount. In particular, the unmet needs of patients and the shortage of organs have created a significant challenge in organ transplantation. The use of human organs for transplantation can raise ethical concerns, as it may involve the use of tissues or organs from living or deceased individuals. For example, corneal transplants can restore vision to individuals with corneal damage,

translations.source: የሂሞቶፔይቲክ ስቴም ሕዋሳትን መተካት አንዳንድ ሉኪሚያዎችን ጨምሮ የተወለዱ ወይም የተገኙ በሽታዎችን ማዳን ይችላል. ስለ ሂሞቶፔይቲክ ስቴም ሕዋሳት ተጨማሪ መረጃ ይመልከቱ የሰዎች የልብ ቫልቭ(ክፍክድ) መተካት ብዙውን ጊዜ የተሻለውን የመተካት ሁኔታን ያካትታል እና ተቀባይ ታካሚዎች የረጅም ጊዜ የፀረ- ደም መፍሰስ ሕክምና አያስፈልጋቸውም። የዜኖ ንቅለ ተከላ የዜኖ ንቅለ ተከላ እንስሳ ወደ ሰው፣ ሕያው ህዋሳት፣ ሕብረ ህዋሳት ወይም የእንስሳት ምንጭ እና የሰው አካል ፈሳሾች ህዋሳት፣ ሕብረ ህዋሳት ወይም እነዚህን ሕያው፣ ዜኖጄክ ቁሳቁሶች ጋር ኤክስ-ቪቮ ያላቸው አካላት ፤ ከሰው የመነጨ ቁሳቁስ አማራጭ የመሆን አቅም ያለው እና ለክትባት የሰው ቁሳቁስ እጥረትን የሚሸፍን ነው።
translations.reference: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation.
translations.hypothesis: Hemopeptide stem cells can potentially treat certain acquired or discovered diseases, including those caused by leukemia. For more information on hemopeptide stem cells, see "Replacing Human Heart Valves." Replacing human heart valves often involves a more favorable outcome, and patients who undergo this procedure typically do not require long-term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenogeneic transplantation involves transferring cells, tissues, or organs from an animal species to a human, or from a living organism to a human. This can include human cells, tissues, or organs that are xenogeneic (i.e., derived from a non-human source) and are compatible with the human host. Xenogeneic transplantation offers the potential to overcome the shortage of human-derived materials for transplantation and vaccination.

translations.source: ልገሳ፡- በአለም ጤና ድርጅት መመሪያ 3 ላይ እንደተገለጸው ከሟቾች የሚበረከቱት ልገሳ በተቻለ መጠን በህይወት ለጋሾች ላይ ሊደርሱ የሚችሉትን ስጋቶች በማስወገድ ከፍተኛውን የህክምና አቅማቸውን ማዳበር አለበት። ማህበረሰቦች እና የጤና ባለሙያዎች ስለ ልገሳ እና ስለ መተከል በተሻለ ሁኔታ እንዲማሩ እውቅና የተሰጠው ፍላጐት አለ እናም ይህ ለሟች ልገሳ ፕሮግራሞች ስኬት ቍልፍ ነው። ይሁን እንጂ ከሟች ለጋሾች የሚለገሱ አካላትን በተደጋጋሚ ጥቅም ላይ ቢውሉም በህይወት ያሉ ለጋሾች የሚሰጡት ልገሳ ለአንዳንድ የንቅለ ተከላ ዓይነቶች አስፈላጊ ነው ወይም የታካሚዎችን ፍላጐት ለማሟላት ከሟች ለጋሾች የሚገኘውን ውስን ቁሳቁስ ለማካካስ አስፈላጊ ናቸው። ሕያው ልገሳ ለለጋሹ ቸል ሊባሉ የማይችሉ አደጋዎችን የሚያካትት ቢሆንም በተግባር ላይ ይውላል። ከሟች ወይም በህይወት ካሉ ለጋሾች የሚተከል የሰው አካላት ግዥ እና ተከታዩ የአሎጄኔክ ንቅለ ተከላ ለተቀባዩም ሆነ ለለጋሹ ሥነ ምግባራዊ እና ደህንነትን አደጋ ላይ ሊጥል ስለሚችል እነሱን ለመጠበቅ በጤና ባለሥልጣናት ጥብቅ ቁጥጥር እና ውጤታማ ቁጥጥር መደረግ አለበት።
translations.reference: Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.
translations.hypothesis: As outlined in WHO guidelines 3, donated organs should be used to their fullest potential to address the needs of living recipients. There is a growing awareness among communities and healthcare professionals about the importance of organ donation and transplantation, which is contributing to the success of organ donation programs. However, even when donated organs are used for living recipients, there is still a need for organs from deceased donors for certain types of transplants or to ensure that the limited resources available from deceased donors are used to meet the needs of living recipients. While living donation can be a risky procedure for the donor, it is often the only option available for living recipients. To ensure the safety and ethical use of both donated and living organs, strict regulations and oversight by health authorities are essential.

translations.source: የመመሪያ መርሆቹ ለለጋሾች እና ተቀባዮች ጥሩ እንክብካቤን ያዛል። ህብረተሰቡ በስርአቱ ላይ ያለውን አመኔታ ለማሳደግ የጤና ባለስልጣናት በስጦታ እና በንቅለ ተከላ ስራዎች ላይ የሚያደርግ ግልፅ ቁጥጥር አስፈላጊ ነው። ከዚህም ባሻገር ለጋሽ የመሆን ውሳኔ ብዙውን ጊዜ የሚወሰነው ለለጋሽ ቤተሰብ የጤና እንክብካቤ በሚያስፈልጋቸው ነገሮች ላይ አስተዋጽኦ ማበርከት እንደሚቻል በመገንዘብ ነው። ንቅለ ተከላ ለመቀበል ከተዘጋጀን ካስፈለገን፤ ለመስጠትም ዝግጁ መሆን አለብን ስነምግባር፡ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት በሰው ሕዋስ፣ ህብረ ሕዋስ እና የአካል ንቅለ ተከላ ላይ መመሪያ መርሆዎች
translations.reference: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation
translations.hypothesis: The guiding principles of the program prioritize the well-being of both donors and recipients. To maintain public trust in the system, health authorities must rigorously monitor and regulate donations and transplantation activities. Furthermore, the decision to become a donor is often driven by a desire to contribute to the health and well-being of loved ones. If we are prepared to donate, we should also be prepared to receive, in accordance with ethical principles and the World Health Organization's guidelines on human, animal, and organ transplantation.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በ 1987 አርባኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ፣ በሰው የአካል ክፍሎች ውስጥ ለትርፍ የሚደረግ ንግድ ያሳሰበው ፣ በ 1991 በጉባኤው በ ድብልዩኤችኤ44.25 ውሳኔ የፀደቀውን የመጀመሪያውን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመመሪያ መርሆችን ማዘጋጀት ጀመረ ። እነዚህ የመመሪያ መርሆዎች ወደ ሁለት አስርት ዓመታት በሚጠጋ ጊዜ ውስጥ በሙያዊ ህጎች እና ልምዶች እንዲሁም በዓለም ዙሪያ ባሉ ህጎች ላይ ተጽዕኖ አሳድረዋል። በርካታ አመታትን ከወሰደው የምክክር ሂደት በኋላ፣ ስድሳ ሶስተኛው የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የተሻሻለውን የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የመመሪያ መርሆችን በማፅደቅ እና የልገሳ እና የንቅለ ተከላ ስራዎችን ለማመቻቸት እ.ኤ.አ. በ 21 ግንቦት 2010 የ ድብልዩኤችኤ63.22 ውሳኔን አጽድቋል። የኢስታንቡል መግለጫ ከሁሉም ክልሎች የተውጣጡ የልገሳ እና የንቅለ ተከላ ባለሙያዎች በበርካታ ድርጅቶቻቸው እና ተቋሞቻቸው አማካኝነት በግንቦት 2008 በ ንቅለ ተከላ ማህበረሰብ እና በአለምአቀፍ ኔፍሮሎጂ ማህበረሰብ መሪነት የተገነባውን የአካል ክፍሎች ዝውውር እና የንቅለ ተከላ ቱሪዝምን የኢስታንቡል መግለጫን እየደገፉ ነው።
translations.reference: In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.
translations.hypothesis: In 1987, the 40th World Health Assembly addressed the issue of commercial surrogacy, and in 1991, the Assembly adopted resolution WHA44.25, which established the first set of international guidelines for surrogacy. These guidelines have had a significant impact on professional codes of conduct and practices, as well as national laws around the world. After years of discussion and debate, the World Health Organization adopted revised guidelines for surrogacy in resolution WHA63.22, which was adopted on 21 June 2010. The Istanbul Declaration, endorsed by experts in reproductive and neonatology from all regions, supports the Istanbul Declaration, which was developed in 2008 by the International Federation of Neonatal Nurses and the International Society of Neonatal Nurses.

translations.source: ጉዞ እና ጤና አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ አለምአቀፍ ጉዞ በጤና ላይ የተለያዩ አደጋዎችን ሊያስከትል ይችላል ይህም እንደ ተጓዡም ሆነ እንደ ጉዞ ባህሪ ነው። ተጓዦች በከፍታ፣ በእርጥበት መጠን፣ በተህዋስያኖች እና በሙቀት ላይ ድንገተኛ እና ጉልህ ለውጦች ሊያጋጥሟቸው ይችላሉ፣ ይህም የጤና እክልን ያስከትላል። በተጨማሪም የመጠለያ ጥራት በሌለበት፣ ንጽህናና ንጽህና ባልተሟሉበት፣ የሕክምና አገልግሎት በደንብ ባልተዳበረ እና ንፁህ ውሃ በማይገኝባቸው አካባቢዎች ከባድ የጤና ችግሮች ሊፈጠሩ ይችላሉ። በየዓመቱ በሚሊዮን የሚቆጠሩ ሰዎች ለሙያ፣ ለማህበራዊ፣ ለመዝናኛ እና ለሰብአዊ ዓላማዎች ስለሚጓዙ ዓለም አቀፍ ጉዞ እየጨመረ ነው።
translations.reference: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year.
translations.hypothesis: A comprehensive view of travel and health: International travel can pose various health risks, depending on the destination and travel style. Travelers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, temperature, and air quality, which can lead to health problems. Furthermore, in areas with poor sanitation, inadequate hygiene, limited access to healthcare, and a lack of clean water, serious health issues can arise. With millions of people traveling for work, social, leisure, and humanitarian purposes each year, international travel is on the rise.

translations.source: ሁሉም ተጓዦች ከመጓዛቸው በፊት፣ ጊዜ እና በኋላ በማያውቋቸው አካባቢዎች ሊጋለጡ ለሚችሉ የተለያዩ የጤና አደጋዎች መዘጋጀት አለባቸው። ለመጓዝ ያቀዱ ሰዎች በተመረጡት መድረሻ ላይ ሊኖሩ ስለሚችሉ አደጋዎች ምክር መጠየቅ እና ጤንነታቸውን እንዴት መጠበቅ እንደሚችሉ እና በበሽታ የመያዝ አደጋን መቀነስ አለባቸው። ወደፊት ማቀድ፣ ተገቢ የመከላከያ እርምጃዎች እና ጥንቃቄ የተሞላበት ጥንቃቄዎች የተጓዦችን ጤና ይጠብቃሉ፣ እና የአደጋ እና በሽታ የመያዝ አደጋዎችን ይቀንሳሉ። ምንም እንኳን የሕክምና ሙያ እና የጉዞ ኢንዱስትሪው ሰፊ እገዛ እና ጥሩ ምክር ሊሰጡ ቢችሉም፣ ተጓዥ መረጃን መፈለግ፣ ሊከሰቱ የሚችሉትን አደጋዎች ለመረዳት እና በሚጓዙበት ጊዜ ጤንነታቸውን ለመጠበቅ አስፈላጊውን ጥንቃቄ ማድረግ የተጓዥው ኃላፊነት ነው. ስጋት፡- የአለም አቀፍ ጉዞ የጤና አደጋዎችን በሚወስኑበት ጊዜ ተጓዦች እና የጤና ባለሙያዎች የሚከተሉትን ቁልፍ ነገሮች ግምት ውስጥ ማስገባት አለባቸው።
translations.reference: All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:
translations.hypothesis: All travelers should be prepared for a variety of health risks that could arise during their journey, whether they are aware of them or not. Travelers should seek advice from trusted sources about the potential dangers at their destination and how to protect their health. By taking appropriate precautions and being vigilant, travelers can minimize their risk of illness and injury. While healthcare professionals and the travel industry can provide valuable information, it is ultimately the traveler's responsibility to research potential risks, take necessary precautions, and protect their health while traveling. Key considerations for travelers and health professionals when assessing health risks associated with international travel include:

translations.source: የመጓጓዣ ዘዴ መድረሻ(ዎች) ቆይታ እና የጉዞ ወቅት የጉዞ ዓላማ የመጠለያ, የምግብ ንፅህና እና የንፅህና አጠባበቅ ደረጃዎች
translations.reference: mode of transport destination(s) duration and season of travel purpose of travel standards of accommodation, food hygiene and sanitation
translations.hypothesis: Transportation method, destination(s), duration of stay, purpose of travel, accommodation, food hygiene and cleanliness standards.

translations.source: የተጓዥ ባህሪ የተጓዥው መሠረታዊ ጤና ተጓዦች በሚሄዱበት ቦታ ላይ በመመርኮዝ የሚከተሉትን የጤና አደጋዎች ሊያጋጥሟቸው ይችላሉ: እንደ ወባ ያሉ በሽታዎችን የሚያሰራጩ ትንኞች፣ መዥገሮች፣ ቁንጫዎች እና አንዳንድ ዝንቦችን ጨምሮ የእንስሳት እና የነፍሳት ንክሻዎች ለደም ወይም ለሌሎች የሰውነት ፈሳሾች መጋለጥ
translations.reference: behaviour of the traveller underlying health of the traveller Travellers may encounter the following health risks depending on their destination: animal and insect bites, including mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, and some flies, which can spread diseases such as malaria exposure to blood or other bodily fluids
translations.hypothesis: Travelers may encounter various health risks while traveling, depending on the location and their activities. These risks can include: diseases transmitted by insects such as mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, and certain types of flies; exposure to animal and bird droppings; and contact with contaminated water or food.

translations.source: እንደ ከፍታ ፣ እርጥበት ፣ የሙቀት መጠን ያሉ የአካባቢ ለውጦች የምግብ ወለድ እና የውሃ ወለድ በሽታዎች የምግብ ወለድ እና የውሃ ወለድ በሽታዎች በቂ ያልሆነ የሕክምና አገልግሎት ከመንገድ ትራፊክ አደጋዎች ወይም ከመዝናኛ እንቅስቃሴዎች ጨምሮ ጉዳቶች ጭንቀትን እና የባህል ድንጋጤን ጨምሮ የስነ-ልቦና ጤና
translations.reference: environmental changes, such as altitude, humidity, temperature foodborne and waterborne diseases inadequate medical services injuries, including from road traffic accidents or recreational activities psychological health, including stress and culture shock
translations.hypothesis: Factors such as altitude, humidity, and temperature can affect the incidence of foodborne and waterborne diseases. These diseases can be caused by a variety of factors, including inadequate access to medical care, traffic accidents, and recreational activities. They can also lead to mental health problems such as stress and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD).

translations.source: ተጓዦች ለብዙ ተላላፊ በሽታዎች ሊጋለጡ ይችላሉ, እና ከእነዚህ ውስጥ ብዙዎቹ በክትባት መከላከል ይቻላል. ይሁን እንጂ በጣም አደገኛ የሆኑትን ጨምሮ ክትባት የሌላቸው አንዳንድ ተላላፊ በሽታዎች አሉ። ማረፊያ፣ ንፅህና እና ንፅህና አጠባበቅ፣ የህክምና እንክብካቤ እና የውሃ ጥራት ከፍተኛ ደረጃ ያላቸው መዳረሻዎች በተጓዦች ጤና ላይ በጣም አነስተኛ የሆኑ አደጋዎችን ይፈጥራሉ። እነዚህ አገልግሎቶች በማይሰጡበት ጊዜ ተጓዦች በሽታን ለማስወገድ ጥብቅ ጥንቃቄ ማድረግ አለባቸው. ከመንግሥት ምንጮች የሚመጡ የጉዞ ማስጠንቀቂያዎች በቁም ነገር መወሰድ አለባቸው።
translations.reference: Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most dangerous, for which no vaccines exist. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.
translations.hypothesis: Travelers are at risk of contracting many infectious diseases, and some of these can be prevented through vaccination. However, there are also some infectious diseases that are not preventable by vaccination. Travelers should take extra precautions to avoid contracting diseases, especially in areas where healthcare and sanitation are not up to par. Travelers should also heed any travel advisories issued by their government.

translations.source: መከላከያ፡ ከጉዞ በፊት፡ የሚፈለገውን ክትባት ለመውሰድ ከጉዞው 4 እስከ 8 ሳምንታት በፊት የጉዞ መድኃኒት ማዕከልን ወይም ሐኪም ያነጋግሩ። ከመድረሻዎ ጋር በተያያዙ የአካባቢያዊ በሽታዎች ላይ ያንብቡ; የወባ ስጋት እና የወባ ትንኝ ንክሻን ለመከላከል መረጃ ይጠይቁ። ይህ ተገቢውን የመከላከያ መድሐኒት መግዛት እና አጎበር እና ፀረ-ነፍሳትን ማሸግ ሊያካትት ይችላል፤ የሕክምና እና የጥርስ ምርመራ ማድረግ;
translations.reference: Prevention: Before travel: contact a travel medicine centre or a physician, preferably 4–8 weeks before departure to receive any required vaccinations; read up on local diseases related to your destination; request information on malaria risk and prevention of mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate preventive medication and packing a bednet and insect repellent; get a medical and dental check-up;
translations.hypothesis: Prevention: Before your trip: Contact your travel clinic or doctor 4 to 8 weeks before your departure to obtain the necessary vaccinations. Research any diseases prevalent in your destination area; ask about prevention measures for malaria and other mosquito-borne illnesses. This may include purchasing and taking antimalarial medication, getting a medical and dental check-up.

translations.source: በቆይታ ጊዜ የመድሃኒት ማዘዣዎችን ማግኘት እና ተስማሚ የሕክምና ኪት ማሰባሰብ; በውጭ አገር ተገቢ የሆነ የመድን ዋስትና ያለው የህክምና ኢንሹራንስ መግዛት በሚጓዙበት ጊዜ፦ በደንብ የበሰለ ምግብ ብቻ ይበሉ እና የታሸገ ወይም የታሸጉ ቀዝቃዛ መጠጦችን ብቻ ይጠጡ። ደህንነት አጠራጣሪ ከሆነ የመጠጥ ውሃ አፍሉት።
translations.reference: obtain prescriptions for medication according to length of stay and assemble a suitable medical kit; purchase medical insurance with appropriate cover abroad During travel: eat only thoroughly cooked food and drink only bottled or packaged cold drinks. Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful.
translations.hypothesis:
During your trip, ensure you have access to necessary medications and assemble a suitable medical kit. When traveling abroad:
*   Eat only well-cooked food and drink only bottled or canned cold beverages.
*   If you are unsure about the safety of drinking water, boil it or use bottled water.

translations.source: ከትራፊክ, ከእንስሳት, ከአለርጂዎች, ከፀሃይ እና ከስፖርት ጋር የተያያዙ አደጋዎችን ወይም ችግሮችን ማወቅ; የደም ቡድንዎን የሚያሳይ ካርድ ይያዙ; አለርጂ ካለብዎ ወይም ሥር የሰደደ በሽታ ካለብዎ የሕክምና ማንቂያ አምባር ያድርጉ ተጓዦች በሚመለሱበት ጊዜ የሚከተሉትን ካደረጉ የሕክምና ምርመራ ማድረግ አለባቸው. ወባ ካለበት ወይም ሊኖር ከሚችልበት አገር ትኵሳት ይዞ መመለስ።
translations.reference: be aware of accidents or problems related to traffic, animals, allergies, sun and sport; carry a card showing your blood group; wear a medical alert bracelet, if you have allergies or a chronic disease Travellers should obtain a medical examination on their return if they: return with a fever from a country where malaria is or may be present
translations.hypothesis: Be aware of potential risks or problems related to traffic, animals, allergies, sun exposure, and sports; carry a card showing your blood type; if you have any allergies or underlying health conditions, wear a medical alert bracelet; upon arrival, travelers should undergo a medical examination. If you are traveling to or from a region where malaria is present or may be present, take malaria prophylaxis.

translations.source: ሥር በሰደደ በሽታ ይሰቃያሉ ወደ ቤት ከተመለሱ በኋላ ባሉት ሳምንታት ውስጥ ህመም አጋጥሟቸዋል በጉዞ ላይ እያሉ የወባ ህክምና አግኝተዋል ለከባድ ተላላፊ በሽታ ሊጋለጥ ይችላል በማደግ ላይ ባለ ሀገር ከ3 ወራት በላይ አሳልፏል
translations.reference: suffer from a chronic disease experience illness in the weeks following their return home received treatment for malaria while travelling may have been exposed to a serious infectious disease spent more than 3 months in a developing country
translations.hypothesis: After returning from their travels, individuals who contracted dengue fever experience symptoms in the following weeks. They were able to obtain treatment for the disease while traveling, which has been a significant factor in preventing it from developing into a severe and potentially life-threatening condition. The disease has been prevalent in the country for over three months.

translations.source: የሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ የሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ የሚከሰተው በባክቴሪያ (ማይኮባክቲሪየም ቲዩበርክሎዝስ) ሲሆን አብዛኛውን ጊዜ በሳንባዎች ላይ ጉዳት ያደርሳል። የሳንባ ነቀርሳ ያለባቸው ሰዎች ሲያስሉ፣ ሲያስነትሱ ወይም ሲተፉ የሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ በአየር ይተላለፋል። አንድ ሰው በበሽታው ለመያዝ ጥቂት ጀርሞችን ብቻ መተንፈስ ያስፈልገዋል። በየዓመቱ 10 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች በሳንባ ነቀርሳ (ቲቢ) ይታመማሉ.
translations.reference: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB).
translations.hypothesis: Overview of Tuberculosis: Tuberculosis is caused by the bacterium *Mycobacterium tuberculosis*, and typically affects the lungs. When a person with tuberculosis coughs, sneezes, or speaks, they can spread the bacteria through the air. It only takes a few bacteria to infect a person. An estimated 10 million people worldwide contract tuberculosis each year.

translations.source: ሊታከም የሚችል እና ሊድን የሚችል በሽታ ቢሆንም፣ 1.5 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች በቲቢ በየዓመቱ ይሞታሉ - ይህም የአለማችን ቀዳሚ ተላላፊ ገዳይ ነው። የሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ ኤችአይቪ ላለባቸው ሰዎች ቀዳሚው ሞት ምክንያት ሲሆን ለፀረ-ተህዋሲያን መቋቋምም ትልቅ አስተዋፅዖ አለው። አብዛኛዎቹ በሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ የታመሙ ሰዎች ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች ውስጥ ይኖራሉ, ነገር ግን ቲቢ በመላው ዓለም ይገኛል. በቲቢ ከተያዙ ሰዎች መካከል ግማሽ ያህሉ በ8 አገሮች ውስጥ ይገኛሉ፡ ባንግላዲሽ፣ ቻይና፣ ህንድ፣ ኢንዶኔዥያ፣ ናይጄሪያ፣ ፓኪስታን፣ ፊሊፒንስ እና ደቡብ አፍሪካ። ከዓለም ሕዝብ አንድ አራተኛ የሚሆኑት በሳንባ ነቀርሳ ባክቴሪያ እንደተያዙ ይገመታል፣ ነገር ግን አብዛኛዎቹ ሰዎች የሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ አይይዛቸውም እና አንዳንዶቹ ኢንፌክሽኑን ያስወግዳሉ።
translations.reference: Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.hypothesis: Although tuberculosis (TB) is a curable and preventable disease, 1.5 million people die from it each year – making it the leading cause of death worldwide. TB is the leading cause of death among people living with HIV, and it also contributes significantly to the development of drug resistance. The majority of people with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is a global problem. Half of all TB deaths occur in just eight countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, the Philippines, and South Africa. It is estimated that over a quarter of the world's population is infected with TB bacteria, but most people do not develop the disease and some are able to clear the infection.

translations.source: በበሽታው የተያዙ ግን (እስካሁን) በበሽታው ያልታመሙ ሰዎች ሊያስተላልፉ አይችሉም። በሳንባ ነቀርሳ ባክቴሪያ የተጠቁ ሰዎች በሳንባ ነቀርሳ የመያዝ ዕድላቸው 5-10% ነው። እንደ ኤች አይ ቪ፣ የተመጣጠነ ምግብ እጥረት ወይም የስኳር በሽታ ያለባቸው ወይም ትንባሆ የሚያጨሱ ሰዎች የመታመም ዕድላቸው ከፍተኛ ነው። ምልክቶች፡ የተለመዱ የቲቢ በሽታ ምልክቶች፡- ረዥም ሳል፣ የደረት ህመም፣ ድክመት ወይም ድካም፣ ክብደት መቀነስ፣ ትኩሳት፣ የምሽት ላብ ብዙውን ጊዜ እነዚህ ምልክቶች ለበርካታ ወራቶች ቀላል ይሆናሉ፣ ስለሆነም እንክብካቤን ለመፈለግ መዘግየት እና ኢንፌክሽኑን ለሌሎች የማሰራጨት አደጋን ይጨምራሉ።
translations.reference: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others.
translations.hypothesis: However, individuals with the disease cannot transmit it to others (at least not yet). People infected with tuberculosis have a 5-10% chance of developing the disease. Individuals with HIV, malnutrition, or diabetes are at a higher risk of developing the disease. Symptoms: Common TB symptoms include: prolonged cough, chest pain, weakness or fatigue, weight loss, fever, and night sweats. These symptoms can persist for weeks or even months, which can lead to delays in seeking treatment and an increased risk of spreading the infection to others.

translations.source: የጤና እንክብካቤ አቅራቢው በሽተኛው የቲቢ በሽታ እንዳለበት ከጠረጠረ በሽተኛውን ለምርመራ ይልካሉ። የሳንባ ሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ በተጠረጠረበት ጊዜ ታካሚዎች የሳንባ ነቀርሳ ባክቴሪያዎችን ለመመርመር የሽንት ናሙና እንዲሰጡ ይጠየቃሉ። የሳንባ ላልሆነ የሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ፣ የተጎዱ የሰውነት ፈሳሾች እና ሕብረ ህዋሳቶች ናሙናዎች ሊሞከሩ ይችላሉ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የሳንባ ነቀርሳ በሽታ ምልክቶችን እና ምልክቶችን ለሚያሳዩ ሰዎች የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ ምርመራዎች ፈጣን የሞለኪውላር ምርመራዎችን ይመክራል። ሌሎች የመመርመሪያ መሣሪያዎች የፈሳሽ ንጣፍ ማይክሮስኮፒ እና የደረት ኤክስ-ሬይዎችን ሊያካትቱ ይችላሉ።
translations.reference: If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria.
