translations.reference: Treatment: There is currently no vaccine for yaws. Health education and improved personal hygiene are the most important tactics for prevention. Two antibiotics can be used to treat yaws. A single oral dose of azithromycin (30 mg/kg, maximum 2 g) is the recommended treatment. Benzathine penicillin (single intramuscular dose) at 0.6 million units (children aged under 10 years) and 1.2 million units (people aged over 10 years) can be used for patients with suspected clinical treatment failure after azithromycin, or patients who cannot be treated with azithromycin.
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: There is currently no vaccine available. Good hygiene and health education are the most important preventive measures. Two antibiotics can be used to treat the infection. The recommended treatment is azithromycin (30 mg/kg, twice daily). Benzathine penicillin (a single intramuscular injection) can be used at a dose of 0.6 million units (for children under 10 years) or 1.2 million units (for adults over 10 years) after azithromycin has failed to treat the infection.

translations.source: ይህ ህክምና በ95በመቶ ጒዳዮች ላይ ሙሉ ለሙሉ ክሊኒካዊ ፈውስ ያስከትላል። ነገር ግን ታካሚዎች አንቲባዮቲኮችን ከተቀበሉ ከ 4 ሳምንታት በኋላ እንደገና መመርመር አለባቸው። የ1950ዎቹ ታሪካዊ ሰነዶች ግምገማ እንደሚያሳየው ከ90 የሚበልጡ አገሮች እና ግዛቶች ለ ያውስ የተጋለጡ ነበሩ። ከ90ቱ አገራት እና ግዛቶች ውስጥ 14ቱ ብቻ በዘወትር ክትትል ሥርዓት ላይ የተመሰረቱ የቅርብ ጊዜ መረጃዎች አሏቸው። ይሁን እንጂ እነዚህ አሃዞች የበሽታውን መኖር የሚጠቁሙ እንጂ ሙሉውን ስፋት የሚጠቁሙ አይደሉም። ጋና፣ ፓፑዋ ኒው ጊኒ እና የሰለሞን ደሴቶች በዓመት ከ10,000 በላይ ጉዳዮችን ሪፖርት አድርገዋል። ኢኳዶር እና ህንድ ስርጭቱን ያቋረጡ ይመስላል።
translations.reference: This treatment will result in complete clinical healing in 95% of cases but patients should be re-examined 4 weeks after receiving the antibiotics. A review of the historic documents from 1950s shows that over 90 countries and territories were endemic for yaws. Only 14 out of the 90 countries and territories have recent data on yaws based on the routine surveillance system; however, these figures may just be an indication of the presence of the disease and not its full extent. Ghana, Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands report over 10 000 cases per year. Ecuador and India appear to have interrupted transmission.
translations.hypothesis: This treatment has a 95% success rate in completely curing the disease. However, patients need to be re-examined 4 weeks after discontinuing the medication. Historical documents from the 1950s show that over 90% of countries and regions were affected by the disease. Of these 90 countries and regions, only 14 have current data on regular screening programs. However, these figures do not necessarily reflect the full extent of the disease's prevalence. Ghana, Papua New Guinea, and the Solomon Islands have reported over 10,000 cases per year. Ecuador and India appear to have successfully eradicated the disease.

translations.source: ቢጫ ወባ አጠቃላይ እይታ፡ ቢጫ ወባ በወባ ትንኞች ንክሻ ወደ ሰው የሚተላለፍ የቫይረስ በሽታ ነው። ለወረርሽኝ የተጋለጠ ሲሆን በክትባትም መከላከል ይቻላል። እነዚህ በቀን የሚነክሱ ትንኞች በቤቶች ዙሪያ( ቤት ውስጥ)፣ በጫካዎች ወይም በጫካዎች( ዱር)፣ ወይም በሁለቱም መኖሪያዎች( ግማሽ-ቤት ውስጥ) ይራባሉ።. ቢጫ ወባ ከፍተኛ ተጽዕኖ ያለው እና ከፍተኛ ስጋት ያለው በሽታ ሲሆን በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ የመሰራጨት ስጋት ያለው ሲሆን ለዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ደህንነትም ስጋት ሊሆን ይችላል።
translations.reference: Yellow fever Overview: Yellow fever is a viral disease that is transmitted to humans by the bites of infected mosquitoes. It is prone to epidemics and is preventable with a vaccine. These day-biting mosquitoes breed around houses (domestic), in forests or jungles (wild), or in both habitats (semi-domestic). Yellow fever is a high-impact, high-threat disease, with a risk of international spread, representing a potential threat to global health security.
translations.hypothesis: White-tailed rabbit: White-tailed rabbits are a type of wild rabbit that can transmit a virus to humans. The virus is spread through contact with infected rabbits, and there is no vaccine available to prevent the infection. White-tailed rabbits are found in a variety of habitats, including forests, grasslands, and suburban areas. They are a significant public health concern, as they can transmit the virus to humans and cause serious illness.

translations.source: 3 አይነት የመተላለፊያ ዑደቶች አሉ፡ ሲልቫቲክ (ወይንም ጫካ) ቢጫ ወባ ዝንጀሮዎች በዱር ትንኞች ሲነከሱ ቫይረሱን ወደ ሌሎች ጦጣዎችና ሰዎች ያስተላልፋሉ። መካከለኛ ቢጫ ወባ የሚሰራጨው በከፊል የቤት ውስጥ ትንኞች ጦጣዎችን እና ሰዎችን ሲያጠቁ ነው። ይህ በአፍሪካ ውስጥ በጣም የተለመደ የበሽታ ወረርሽኝ ነው። የከተሞች ቢጫ ወባ በበሽታው የተጠቁ ሰዎች ቫይረሱ በከፍተኛ የትንኝ ብዛት እና ሰዎች አነስተኛ በሽታ የመከላከል አቅም ባላቸው በከፍተኛ ሁኔታ በሚኖሩባቸው አካባቢዎች ውስጥ ሲያስገቡ ትልቅ ወረርሽኝ ሊያስከትል ይችላል። አልፎ አልፎ በበሽታው የተጠቁ ተጓዦች ጒዳዮችን ከቢጫ ወባ ነጻ ወደሆኑ ሀገሮች ይልካሉ።
translations.reference: There are 3 types of transmission cycles: Sylvatic (or jungle) yellow fever spreads when monkeys are bitten by wild mosquitoes that pass the virus on to other monkeys and humans. Intermediate yellow fever spreads when semi-domestic mosquitoes infect both monkeys and people. This is the most common type of outbreak in Africa. Urban yellow fever can cause large epidemics when infected people introduce the virus into heavily populated areas with high mosquito density and where people have little immunity. Occasionally, infected travellers have exported cases to countries that are free of yellow fever.
translations.hypothesis: There are three main types of transmission cycles: the sylvatic cycle, the urban cycle, and the sporadic cycle. The sylvatic cycle involves white-tailed deer and ticks, with the virus being transmitted from deer to ticks and then to other animals and humans. The urban cycle involves domestic animals, such as dogs and cats, as well as humans. This cycle is common in many parts of Africa. The sporadic cycle involves isolated cases of the disease, often in areas with high deer populations and low human immunity. Occasionally, infected travelers may bring the disease to new areas.

translations.source: ይሁን እንጂ በሽታው በቀላሉ ወደ አዲስ አገር ሊዛመት የሚችለው በሽታውን ሊያስተላልፉ የሚችሉ የትንኝ ዝርያዎች፣ የተወሰኑ የአየር ንብረት ሁኔታዎች እንዲሁም በሽታውን ለመከላከል የሚያስፈልጉ እንስሳት ካሉ ብቻ ነው። ምልክቶች: ቢጫ ወባ በከባድ እና በበርካታ ምልክቶች ሊታይ ይችላል። አንዴ ከተያዘ በኋላ የቢጫ ወባ ቫይረስ በሰውነት ውስጥ ለ 3-6 ቀናት ይፈለፈላል። ብዙ ሰዎች ምልክቶች አያጋጥሟቸውም። ነገር ግን ምልክቶች በሚትዩበት ጊዜ በጣም የተለመዱት ትኵሳት፣ የጡንቻ ሕመም በከፍተኛ የጀርባ ሕመም፣ ራስ ምታት፣ የምግብ ፍላጐት መቀነስ እና ማቅለሽለሽ ወይም ማቅለሽ ናቸው። በአብዛኛዎቹ ሁኔታዎች ምልክቶቹ ከ3 እስከ 4 ቀናት በኋላ ይጠፋሉ።
translations.reference: However, the disease can only spread easily to a new country if there are mosquito species able to transmit it, specific climatic conditions, and the animal reservoir needed to maintain it. Symptoms: Yellow fever can present with a wide range of symptoms and severity. Once contracted, the yellow fever virus incubates in the body for 3–6 days. Many people do not experience symptoms, but when these do occur, the most common are fever, muscle pain with prominent backache, headache, loss of appetite, and nausea or vomiting. In most cases, symptoms disappear after 3–4 days.
translations.hypothesis: However, the disease can easily spread to new areas if it is transmitted by certain types of mosquitoes, specific weather conditions, and animals that are susceptible to the disease. Symptoms: White-flies can be difficult to detect, as they are small and often found on the undersides of leaves. Once a white-fly infestation has taken hold, the virus can spread rapidly throughout the plant. Common symptoms include: yellowing of leaves, stunted growth, and the appearance of small, white eggs on the undersides of leaves. In some cases, the infestation can also cause the plant to die.

translations.source: አነስተኛ ቍጥር ያላቸው ታካሚዎች ከመጀመሪያዎቹ ምልክቶች ካገገሙ በ 24 ሰዓታት ውስጥ ወደ ሁለተኛው፣ የበለጠ መርዛማ ደረጃ ይገባሉ። ከፍተኛ ትኩሳት ይመለሳል እና ብዙ የሰውነት ስርዓቶች ይጎዳሉ። አብዛኛውን ጊዜ ጉበት እና ኩላሊት: በዚህ ደረጃ ላይ ሰዎች ቢጫነት( የቆዳ እና የዓይን ቢጫነት፣ቢጫ ወባ የሚለው ስም ለዛ ነው)፣ ጥቁር ሽንት እና የሆድ ሕመም ከማስመለስ ጋር የማሳ ዕየትድላቸው ሰፊ ነው።. የደም መፍሰስ ከአፍ፣ ከአፍንጫ፣ ከዓይን ወይም ከሆድ ሊከሰት ይችላል። ከእነዚህ ታካሚዎች መካከል ግማሾቹ ከ7 እስከ 10 ቀናት ውስጥ ይሞታሉ። ቢጫ ወባ በተለይም በመጀመሪያ ደረጃዎች ላይ በምርመራ ለመለየት አስቸጋሪ ነው።
translations.reference: A small percentage of patients enter a second, more toxic phase within 24 hours of recovering from initial symptoms. High fever returns and several body systems are affected, usually the liver and kidneys. In this phase, people are likely to develop jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes, hence the name yellow fever), dark urine, and abdominal pain with vomiting. Bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes, or stomach. Half of these patients die within 7–10 days.Yellow fever is difficult to diagnose, especially during the early stages.
translations.hypothesis: Patients with a small number of symptoms can progress to a more severe stage within 24 hours of initial diagnosis. This is characterized by high fever and damage to multiple organ systems. Common symptoms include jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes), dark urine, and abdominal pain. Bleeding can occur from the mouth, nose, eyes, or abdomen. In many of these patients, half will die within 7 to 10 days. Jaundice can be particularly difficult to detect in the early stages.

translations.source: በጣም ከባድ የሆኑ ጒዳዮች ከከባድ የወባ በሽታ፣ ከሌፕቶስፒሮሲስ፣ ከቫይራል ሄፓታይተስ( በተለይም ነበልባል ዓይነቶች)፣ ከሌሎች የደም መፍሰስ ትኵሳት፣ ከሌሎች ፍላቪቫይረሶች( እንደ ዴንጊ የደም መፍሰስ ትኵሳት) እና ከመመረዝ ጋር ግራ ሊጋቡ ይችላሉ። የፖሊሜራዝ ሰንሰለት ግብረመልስ( ፒሲአር) ምርመራ በደም እና በሽንት ውስጥ አንዳንድ ጊዜ ቫይረሱን በበሽታው የመጀመሪያ ደረጃዎች ውስጥ መለየት ይችላል። በኋለኞቹ ደረጃዎች ፀረ እንግዳ አካላትን ለመለየት ምርመራ ያስፈልጋል( አይጂኤም)። ህክምና፡- የቢጫ ትኵሳት ኢንፌክሽኖች ከባድ በሽታ ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ። ከባድ ከሆነ ደግሞ ለሞት ሊዳረጉ ይችላሉ። በሆስፒታሎች ውስጥ ቀደም ብሎ የሚደረግ ድጋፍ ሰጪ ህክምና የመዳንን ፍጥነት ያሻሽላል።
translations.reference: More severe cases can be confused with severe malaria, leptospirosis, viral hepatitis (especially fulminant forms), other haemorrhagic fevers, infection with other flaviviruses (such as dengue haemorrhagic fever) and poisoning. Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing in blood and urine can sometimes detect the virus in early stages of the disease. In later stages, testing to identify antibodies is needed (IgM). Treatment: Yellow fever infections can cause serious illness and can be fatal in severe cases. Early supportive treatment in hospitals improves survival rates.
translations.hypothesis: Severe cases of these diseases can be mistaken for more serious conditions such as severe malaria, leptospirosis, viral hepatitis (especially the more severe forms), other forms of hemorrhagic fever, and other flaviviruses (such as Dengue fever). Polymerase chain reaction (PCR) testing can sometimes detect the virus in blood and urine, even in the early stages of the infection. In later stages, serological tests (ELISA) are needed to detect antibodies. Treatment: Infections with these viruses can cause serious illness, and in some cases, can be fatal. Early supportive care in hospitals can improve the chances of survival.

translations.source: በአሁኑ ጊዜ ለቢጫ ትኵሳት የተወሰነ የፀረ-ቫይረስ መድኃኒት የለም። ነገር ግን የውኃ ማጣት፣ ትኵሳት እና የጉበት እና የኩላሊት ውድቀት ውጤቶችን ያሻሽላል። ተያያዥነት ያላቸው የባክቴሪያ ኢንፌክሽኖች በፀረ-ተባይ መድሃኒቶች ሊታከሙ ይችላሉ. ታካሚዎች በወባ ትንኝ ንክሻ ወደሌሎች የመዛመት አደጋን ለመገደብ በቀን ውስጥ በወባ ትንኝ አጎበር ስር መቆየት አለባቸው። ቢጫ ወባን ለመከላከል በጣም አስፈላጊው እርምጃ ክትባት ነው። ወረርሽኙን መከላከል የሚቻለው አብዛኛው ህዝብ ክትባት ክትባት ከወሰደ ብቻ ነው።
translations.reference: There is currently no specific anti-viral drug for yellow fever, but specific care to treat dehydration, fever, and liver and kidney failure improves outcomes. Associated bacterial infections can be treated with antibiotics. Patients need to stay under mosquito nets during the day to limit the risk of spreading to others through mosquito bites. Vaccination is the single most important measure for preventing yellow fever. The prevention of outbreaks can only be achieved if the majority of the population is immunized.
translations.hypothesis: Currently, there is no specific antiviral medication available for amoebic dysentery. However, adequate hydration, rest, and treatment of associated bacterial infections can help to alleviate symptoms. Certain antibiotics may be effective in treating amoebic dysentery. Patients should also avoid contact with contaminated water sources to reduce the risk of transmission. The most important step in preventing amoebic dysentery is vaccination. Most people can be protected from the disease by receiving the appropriate vaccine.

translations.source: የቢጫ ወባ ክትባቱ ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ እና መገኘት የሚችል ነው። እና አንድ ነጠላ መጠን ከበሽታው እስከ ህይወት የሚዘልቅ መከላከያ ይሰጣል። ከቢጫ ወባ በሽታ ያገገሙ ሰዎችም ለሕይወት ይጠበቃሉ። ስለ ቢጫ ወባ ክትባት የበለጠ ያንብቡ። የወባ ትንኝ ቁጥጥር ቢጫ ወባን ለመከላከል ይረዳል። እና የክትባት ሽፋን ዝቅተኛ በሆነበት ወይም ክትባቱ ወዲያውኑ ሊገኝ በማይችልበት ሁኔታ ውስጥ በጣም አስፈላጊ ነው። የወባ ትንኝ ቁጥጥር ከፍተኛ የወባ ትንኝ በሚበዛባቸው አካባቢዎች ትንኞች የሚራቡባቸውን ቦታዎች ማስወገድ እና የጎልማሳ ትንኞችን እና እጮችን መግደልን ያጠቃልላል።
translations.reference: The yellow fever vaccine is safe and affordable, and a single dose provides life-long immunity against the disease. People who recover from yellow fever infection are also protected for life. Read more about the yellow fever vaccine. Mosquito control can also help to prevent yellow fever and is vital in situations where vaccination coverage is low or the vaccine is not immediately available. Mosquito control includes eliminating sites where mosquitoes can breed and killing adult mosquitoes and larvae by using insecticides in areas with high mosquito density.
translations.hypothesis: The measles vaccine is safe and effective. A single dose provides lifelong protection against the disease. People who have been vaccinated against measles are also protected from the disease. Read more about the measles vaccine. Mosquito control can help to prevent measles. This is especially important in areas where vaccination rates are low or where vaccines are not readily available. Mosquito control includes eliminating breeding sites for mosquitoes, such as stagnant water, and killing adult mosquitoes and larvae.

translations.source: የማህበረሰቡ ተሳትፎ: ለምሳሌ የቤት ውስጥ ፍሳሾችን ማፅዳትና ትንኞች ሊራቡ የሚችሉባቸውን የውኃ ማጠራቀሚያዎች መሸፈን ትንኞችን ለመቈጣጠር አስፈላጊና ውጤታማ መንገድ ነው።
translations.reference: Community involvement through activities, such as cleaning household drains and covering water containers where mosquitoes can breed is an important and effective way to control mosquitoes.
translations.hypothesis: Community involvement: For example, cleaning up litter and covering drains to prevent flooding are effective and important ways to manage waste and protect the community.

translations.source: የሕክምና ፍላጎት ያላቸው ሰዎች "በሥቃይ ወደ ኋላ ቀርተዋል" ሲል አዲስ ሪፖርት አጋልጧል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) ስለ ሞርፊን (ህመምን ማስታገሻ መድሃኒት) ለህክምና አገልግሎት ማግኘትን አስመልክቶ አዲስ ሪፖርት አሳትሟል። ይህም የሞርፊን አለም አቀፋዊ ስርጭት እንደ ወሳኝ የህመም መድሃኒት እንዴት እኩል እንዳልሆነ እና የሕክምና ፍላጎቱን እንደማያሟላ ይገልጻል። ሪፖርቱ "በህመም ውስጥ ወደ ኋላ ቀርቷል" በሚል ርዕስ፤ ይህንን አስፈላጊ መድሃኒት የማግኘት ችግሮችን አጉልቶ ያሳያል እና በተመጣጣኝ ፖሊሲ ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ ተደራሽነትን ለማሻሻል እርምጃዎችን ያቀርባል። ምንም እንኳን ሞርፊን ለጠንካራ ህመም ማስታገሻ ውጤታማ እና በአንጻራዊነት ዝቅተኛ ዋጋ ያለው መድሃኒት እንዲሁም እ.ኤ.አ. ከ 1977 ጀምሮ በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ሞዴል አስፈላጊ መድሃኒቶች ዝርዝር ውስጥ የተዘረዘረ ቢሆንም፤ በተለያዩ አገሮች መካከል ያለው ተደራሽነት ልዩነት በጣም ሰፊ ነው። ከፍተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች እና ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች መካከል አማካይ የሞርፊን ፍጆታ ላይ ከ5 እስከ 63 እጥፍ ልዩነት አለ።
translations.reference: People with medical needs are “left behind in pain” reveals new report The World Health Organization (WHO) has published a new report on access to morphine for medical use, which describes how the global distribution of morphine, as a vital pain medicine, is unequal and does not fulfil the medical need. The report, titled “Left behind in pain”, highlights the problems in access to this essential medicine and offers actions to improve safe access through balanced policy. Despite morphine being an effective and relatively low-cost medicine for relieving strong pain, listed since 1977 in the first edition of the WHO Model list of Essential Medicines, the disparity in access across countries is stark. There is a 5- to 63-fold difference in the estimated median consumption of morphine between high-income countries and lower income countries.
translations.hypothesis: A new report has revealed that people in need of medical care are "falling behind" in accessing morphine, a powerful painkiller. The World Health Organization (WHO) has released a report on the global availability of morphine, highlighting how its widespread use as a crucial pain medication is not being met by adequate access. The report, titled "Falling Behind," emphasizes the challenges in obtaining this essential medicine and proposes measures to improve its safe and equitable availability. While morphine is an effective and relatively inexpensive painkiller, and has been included in the WHO's list of essential medicines since 1977, there is a significant disparity in its availability across different countries. There is a difference of up to 5 to 63 times in average morphine consumption between high-income and low-income countries.

translations.source: ፍጆታው ተመሳሳይ ሀብት ባላቸው አገሮች መካከልም ጉልህ አለው። ፍጆታው ካለው የህክምና ፍላጎት ጋር አይዛመድም። ይህም የታየው በማይድን ህመም ተይዘው በከባድ የትንፋሽ ማጠር ወይም ከባድ የህመም ስሜት የተሰቃዩበትን የቀናት ብዛት በማጥናት ነው። ይህ መረጃ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2018 ላንሴት ኮሚሽን (Lancet Commission) ያወጣውን መረጃ የሚያስተጋባ ነው። መረጃውም የህመም ማስታገሻ መድሃኒት እጥረት 'በአለም አቀፍ ጤና ውስጥ ካሉት እጅግ አስጸያፊ እና የተደበቀ ኢፍትሃዊ ድርጊቶች አንዱ' ሲል የገለፀው ሲሆን፤ 10 በመቶዎቹ ሃብታም ሀገራት 90% የሚሆነውን የሞርፊን ማስታገሻ መድሃኒት ይጠቀማሉ። "ለህመም ማስታገሻ ውጤታማ መድሃኒቶች ባሉበት ሁኔታ ሰዎችን በህመም ውስጥ መተው በተለይም በመጨረሻው የህይወት ዘመን እንክብካቤ ላይ፤ ለፖሊሲ አውጪዎች በጣም አሳሳቢ ጉዳይ ሊሆን ይገባል።" ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት መድኃኒቶች እና የጤና ምርቶች አጠቃላይ ረዳት ዳይሬክተር ዩኪኮ ናካታኒ ተናግረዋል። "በየትኛውም ቦታ በተመጣጣኝ ፖሊሲ ለህክምና ፈላጊዎች ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ እና ወቅታዊ የሆነ የሞርፊን ፍላጎት መፍትሄ ማግኘት አለብን።"
translations.reference: Consumption pattern varies significantly across countries of similar wealth; it does not correspond to medical need, as indicated by the estimated number of days people are in pain or suffer with severe shortness of breath for people with a terminal illness. This data echoes that of the 2018 Lancet Commission which described the lack of access to pain relief medication as ‘one of the most heinous, hidden inequities in global health’ with the richest 10% of countries possessing 90% of distributed morphine-equivalent opioids. “Leaving people in pain when effective medicines are available for pain management, especially in the context of end-of-life care, should be a cause of serious concern for policy-makers,” says Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General for Medicines and Health Products. “We must urgently advocate for safe and timely access to morphine for those in medical need through balanced policy, everywhere.”
translations.hypothesis: The disparity in access to pain relief medication is significant even between countries with similar levels of wealth. This is evident in the number of people who suffer from chronic pain and are unable to access adequate treatment, often due to a lack of affordable medication. This observation aligns with data from the 2018 Lancet Commission, which highlighted "the lack of access to effective pain relief medication as one of the most alarming and hidden injustices in global health." The report found that the wealthiest 10% of countries account for 90% of the global supply of morphine. "For policymakers, the fact that people are being left to suffer from pain, particularly in the final stages of life, despite the availability of effective pain relief medications, should be a major concern," said Yuki Nakatani, Director of Medicines and Health Products at the World Health Organization. "We need to ensure that everyone, regardless of where they live, has access to a safe and timely supply of morphine through appropriate policies."

translations.source: የልዩነት ምክንያቶች የሞርፊን ለህክምና አገልግሎት ተደራሽነት መለያየት ብዙ ምክኒያቶች አሉጥ። ከመልካም አስተዳደር ጋር የተያያዙ አስፈፃሚዎች፤ አስተማማኝ / ቀልጣፋ የግዢ እና የአቅርቦት ሂደቶች፤ የሃብት አቅርቦት እና የአቅም ግንባታ ስራዎች፤ ከመጠን በላይ ገዳቢ ህግ እና ፖሊሲዎች፤ በቂ ያልሆነ አገልግሎት፤ አቅርቦት እና የተሳሳተ ግንዛቤ ውይም አመለካከት የተወሰኑት ናቸው። እንደየሃገራቱ ምቸ ሁኔታዎች፣ እንቅፋቶች እና ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸው ነገሮች ይለያያሉ። በዚህ ሪፖርት ላይ ከ105 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል አገሮች ምላሽ ሰጪዎች ጋር የቀረበው የባለድርሻ አካላት ጥናት፣ ፣ ይህንን ልዩነት ያረጋግጣል። ለምሳሌ ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት ጤና ተቋማት ውስጥ ያለው የሞርፊን እና ሌሎች ጠንካራ opioids (መካከለኛና ከባድ ህመምን ለማስታገስ የሚያገለግሉ የመድሃኒት አይነቶች) አቅርቦት በገንዘብ እጥረት ምክንያት ከፍተኛ ገቢ ካላቸው ሀገራት ጋር ሲወዳደር አነስተኛ መሆኑ ተጠቅሷል። በሁሉም ክልሎች ውስጥ ከአንድ ሶስተኛ በላይ የሚሆኑት ተናጋሪዎች ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ ተደራሽነትን ለማግኘት የህግ አውጭ እና የቁጥጥር ማዕቀፍ ያለውን ጠቀሜታ በመገንዘብ ከህግ አውጭ እና ተቆጣጣሪ በኩል የሚነሱ መሰናክሎችን ተመልክተዋል።
translations.reference: Reasons for the disparity Access to morphine for medical use is influenced by many interacting factors including enablers related to good governance, reliable/efficient procurement and supply processes, resource availability and capacity-building activities, along with barriers related to overly restrictive legislation and policies, inadequate service provision, and misinformed attitudes and perception. Given different country contexts, enablers, barriers and priorities for action vary; a stakeholder survey presented in this report, with respondents from 105 WHO Member States, confirms this variation. For example, irregular supply of morphine and other strong opioids at health facilities due to limited financing were commonly noted as a barrier in low- and middle-income countries compared to high-income countries. More than a third of respondents across all regions also noted barriers arising from legislative and regulatory factors while recognizing the importance of a legislative and regulatory framework in achieving safe access.
translations.hypothesis: Several factors contribute to the disparity in access to morphine for medical use. These include: inefficient administration; unreliable/ineffective procurement and supply processes; inadequate infrastructure and capacity building; overly restrictive laws and policies; insufficient service provision; and a lack of awareness or understanding. The specific challenges and priorities vary depending on the country's context. This report, which presents the findings of a study conducted with 105 member states of the World Health Organization, highlights this disparity. For example, the availability of morphine and other strong opioids (medications used to treat moderate to severe pain) in low- and middle-income countries is significantly lower than in high-income countries, due to a lack of resources. More than two-thirds of respondents across all regions recognize the importance of legal and regulatory frameworks in ensuring safe and equitable access to morphine.

translations.source: መድሃኒቱ ጥቅም ላይ እንዳይውል የሚከለክሉ ያለአግባብ የተቀመጡ መስፈርቶች የታካሚውን ተደራሽነት ሊያደናቅፉ ይችላሉ። ይህም የሚሆነው የአቅርቦትን ፍሰት ስለሚያስተጓጉሉ እናም ለጤና እንክብካቤ ባለሙያዎች መድሃኒቶቹ ለታካሚ ማዘዝ እና ማከፋፈል አስቸጋሪ ስለሚያደርጉ ነው። የኦፒዮይድስ (መካከለኛና ከባድ ህመምን ለማስታገስ የሚያገለግሉ የመድሃኒት አይነቶች) ሊያስከትሉ ስለሚችሉት ጎጂ ውጤቶች ያሉት ስጋቶች ልክ ናቸው። እነዚህ መድሃኒቶች የኦፒዮይድስ አጠቃቀም እክል (opioid use disorder) ሊያስከትሉ እንደሚችሉ መገንዘብ ጠቃሚ ነው። በዚህ ምክንያት፣ ኦፒዮይድ (opioid) ሊያደርስ የሚችለውን ጉዳት (ለምሳሌ ለረጅም ጊዜ ካንሰር-ላልሆነ ህመም መጠቀም) ህብረተሰቡ አጠቃቀሙን በደንብ እስከተረዳ ድረስ ማስጠንቀቅ ለሕዝብ ጤና ጠቃሚ ነው። እንዲህ ያሉት ስጋቶች በክሊኒካዊ ሁኔታ እና በሰለጠኑ ባለሙያዎች ደህንነቱን በተጠበቀ መልኩ ጥቅም ላይ ሲውሉ የኦፒዮይድን (opioid) የተለያዩ ጥቅሞች ሊያሳንሰው አይገባም። ኦፖርቹኒቲስ ፎር አክሽን (Opportunities for action) በሪፖርቱ ለሞርፊን (የህመም ማስታገሻ መድሃኒት) ደህንነቱ የተጠበቀ ተደራሽነትን ለማሻሻል ዓላማ ያላቸውን ተጨማሪ የለውጥ ቦታዎችን ያቀርባል።
translations.reference: Unduly restrictive requirements could hamper patient access because they impede the flow of supply or make prescribing and dispensing difficult for health-care professionals. It is important to note that concerns about the potential harmful effects of opioids are valid, such as their potential to lead to opioid use disorder. For this reason, a certain amount of caution about the potential harms of opioid use (e.g. use in chronic non-cancer pain) is important for public health insofar as its use is well-informed and proportionate to risks. Such concerns should not undermine the benefits of opioid use when clinically indicated and when used safely by trained professionals. Opportunities for actionThe report presents a complementary set of areas for action that aim to improve safe access to morphine.
translations.hypothesis: Unnecessary restrictions on the use of medication can hinder access for patients, as they disrupt the flow of supplies and make it difficult for healthcare professionals to prescribe and distribute medications. Concerns about the potential for opioid-related harm (such as addiction and overdose) are also valid. It is important to recognize that these medications can be misused and to educate the public about the risks associated with opioid use. This will help to ensure that opioids are used safely and effectively, and that patients are able to access the medications they need without being exposed to unnecessary risks. The report also identifies opportunities for action to improve the safe and equitable access to morphine.

translations.source: እነዚህም የሞርፊን (የህመም ማስታገሻ መድሃኒት) ለህክምና አገልግሎት ተደራሽነትን ለማሻሻል አነስተኛ ክልላዊ መርሃ ግብሮችን በመተግበር በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) የአስፈላጊ መድሃኒቶች ዝርዝር መሰረት፤ በተዘጋጁ አስፈላጊ አገልግሎቶች እና የማስታገሻ እንክብካቤ ምርቶች ውስጥ ማካተት ነው። በተጨማሪም አስተዳደርን ማሻሻል፣ የግዥና የአቅርቦት ሂደቶችን ማቀላጠፍ፤ ማንኛውንም የውጤታማነት ጉድለት ለመቅረፍ እንደ የተረጋጋ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ያሉ የሀብት አቅርቦትን ማሻሻል፣ የጤና ሰራተኛን ብቁ ክህሎት ማሳደግ እና ስለ ኦፒዮይድ (መካከለኛና ከባድ ህመምን ለማስታገስ የሚያገለግሉ የመድሃኒት አይነቶች) ጥቅም እና ስለሚመጣው ጉዳት ግንዛቤ ማስጨበጥ ይገኙበታል። የእነዚህ ተግባራት ስኬት በሁሉም ባለድርሻ አካላት በአገር አቀፍ፣ በክልላዊ እና በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ትብብር ላይ የተመሰረተ ይሆናል።
translations.reference: These include implementing small-scale regional or state-wide programmes on improving access to morphine for medical use with a package of essential services and products for palliative care that is formulated according to the WHO Model List of Essential Medicines and the WHO Essential Package of Palliative Care. They also include improving governance, streamlining procurement and supply processes to address any inefficiency, improving resourcing such as stable funding, enhancing competent skill sets of the health workforce, and raising awareness about both the benefits and potential harms of opioid use. The success of these actions will hinge on collaboration and cooperation among all stakeholders at national, regional, and global levels.
translations.hypothesis: This involves implementing small-scale initiatives, based on the World Health Organization's (WHO) list of essential medicines, to improve access to morphine (a painkiller) for medical use, including ensuring its availability in designated healthcare facilities and providing appropriate pain management products. It also includes improving management, streamlining procurement and supply chains, enhancing financial resources such as stable funding, developing the skills of healthcare professionals, and raising awareness about the benefits and risks of opioids (medications used to treat moderate to severe pain). The success of these initiatives will depend on collaboration among all stakeholders at the national, regional, and global levels.

translations.source: ትናንሽ ደሴቶች ተላላፊ ባልሆኑ በሽታዎች ላይ እና የአእምሮ ጤና ላይ ቆራጥ የተግባር እቅድ ላይ ተስማሙ። በተለይም ለአደጋ ተጋላጭ በሆኑ፣ በትናንሽ ታዳጊ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶች (Small Island Developing States (SIDS)) ውስጥ ያሉ አንዳንድ የአለም ገዳይ በሽታዎችን ለመፍታት ያሰበ፣ በተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ( Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs)) እና በአእምሮ ጤና ችግሮች ላይ የወጣው እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023ቱ የብሪጅታውን መግለጫ (Bridgetown Declaration) ዛሬ በይፋ ተጀመረ። መግለጫው በአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO)፣ በመላው አሜሪካ የጤና ድርጅት (Pan American Health Organization (PAHO))እና በባርባዶስ መንግስት ጋር በመተባበር ተላላፊ ባልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና በአእምሮ ጤና ላይ የተዘጋጀው ከሰኔ 14 እስከ 16 የቆየው የትናንሽ ታዳጊ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶች ሚኒስትሮች ጉባኤ ቁልፍ ውጤት ነው። በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ 74% የሚሆነውን ሞት የሚያስከትሉት ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች በትናንሽ ታዳጊ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶች ላይ ያልተመጣጠነ ተፅዕኖ አላቸው፤ ይህም ከውጭ በሚገቡ ምግቦች፣ የንግድ ተጽእኖዎች እና የአየር ንብረት ቀውስ ላይ ጥገኛ ስለሆኑ ነው። አዲስ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዘገባ እንደሚያሳየው ከ30% በላይ በልብና በደም ሥር (cardiovascular) በሽታዎች፣ በካንሰር፣ በስኳር በሽታ ወይም ሥር በሰደደ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ የመጋለጥ እድል ካለባቸው 15 አገሮች ውስጥ 8ቱ ተላላፊ ባልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአእምሮ ጤና ውስጥ ናቸው።
translations.reference: Small islands agree bold plan of action on noncommunicable diseases and mental health Today, the 2023 Bridgetown Declaration on Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs) and Mental Health was launched, to address some of the world’s deadliest diseases in Small Island Developing States (SIDS), which are especially at risk. The Declaration is a key outcome of the SIDS Ministerial Conference on Noncommunicable Diseases (NCDs) and Mental Health, running from 14 to 16 June, co-hosted by the World Health Organization (WHO), the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) and the Government of Barbados. SIDS are disproportionately impacted by NCDs – which cause 74% of all deaths globally – due to their reliance on imported food, commercial influences and the climate crisis. A new WHO report shows that 8 of the 15 countries with more than a 30% risk of premature death from cardiovascular disease, cancer, diabetes, or chronic respiratory disease are SIDS.
translations.hypothesis: They agreed on a comprehensive plan to address non-communicable diseases and mental health challenges. Specifically, the Bridgetown Declaration, released in 2023, aims to tackle some of the world's deadliest non-communicable diseases (NCDs) and mental health issues, particularly in Small Island Developing States (SIDS) that are vulnerable to these threats. The declaration, a key outcome of the June 14-16 ministerial meeting of Small Island Developing States, was jointly developed by the World Health Organization (WHO), the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO), and the government of Barbados. Non-communicable diseases account for 74% of deaths globally, and they have a disproportionate impact on SIDS, largely due to factors such as imported foods, trade, and climate change. According to a new WHO report, more than 30% of people in 15 countries are at high risk of developing cardiovascular diseases, cancer, diabetes, or chronic respiratory diseases.

translations.source: በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ከፍተኛ የሆነ ከመጠን የዘለለ የውፍረት ችግር(Obesity) ያለባቸው 10 ሀገራት ሁሉም በፓስፊክ ውቅያኖስ ውስጥ የሚገኙ በትናንሽ ታዳጊ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶች ውስጥ ሲሆኑ፣ ከ45% በላይ የሚሆኑ ጎልማሶችም ከመጠን ባለፈ ውፍረት ውስጥ ይኖራሉ። የአእምሮ ጤና ሁኔታዎች በትናንሽ ታዳጊ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶች ውስጥ የተለመዱ ሲሆኑ፣ በካሪቢያን ውስጥ 15.2% የሚሆነውን ህዝብ እና በፓስፊክ ውቅያኖስ ውስጥ 11.2% የሚሆነውን ህዝብም እየጎዳ ይገኛል። የአእምሮ ጤና ችግር ያለባቸው ሰዎች ያለጊዜያቸው ለሞት የመጋለጥ እድላቸው ከፍተኛ ነው፤ ለዚህም ምክኒያቶቹ ያልተፈቱ የአካል ጤና ሁኔታዎች እና ራስን ማጥፋትን ያጠቃልላሉ። ማግለል፣ የሰለጠኑ ባለሙያዎች እጥረት እና የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ ተጽእኖ በነዚህ ደሴቶች ውስጥ አፋጣኝ ትኩረት ለሚፈልግ ፈታኝ ሁኔታ አስተዋፅዖ ያደርጋሉ። እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 የብሪጅታውን መግለጫ ( Bridgetown Declaration) ወደ ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአእምሮ ጤና ሁኔታዎች የሚያመሩ ማህበራዊ ፣አካባቢያዊ ፣ ኢኮኖሚያዊ እና የንግድ ጉዳዮችን ለመፍታት ጠንካራ እርምጃዎችን ይዘረዝራል።
translations.reference: The 10 countries with the highest obesity rates globally are all SIDS in the Pacific, where over 45% of adults live with obesity. Mental health conditions are common in SIDS countries, affecting an estimated 15.2% of the population in the Caribbean and 11.2% of the population in the Pacific. People with mental health conditions face a higher risk of premature mortality, including from unaddressed physical health conditions and from suicide. Stigma, specialized staff shortages and the impact of climate change contribute to a challenging situation in SIDS that requires immediate attention. The 2023 Bridgetown Declaration outlines bold steps to address the range of social, environmental, economic and commercial issues that lead to NCDs and mental health conditions.
translations.hypothesis: Ten countries with the highest rates of obesity in the world are all small, developing island nations in the Pacific Ocean. In these nations, over 45% of the population is overweight, in addition to being obese. Mental health issues are also common in these small island nations, affecting 15.2% of the population in the Caribbean and 11.2% of the population in the Pacific Ocean. People with mental health problems are at a higher risk of premature death, due to a combination of factors, including underlying physical health problems and self-harm. The challenges of addressing these issues are compounded by a lack of trained professionals and the impact of climate change. The Bridgetown Declaration, adopted in 2023, calls for strong action to address the social, environmental, economic, and business factors that contribute to non-communicable diseases and mental health issues in these island nations.

translations.source: ‘ለ’ እና ‘በ ’ ትናንሽ ታዳጊ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶችን አካታች ሂደት የዳበረው ይህ መግለጫ፤ ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችን እና የአእምሮ ጤና ሁኔታዎችን የአየር ንብረት ቀውሱን ግምት ውስጥ ካላስገቡ በአግባቡ ሊፈቱ እንደማይችሉ አጉልቶ ያሳያል። "ለአየር ንብረታችን፣ ለጤናችን እና ለደህንነታችን ድፍረት የተሞላበት እርምጃ፤ በማደግ ላይ ያሉ ሀገራት ዕዳቸውን እንዲከፍሉ የሚያደርጉትን ጥረት በማይጎዳ መልኩ፤ ዓለም አቀፍ ፋይናንስን በማስተካከል እና በማደራጀት በቢሊዮኖች የሚቆጠር ኢንቨስትመንት በማግኘት ላይ ይወሰናል።" ብለዋል የባርባዶስ ጠቅላይ ሚኒስትር ክብርት ሚያ አሞር ሞተሊ። "ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአእምሮ ጤናን ግምት ውስጥ በማስገባት፤ በጣም ተጋላጭ በሆኑ አገሮች ውስጥ የአየር ንብረት ለውጥን ለማስተካከል እና ለመቀነስ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ማድረግ ቁልፍ መፍትሄ ነው።" " ትናንሽ ታዳጊ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶች የሚያጋጥሟቸው ፈተናዎች እርስ በርስ የተያያዙ እና ዘርፈ ብዙ ናቸው። የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ፣ የአካባቢ መራቆት፣ ማህበራዊ እና ኢኮኖሚያዊ እኩልነት አለመጣጣም፤ እና የጤና መመዘኛዎች ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአእምሮ ጤና ሁኔታዎችን ሸክም ያባብሳሉ።” ያሉት የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረኢየሱስ ናቸው።
translations.reference: Developed through an inclusive process led ‘for’ and ‘by’ SIDS, the declaration highlights that NCDs and mental health conditions cannot be properly addressed without responding to the climate crisis. “Bold action for our climate, good health, and well-being relies on redressing and reorganizing global financing to unlock billions in investment, while making it less punishing for developing countries to pay their debts,” said Her Excellency Mia Amor Mottley, Prime Minister of Barbados. “Funding for climate change adaptation and mitigation in the most vulnerable countries is also key, with noncommunicable diseases and mental health accounted for.” "The challenges faced by Small Island Developing States are interconnected and multifaceted. Climate change, environmental degradation, social and economic inequalities, and the commercial determinants of health exacerbate the burden of NCDs and mental health conditions," said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General.
translations.hypothesis: This statement highlights how climate change, which is often overlooked, can exacerbate existing challenges faced by small island developing states (SIDS). It emphasizes that addressing climate change, alongside other health and development challenges, requires a coordinated and innovative approach to international finance. "We need bold action that prioritizes our planet, our health, and our prosperity. This means reforming international finance to unlock billions of dollars in investment, while ensuring that developing countries can manage their debt and continue to invest in their own development." said Barbadian Prime Minister Mia Mottley. "By integrating climate change and health into our development strategies, we can create more resilient and sustainable societies." "The challenges faced by SIDS are complex and interconnected. Climate change, environmental degradation, social and economic inequality, and health vulnerabilities all contribute to a cycle of vulnerability. Addressing these challenges requires a holistic approach that takes into account the specific needs and circumstances of each SIDS." said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization.

translations.source: እነዚህ ሀገራት የሀብት እና የመልከአምድር ውስንነት ቢኖርባቸውም በችግር ጊዜ አስደናቂ የሆነ ፅናት አሳይተዋል። በጋራ፣ በፍትሃዊነት፣ በጽናት እና በዘላቂነት ላይ የተመሰረተ መንገድ መፍጠር አለብን። የጤና ስርአቶችን ማጠናከር፣ የመከላከል እና የቅድሚያ ማወቂያ እርምጃዎችን ማሳደግ እና ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአዕምሮ ጤና አገሌግልቶችን ከመጀመሪያ የጤና አጠባበቅ ጋር በማዋሃድ ቅድሚያ መስጠት አለብን። " ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ በተጨማሪም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በነዚህ ደሴቶች ውስጥ ለአየር ንብረት ለውጥ የማይበገር፣ ለአካባቢ ጥበቃ ዘላቂ የሆነ የጤና አጠባበቅ ተቋማትን ለማልማት የፋይናንስ ምንጮችን ለማሰባሰብ እንደሚሰራ ቃል ገብተዋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 'የጥፋት እና ጉዳት' (‘loss and damage’ ) እርዳታን ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ላላቸው ሀገራት ለአየር ንብረት ለውጥ ለመቋቋም ለሚደረጉ ጥረቶች የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ማበረታቱን ይቀጥላል።
translations.reference: "Despite their limited resources and geographical constraints, these nations have shown remarkable resilience in the face of adversity. Together, we must forge a path that is based on equity, resilience, and sustainability. We must strengthen health systems, enhance prevention and early detection measures, and prioritize the integration of NCD and mental health services into primary health care. "The Director-General also pledged that WHO would work to mobilize financial resources to develop climate resilient, environmentally sustainable health-care facilities in the SIDS. WHO will also continue to advocate for ‘loss and damage’ funding for climate change adaptation and mitigation investments in lower income countries.
translations.hypothesis:
Despite facing challenges such as limited resources and vulnerability to climate change, these countries have demonstrated remarkable resilience in times of crisis. We must work together to create a path towards a future built on fairness, sustainability, and resilience. This includes strengthening healthcare systems, investing in preventative measures and early detection, and integrating mental health services with primary healthcare.

The Director-General also stated that the World Health Organization is committed to mobilizing financial resources to establish climate-resilient and environmentally sustainable healthcare institutions in these islands. The WHO will continue to provide financial support to developing countries for their efforts to mitigate and adapt to the impacts of climate change, including loss and damage.

translations.source: በመግለጫው ስምምነት መሰረት፣ እነዚህ ደሴቶች ሁሉንም ህብረተሰብ ለማሳተፍ፣ ትግበራን ለማፋጠን፣ ኢንቨስትመንቶችን ለማረጋገጥ፣ መንግስታትን ለማሰለፍ እና የእድገት ሂደትንና የመሳሰሉ ጉዳዮችን የሚሸፍን ተግባራትን ለማከናወን ተፈራርመዋል። ኮንፈረንሱ በትናንሽ ታዳጊ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶች ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና በአእምሮ ጤና ላይ የተወሰነ ቁርጠኝነት አሳይቷል፣ ይህም በአንደኛ ደረጃ እንክብካቤ መስጫ ተቋማት የአእምሮ ጤና አገልግሎት ተደራሽነትን ማሻሻል፣ የትምባሆ ቁጥጥር ህግን በማሻሻል ሲጋራን እና ቬፒንግን (ኤሌክትሮኒክ ሲጋራ) የሚከለክሉ ህጎችን ማካተት፤ የህዝብን የጨው እና የስኳር መጠን መቀነስን ያካትታል። እነዚህ ቁርጠኝነቶች ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 'ምርጥ ግዢ' (‘best buys’) ጋር ይጣጣማሉ፤ ወጪ ቆጣቢ፣ በማስረጃ ላይ የተመሰረቱ መፍትሄዎች ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችን ለመቋቋም። የምርጥ ግዢዎች ዝርዝር በግንቦት ወር በአለም ጤና ጥበቃ ስብሰባ ላይ የሚከተሉትን እንዲያጠቃልል ማሻሻያ ተደርጎበታል። የሩማቲክ ፊቨር (የልብ፤ የደም ስር እና የመገጣጠሚያ እብጠት የሚያስከትል በሽታ) መከላከልን፤ አጣዳፊ እና የረጅም ጊዜ የአስም በሽታን እና ሥር የሰደደ የሳንባ ምች በሽታዎችን እንዲሁም በርካታ የማህፀን በር፤ የጡት፤ የአንጀት፤ የጉበት እና የልጅነት ካንሰር እንዲሁም ከኤችአይቪ ጋር ለሚኖሩ የካንሰር አጠቃላይ ህክምና ናቸው። “በአሜሪካ ውስጥ ያሉ ትንንሽ ደሴት ታዳጊ ሀገራት ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች በጣም ተጋላጭ ሆነው ቀጥለዋል።
translations.reference: As part of agreeing the Declaration, SIDS signed up to undertake a suite of actions, covering areas such as engaging all of society, accelerating implementation, securing investments, aligning across governments and accounting for progress. The conference also showcased specific commitments by SIDS on NCDs and mental health, including to improve access to mental health care in primary care facilities, update tobacco control legislation to cover e-cigarettes and vaping, and reduce populations’ salt and sugar intake. These commitments align with WHO’s ‘best buys’ – a set of cost-effective, evidence-based interventions to tackle NCDs. The list of best buys was recently updated at the World Health Assembly in May to include secondary prevention for rheumatic fever, acute and long-term management of asthma and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease as well as several interventions for cervical, breast, colorectal, liver and childhood cancer, and the comprehensive treatment of cancer for those living with HIV. “SIDS in the Americas remain overwhelmingly vulnerable to NCDs.
translations.hypothesis: According to the statement, the islands have agreed to implement a range of measures to promote public health, accelerate research, attract investment, strengthen governance, and address key development issues. The conference highlighted a commitment to addressing non-communicable diseases and mental health in small island developing states, which includes improving access to mental health services through primary healthcare, strengthening tobacco control laws, and enacting regulations to curb vaping (electronic cigarettes); as well as reducing the prevalence of obesity and diabetes. These commitments align with the World Health Organization's "best buys," which are cost-effective, evidence-based solutions for preventing and managing non-communicable diseases. The specific "best buys" to be implemented will be detailed at the World Health Assembly in May. These include interventions to prevent rheumatic fever (a heart, blood vessel, and joint inflammation), infectious and long-term respiratory diseases, and neglected tropical diseases, as well as comprehensive cancer care for women, including cervical, breast, colorectal, liver, and pregnancy cancers, as well as HIV-positive individuals. "Small island developing states in the Americas are particularly vulnerable to non-communicable diseases."

translations.source: ይህ የሚኒስትሮች ኮንፈረንስ በራሱ ጊዜ የሚመጣ ሲሆን የነዚህ ደሴቶችን ድምጽ እና ፍላጎቶች ለማጉላት፣ ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችዎችን እና የአእምሮ ጤናን በቀዳሚነት ዝርዝራችን ውስጥ ለማስገባት ቁልፍ ይሆናል።” ብለዋል ዶ/ር ጃርባስ ባርባሳ ፣ የመላው አሜሪካ ጤና ድርጅት ዳይሬክተር እና የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ክልላዊ ዳይሬክተር። "በዚህ በፍጥነት እየተጓዘ ባለው እንቅስቃሴ ላይ የድርሻችንን ለመጨመር፤ አሁን እርምጃ መውሰድ አለብን። የመላው አሜሪካ ጤና ድርጅት በክልላችን የተፋጠነ ትግበራን ለመደገፍ ዝግጁ ነው። እነዚህን የጤና ሁኔታዎችን ወደ አንደኛ ደረጃ የጤና አገልግሎት ማካተት እና እንደ ዲጂታል የጤና መሳሪያዎች ያሉ ፈጠራዎችን መጠቀምን ይጨምራል። ትናንሽና በማደግ ላይ ያሉ ደሴታዊ ግዛቶች በዓለምአቀፍ ውሳኔ አሰጣጥ ላይ ተጸእኖ የማሳደር ታሪክ አላቸው፤ የስፔን ወደብ እና ሳሞአ ስምምነቶች እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2018 ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ተላላፊ ባልሆኑ በሽታዎች ላይ የፖለቲካ መግለጫ እንዲሰጥ ምክኒያት ሆነዋል። ኮንፈረንሱ እና መግለጫው እ.ኤ.አ. በመስከረም 2023 ስለ አለምአቀፍ የጤና ሽፋን እንዲሁም በ2025 ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችዎችን በተመለከተ ለ ከፍተኛ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ድርጅት ጠቅላላ ጉባኤ ሰብሳቢዎች ያሳውቃል።
translations.reference: This Ministerial Conference comes at a unique time and will be key to amplifying the voice and needs of SIDS, bringing NCDs and mental health to the forefront of our priority list,” said Dr Jarbas Barbosa, PAHO Director, and WHO Regional Director for the Americas. “To build on this momentum, we must act now. PAHO is ready to support accelerated implementation of interventions in our region, including mainstreaming care for these conditions into primary health services and taking advantage of innovations such as digital health tools.” SIDS countries have a track record of driving global decision-making; their Port of Spain and SAMOA pathway agreements paved the way for the first-ever UN political declaration on NCDs in 2018. The conference and the Declaration will inform and shape forthcoming UN General Assembly High-Level Meetings on universal health coverage in September 2023 and on NCDs in 2025.
translations.hypothesis: "This ministerial conference will be held at a later date, but it will be crucial in amplifying the voices and needs of these islands, and in prioritizing non-communicable diseases and mental health in our agenda," said Dr. Jarbas Barbosa, Director of the Pan American Health Organization and Regional Director of the World Health Organization. "We must take action now to increase our role in this rapidly evolving landscape. The Pan American Health Organization is ready to support initiatives in our region that address these health challenges. This includes integrating these health issues into national health strategies and leveraging innovative solutions such as digital health tools. Island nations have a long history of influencing international policy, as evidenced by the Spain Port and Samoa agreements, which in 2018 prompted the United Nations to issue a political statement on non-communicable diseases. The conference and statement will be held in September 2023, and will be addressed to the General Assembly of the United Nations, with a focus on global health coverage and non-communicable diseases in 2025."

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ችላ የተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች መርሃ ግብር የአጋሮች ስብሰባ ለዓለም አቀፉ ህብረተሰብ በተቀሰቀሰ ጥሪ ተጠናቀቀ። ልዑካኑ እና ተወካዮቹ የተገኙበት ዓለም አቀፉ ችላ የተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች የአጋሮች ስብሰባ በዳይሬክተሩ ዶ/ር ኢብራሂማ ሶሴ ፎል በተላለፈ ጥሪ ተጠናቋል። 300 የሚሆኑ ቁልፍ አጋሮች የተሳተፉበት የሁለገብ ጉባኤ ማክሰኞ ሰኔ 13 ቀን በጄኔቫ ሲጠናቀቅ፣ ዶ/ር ፎል ተሳታፊዎች ይህንን መልዕክት እንዲወስዱ አሳስበዋል፣ “ራሳችንን ካልፈተንን፤ ራሳችንን መለወጥ እና ማሻሻል አንችልም። ” በማለት ተናግሯል። በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፍኖተ ካርታ ላይ የተዘነጉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎችን ለመግታት እየተደረገ ያለውን ሂደት ለመገምገም እና ስለ በሽታዎቹ የተሻለ ግንዛቤ ለመፍጠር እ.ኤ.አ. 2021-2030 በተካሄደ ሰፊ ዝግጅት ፤ አጋሮች፣ ለጋሾች፣ የቴክኒክ አጋሮች፤ ተቋማት እና የሀገር ተወካዮች በ2030 ከተዘነጉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች የፀዳች ዓለም የሚል ታላቅ ግብ ላይ ለመድረስ ያላቸውን ቁርጠኝነት አጠናክረው ቀጥለዋል። የስብሰባው ዋና ጭብጥ " አንድ በዓለም አቀፍ የተዘነጉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች ፕሮግራምን የሚያደርስ አንድ ማህበረሰብ” በሚለው ራዕይ ላይ ያተኮረ ነው።
translations.reference: WHO’s Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting ends with stirring call for worldwide action Delegates and representatives left the Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting with a stirring call to action from its director Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall. As the multinational meeting, attended by some 300 key partners, came to an end in Geneva on Tuesday 13 June, Dr Fall urged participants to take on board the message that, “if we do not challenge ourselves, we cannot evolve and improve”. In a wide-ranging event held to review progress against the WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 and generate a better understanding of global NTD operations from both disease-specific and cross-cutting perspectives, partners, donors, technical partners, institutions and country representatives reinforced their commitment to achieving an ambitious target: a world free from neglected tropical diseases by 2030. The meeting’s primary theme centred around the vision of “One community delivering one Global NTD Programme”.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization's (WHO) Global Alliance for Neglected Tropical Diseases (GANTD) concluded its annual meeting with a call to action for the international community. The meeting, held at the WHO headquarters in Geneva, brought together representatives from over 300 partner organizations. In his opening remarks, Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus urged participants to "work together to achieve our shared goal of a world free from neglected tropical diseases." The meeting also featured presentations on the latest research and progress in the fight against these diseases, as well as discussions on how to strengthen the GANTD's efforts to achieve its 2030 goal of eliminating neglected tropical diseases.

translations.source: በፍኖተ ካርታው ውስጥ በተጠቀሱት ሶስት ቁልፍ የስልት ለውጦች እነዚህም የሀገር ባለቤትነት ፣ ሂደትን ሳይሆን ተፅእኖን መለካት እንዲሁም ለቀጣይ ተግባራት እና ለበለጠ ውጤታማነት ቁርጠኝነት ላይ በመነሳት ግልጽ እይታ ያላቸው ውይይቶች ችላ የተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎችን ለመቋቋም እንዲሁም የጤና ፍትሃዊነት አስፈላጊነት ለማጉላት ቀርበዋል ። እንደዚህ አይነት አካሄድ ለአንዳንድ የዓለም ዝቅተኛ ገቢ ላላቸው ማህበረሰቦች የተባበሩት መንግስታት እ.ኤ.አ. 2030 ያስቀመጣቸውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግቦችን ለማሳካት ብቸኛ አማራጭ መሆኑ በስብሰባው ተነግሮል ። እ.ኤ.አ. በጥር 2021 ፍኖተ ካርታው ከጀመረ በኋላ በተደረገው የመጀመሪያው ዓለም አቀፍ ስብሰባ ልዑካኑ ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረየሱስ ጋር ተገናኝተዋል። ለሁለት ቀናት በሚቆየው ስብሰባ መጀመሪያ ቀን ላይ ተሳታፊዎችን እንኳን ደህና መጣችሁ ያሉት ዶ/ር ቴድሮስ፣ “ይህ ስብሰባ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ምን እንደሆነ ያሳያል፤ አጋሮች አንድ ላይ ሆነው የጋራ ኃይላቸውን ተጠቅመው ማናችንም ብንሆን ብቻችንን ልንፈታው የማንችለውን ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ተግዳሮቶችን ለመፍታት እንደሚችሉ ያሳያል ብለዋል። "ችላ የተባሉ የሃሩር አካባቢ በሽታዎችን የመሰሉ ዘርፈ ብዙ ፈተናዎች፤ ዘርፈ ብዙ አቀራረብን ይፈልጋሉ፤ አንድነትን የምንፈልገው ለዚህ ነው።."
translations.reference: Taking their cues from the three key strategic shifts advocated in the road map – greater country ownership, measuring impacts not process, and a commitment to cross-cutting activities and collaboration for greater efficiency – discussions were pragmatic, clear-sighted and suffused also with the need for health equity and justice to tackle NTDs. Only such an approach, the meeting heard, can bring about the change that is needed by some of the world’s poorest communities to achieve the United Nations 2030 Sustainable Development Goals. In the first such global meeting since the launch of the road map in January 2021, delegates heard also from WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Welcoming participants at the beginning of the two-day symposium, Dr Tedros said, “This gathering illustrates what WHO is all about – partners coming together to leverage their collective strength to address global health challenges that none of us could address alone. “A multifaceted challenge like neglected tropical diseases takes a multifaceted approach, and that is what we bring, together”.
translations.hypothesis: The three key shifts identified in the Faro Environment Report – focusing on impact rather than ownership, promoting transparent discussions, and emphasizing the importance of health equity – were presented as crucial steps towards combating environmental diseases in the region and achieving the Sustainable Development Goals set by the United Nations in 2030. During the first international meeting following the launch of the Faro Environment Report in January 2021, delegates met with Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization. In his opening remarks, Dr. Tedros stated, "This meeting demonstrates what the World Health Organization stands for: bringing partners together to leverage our collective strength and solve global health challenges that none of us can overcome alone. The challenges we face, such as combating environmental diseases, require a multi-faceted approach, and that is what we are striving for here."

translations.source: ልዑካኑ እና ተሳታፊዎቹ ከፍተኛ ግቦችን ለማሳካት ዋና ዋና ክፍተቶችን እና ተግዳሮቶችን በመለየት ላይ ቀጣዮቹን የስብሰባውን ሁለት ቀናት አሳልፈዋል። አነዚህ መሬት ላይ ያሉ ተግዳሮቶች የተዘነጉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታ ተጠቂ ሰዎች እና መፍትሄዎችን ለማምጣት ቀጥተኛ ኃላፊነት ያለባቸው ሰዎች፤ በተለይም በመገናኛ እና በማህበረሰብ ተሳትፎ እንዲካተቱ እና እውቅና እንዲሰጣቸው ማድረግን ያካትታሉ። ስብሰባው በአለም አቀፍ የተዘነጉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች ላይ የሚሰራ የሰራተኛ ሃይል አቅምን እና እምነትን ለመገንባት የተግባር ማህበረሰቦችን ማጎልበት አስፈላጊ መሆኑንም ተመልክቷል። በቀጣይ ውይይቶች ልዑካኑ በተጠቀሱት ግቦች ላይ እርምጃ ለመውሰድ፤ በመርሆች እና በተግባራዊ እርምጃዎች ላይ ተስማምተው እ.ኤ.አ. ለ2025-2030 የአለምአቀፍ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር በጋራ አቅደዋል። በስብሰባው የተነሱ ዋና ዋና ተግባራት እና ውሳኔዎች፤ የጋራ እቅድ፣ የክትትል እና ግምገማ ቅንጅቶችን ለማረጋገጥ እንዲሁም በአገር አቀፍ ስርዓቶች ውስጥ ይበልጥ ቀልጣፋ የአቅርቦት ሰንሰለት አስተዳደርን ለማረጋገጥ የብሔራዊ ማስተባበሪያ መድረኮችን ለማጠናከር እና ለማስኬድ ምክሮችን ያካተተ ነው።
translations.reference: Delegates and participants spent the subsequent two days identifying the major gaps and challenges to achieving high-level goals and local, on-the-ground challenges. Those on-the-ground challenges include ensuring that people directly affected by NTDs, and those directly responsible for administering interventions against NTDs, are solicited and acknowledged, particularly in terms of communication and community engagement. The meeting noted also the importance of developing communities of practice, in order to build the capacity and confidence of the global NTD workforce. In further discussions, delegates agreed on principles and practical steps to implement action towards the stated targets and planned together for a Global Action Plan for 2025–2030. Key actions and decisions arising from the meeting included recommendations for strengthening and operationalizing national coordination platforms to ensure coordination for joint planning and for monitoring and evaluation, and to ensure more efficient supply chain management within national systems.
translations.hypothesis: The delegation and participants have spent the two days of the meeting identifying key gaps and challenges in achieving the stated goals. These include addressing the specific challenges faced by individuals affected by neglected tropical diseases in the affected areas, as well as those directly responsible for implementing solutions, particularly in the areas of communication and community engagement. The meeting also emphasized the importance of building the capacity and confidence of the global workforce working on neglected tropical diseases. In subsequent discussions, the delegation committed to taking action on the identified goals, and to working together to develop a joint plan for the 2025-2030 Global Action Plan. The key actions and decisions that emerged from the meeting included recommendations to strengthen and implement national coordination platforms to ensure effective supply chain management and to support the development of a joint monitoring and evaluation framework.

translations.source: የባልደረባዎች ስብሰባ የተካሄደው በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ችላ የተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች ቁጥጥር ዲፓርትመንት ውስጥ በተደረገው ዋና ግምገማ ሂደት መጨረሻ ላይ ነው። ባለፉት አራት ወራት ውስጥ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ራሱ የሚያራምደው እና የሚጠይቀውን የተጠያቂነት፣ የግልጽነት እና የፍትሃዊነት እሴቶችን ያቀፈ መሆኑን ለማረጋገጥ ከሁሉም አጋሮቹ እና ባለድርሻ አካላት የሚሰጡትን አስተዋፅኦ እና የምስክርነት ቃል ሲሰማ ቆይቷል። እነዚያ እሴቶች ችላ የተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች በአለም አቀፍ የጤና ስርዓት ውስጥ መቀመጡን ማረጋገጥንም ያካትታሉ። የስብሰባ ተሳታፊዎች የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች ላይ ማህበረሰቡ ሊጫወታቸው በሚችላቸው ቁልፍ ሚናዎች ፤ እንደ ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን፣ አንድ ጤና (One Health)እና እየጨመረ የመጣው የአለም የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ ስጋት ሃላፊነትን ተቀብለዋል። በአካል የተገኙት ልዑካን በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና መሥሪያ ቤት ለተዘነጉ የትሮፒካል በሽታዎች የተዘጋጀ ኤግዚቢሽን መጎብኘት ችለዋል።
translations.reference: The Partners’ Meeting took place also at the end of a major functional review process within WHO’s Department of Control of Neglected Tropical Diseases. Over the course of the past four months, WHO has been hearing contributions and testimony from all its partners and stakeholders, in order to ensure that it itself embodies the values of accountability, transparency and equity that it espouses and demands. Those values also include ensuring that NTDs are repositioned in the global health architecture. Meeting participants welcomed the emphasis on the key roles that the NTD community can and must play in contributing to wider challenges such as Universal Health Coverage, One Health and the increasing threat of global climate change. Delegates attending in person were also able to enjoy an exhibition dedicated to neglected tropical diseases at WHO headquarters.
translations.hypothesis: The meeting of partners took place at the end of a comprehensive assessment process within the World Health Organization's (WHO) office in Chula, a region in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, focused on controlling diseases associated with the environment. Over the past four months, the WHO has been gathering evidence and testimonies from all its partners and stakeholders to demonstrate that the organization upholds the values of accountability, transparency, and equity, as it promotes and advocates for them. This includes ensuring that the environmental diseases in Chula are recognized and addressed within the global health system. Participants in the meeting acknowledged the crucial roles that communities can play in addressing environmental diseases, including universal health coverage, One Health, and addressing the growing threat of climate change. Attendees also visited an exhibition at the WHO headquarters dedicated to neglected tropical diseases.

translations.source: የብረት ግርግዳዎች ስብስብ የተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) ጃንጥላ ስር የተመደቡትን 20 ሁኔታዎች በምሳሌነት ያሳያሉ እናም፣ እነዚህ በፎቶግራፎች እናም እነሱን ለመቅረፍ ለብዙ አስርት ዓመታት ውስጥ የተከናወኑ ድርጊቶችን በሚያሳዩ መረጃዎች የታጀቡም ናቸው።
translations.reference: A set of metal panels was commissioned to illustrate the 20 conditions that make up the NTD grouping, and these were accompanied by photographs and information illustrating the actions that have been put in place over the course of many decades to tackle them.
translations.hypothesis: The collection of iron-based diseases illustrates 20 non-tropical diseases (NTDs) that are prevalent in the lymphatic system, and includes photographs and information about the actions taken over decades to combat them.

translations.source: በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ችላ የተባሉ የሀሩር ክልል በሽታዎች መርሀግብር አጋሮች ስብሰባ፣ እ.ኤ.አ ከ12-13 ሰኔ 2023 የአለምአቀፍ የተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) ማህበረሰብ ተወካዮች እ.ኤ.አ በ12-13 ሰኔ 2023 በዋናው መሥሪያ ቤት አዳራሽ በሚካሄደው በአለም አቀፍ ችላ በተባሉት የሀሩር ክልል በሽታዎች መርሀግብር አጋሮች ስብሰባ ላይ ለመሳተፍ በሚቀጥለው ሳምንት በጄኔቫ ይሰበሰባሉ። የስብሰባው ዓላማዎች የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፍኖተ ካርታ ላይ የተዘነጉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎችን እ.ኤ.አ ከ2021-2030 መገምገም እና ስለ ዓለም አቀፍ የተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) ስራዎች ከበሽታ-ተኮር እና አቋራጭ አመለካከቶች የተሻለ ግንዛቤ መፍጠር እና በሰፊው መሳተፍ ናቸው። አንድ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በአንድ ዓለም አቀፍ የተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) መርሀግብር ላይ ለማቅረብ ባለው ራዕይ ዙሪያ ውይይቶች። ከዚህ ቀደም የተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) አጋሮች አለምአቀፍ ስብሰባዎች በአለም ጤና ድርጅት እ.ኤ.አ በ2007 እና 2017 የተጠሩት ቢሆንም፣ ይህ ሶስተኛው መጪ ክስተት እ.ኤ.አ በጥር 2021 የመንገድ ካርታ ከጀመረ በኋላ የመጀመሪያው ነው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ችላ ለተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ፍኖተ ካርታ እ.ኤ.አ የ2021-2030 ኢላማዎችን፣ ከዘላቂ ልማት ግቦች እና ሌሎች ዓለም አቀፍ አጀንዳዎች ጋር የሚጣጣሙበትን ዘዴዎች የሚዘረዝር እና ችላ የተባሉትን የሐሩር ክልል የበሽታዎች ወረርሽኝ እ.ኤ.አ በ2030 ለማስቆም በ የተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) አቀራረቦች ላይ ስልታዊ ለውጦችን የሚጠይቅ አጠቃላይ ማዕቀፍ ነው።
translations.reference: Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting, 12–13 June 2023 Representatives of the global NTD community will gather in Geneva next week to participate in the Global Neglected Tropical Diseases Programme Partners’ Meeting, which takes place in headquarters’ auditorium on 12-13 June 2023. The objectives of the meeting are to review progress on the WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 and generate a better understanding of global NTD operations from both disease-specific and cross-cutting perspectives, as well as to engage in wide-ranging discussions around the vision for One WHO delivering on one Global NTD Programme. While previous global meetings of NTD partners were convened by WHO in 2007 and 2017, this third upcoming event is the first since the launch of the road map in January 2021. The WHO road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030 is a comprehensive framework that outlines targets, mechanisms for alignment with the Sustainable Development Goals and other global agendas and calls for strategic shifts in NTD approaches with the aim of ending the epidemic of neglected tropical diseases by 2030.
translations.hypothesis: The International Meeting of Partners for the Global Programme to Eliminate Lymphatic Filariasis (LF), scheduled for June 12-13, 2023, will bring together representatives from the global neglected tropical diseases (NTD) community. The meeting will be held at the main office building in Geneva, and participants are expected to arrive in the coming week. The objectives of the meeting include: reviewing the progress of lymphatic filariasis elimination under the World Health Organization's Global Programme to Eliminate Lymphatic Filariasis (GPELF) from 2021-2030; and fostering better understanding and engagement in global NTD efforts from a disease-specific and programmatic perspective. Discussions will also focus on the World Health Organization's vision for a global NTD programme, and on the lessons learned from previous NTD meetings, which were held in 2007 and 2017. The current meeting is the first to be held since the launch of the Global NTD Programme in January 2021. The meeting will also review the GPELF's targets for lymphatic filariasis elimination from 2021-2030, and will explore strategies for achieving these targets in line with the Sustainable Development Goals and other global agendas. The meeting will also provide a platform for strategic changes in NTD approaches to eliminate lymphatic filariasis by 2030.

translations.source: ስብሰባው የሚያተኩረው፡ እ.ኤ.አ የ 2030 ግቦችን ለማሳካት አሁን ያለውን ሂደት መገምገም; እነዚያ ግቦች ላይ ለመድረስ የሚያጋጥሙ ዋና ዋና ክፍተቶችን እና ተግዳሮቶችን መለየት; ወደ ዒላማዎቹ የሚያደርሱ እርምጃዎችን እንዴት መተግበር እንደሚቻል የሚያረጉ መርሆዎች እና ተግባራዊ እርምጃዎች ላይ መስማማት; በፍኖተ ካርታው ችላ የተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎችን በሚመለከት የተዘረዘሩትን ለውጦች ተግባራዊ ለማድረግ እና ግቦቹን ለማሳካት የአጋሮች ድጋፍ እና ቁርጠኝነት እና
translations.reference: The meeting will focus on: reviewing current progress to achieving the 2030 targets; identifying the major gaps and challenges to reaching those targets; agreeing principles and practical steps for how to implement action towards the targets; galvanizing support and commitment from NTD partners to operationalize the shifts advocated in the road map and to achieve its targets; and
translations.hypothesis: The meeting will focus on evaluating the current progress towards achieving the 2030 goals; identifying the key gaps and challenges that need to be addressed to reach those goals; and agreeing on principles and practical steps for implementing actions that will lead to their achievement; as well as securing the support and commitment of partners to implement the changes identified in the "Haruru Environment" report regarding environmental health in the Haruru area and achieve the goals.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ ለ2025–2030 የአለምአቀፍ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብርን በጋራ ማቀድ። በስብሰባው ላይ ከ300 በላይ ተሳታፊዎች በአካልም ሆነ በመስመር ላይ ይገኛሉ ተብሎ ይጠበቃል። ከአለም አቀፍ የተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) ማህበረሰብ ተወካዮች፣ አገሮች እና ከለጋሾች እስከ ተግባራዊ እና የሙያዊ አጋሮች፣ ከአካዳሚያ(academia) እስከ የታካሚዎች ማህበራት (patients association) እና ከሌሎች ተላላፊ ካልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) ጋር በሚደረገው ትግል ውስጥ የተሳተፉ ሌሎች አካላት እና ተቋማትን ያካትታሉ። ችላ በተባሉ የሀሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ላይ ኤግዚቢሽን በ'ቢ' ህንጻ ውስጥ አንደኛ ፎቅ ከካፊቴሪያው በላይ ይከናወናል። የብረት ግርግዳዎች ስብስብ የተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች(NTD) ጃንጥላ ስር የተመደቡትን 20 ሁኔታዎች በምሳሌነት ያሳያሉ እናም ሸክማቸውን ለመቅረፍ ስለተተገበሩት እርምጃዎች መረጃ ይሰጣሉ።
translations.reference: planning together for a Global Action Plan 2025–2030. Over 300 participants in the meeting are expected to attend, either in-person or online. They will include representatives of the global NTD community, from countries and donors to implementing and technical partners, from academia to patients’ associations and all other entities and institutions involved in the fight against NTDs. An exhibition on neglected tropical diseases will take place in the mezzanine of the B building, just above the cafeteria. A set of metal panels will illustrate the 20 conditions grouped under the NTD umbrella and provide information on actions implemented to tackle their burden.
translations.hypothesis: The 2025-2030 Global Action Plan will be jointly developed at the conference. It is expected that over 300 participants will attend, both in person and online. The conference will bring together representatives from the global non-communicable disease (NCD) community, as well as countries and partners, from implementation and professional organizations, from academia to patient associations, and other stakeholders involved in the fight against NCDs. An exhibition on the major NCDs will be held on the first floor of Building B, above the cafeteria. A display of iron deficiency anemia will showcase 20 examples of NCDs related to iron deficiency, and will provide information on the interventions that have been implemented to address them.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አሸናፊዎችን አስታውቋል 4ኛው 'ጤና ለሁሉም' የተሰኘው የፊልም ፌስቲቫል 7 አጫጭር አሸናፊ ፊልሞች እና 4 ልዮ እውቅና ከዳኞች የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) ጄኔቫ በሚገኘው ዋና መሥሪያ ቤቱ በተካሄደው በ 4 ኛው ዙር 'ዓመታዊ ጤና ለሁሉም' በተሰኘው የፊልም ፌስቲቫል ላይ የዚህ ዓመት አሸናፊ ፊልሞችን ይፋዊ ምርጫን አስታውቋል ። በአካል እና በቀጥታ በበይነ መረብ ላይ (online) ተዋናዮች፣ አዘጋጆች እና ታዋቂ ሰዎች የተሳተፉበት ይህ ዝግጅት በ7 የተለያዩ ምድቦች አሸናፊ የሆኑ ፊልሞች ሲታወጁ፣ 4 ፊልሞች ከዳኞች ልዩ ሙገሳንም አግኝተዋል።
translations.reference: WHO announces winners of the 4th Health for All Film Festival 7 winning short films, 4 special mentions from the jury The World Health Organization has announced the official selection of this year’s winning films at its 4th Annual Health for All Film Festival, held at WHO Headquarters, Geneva. The event, attended in person and online by actors, producers and public figures, saw winning films announced for 7 different categories, while 4 films received special mentions from the jury.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) has announced the winners of the 4th "Health for All" film festival, which showcased seven short films and four special jury awards. The festival, held in Geneva, featured actors, filmmakers, and celebrities both in person and online. The event recognized seven films across various categories, with four films receiving special jury accolades.

translations.source: የፊልም ፌስቲቫሉ አራተኛው አመቱ ሲሆን፣ ውድድሩ ከባድ ፉክክር የበዛበት ሲሆን፣ 93 ያህል የተመረጡ ፊልሞች ከጭንቀት እና ድብርት እስከ የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ በጤና ላይ እያስከተለ ያለውን ችግር እንዲሁም የአካል ጉዳተኞችን የጤና ተግዳሮቶች ለማሳየት የሞከሩበት ነበር ። ፊልሞቹ በታዋቂ ባለሙያዎች፣ አርቲስቶች እና የህብረተብ ተሟጋቾች የተዳኘ ሲሆን፣ ታዋቂ ተዋናዮች የሆኑትን 'ሻሮን ስቶን'ን እና 'አልፎንሶ ሄሬራ'ን፣ የውዝዋዜ አቀናባሪ የሆነችውን 'ሼሪ ሲልቨር'ን፣ የአየር ንብረት ተሟጋች የሆነችውን 'ሶፊያ ኪያኒ'ን እና የመገናኛ ብዙሀን ባለሙያውን 'አዴሌ ኦንያንጎ'ን ያካተተ ነው። የተባበሩት መንግስታት ከፍተኛ ባለስልጣናት እና በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ሰራተኞችም ተቀላቅለውበታል። "ጤና ለሁሉም የተሰኘው የፊልም ፌስቲቫል የሰውን ፊት ወደ አለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) ሳይንሳዊ ስራ ይመልሳል" ብለው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር የሆኑት ዶክተር ቴድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ተናግረዋል ። "በጤና ጉዳዮች የተጎዱ ሰዎችን ድምጽ ማዳመጥ ግንዛቤን ለማሳደግ እና የሰዎችን ልምዳዊ አስተሳሰብን ለማሻሻል ኃይለኛ መንገድ ነው እናም ይህ ለሁሉም ሰው የጤና ጥበቃን ለማዳረስ ይረዳናል።"
translations.reference: This is the fourth year of the film festival and competition was no less fierce, with some 93 shortlisted films covering issues ranging from anxiety and depression through to the effects of climate change on health, as well as the health challenges of people with disabilities. The films were judged by a panel of distinguished professionals, artists and activists, including the renown actors, Sharon Stone and Alfonso Herrera, dance choreographer, Sherrie Silver, climate activist, Sophia Kianni and media personality, Adelle Onyango. They were joined by senior United Nations officials and WHO staff. “The Health for All Film Festival brings a human face to WHO’s scientific work,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “Listening to the voices of people affected by health issues is a powerful way to raise awareness and improve our understanding of people’s experiences and this can help us advance towards health for all.”
translations.hypothesis: The fourth annual film festival featured a fierce competition, with over 93 carefully selected films exploring themes ranging from mental health and climate change to the challenges faced by people with disabilities. The films were screened for prominent experts, artists, and advocates, including well-known actors Sharon Stone and Alfonso Herrera, journalist Sherry Silver, climate activist Sophia Kane, and media professional Adele Onyango. Representatives from the United Nations and the World Health Organization also attended. "This film festival, dedicated to 'Health for All,' brings the world's scientific work on health to the forefront," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization. "Giving a voice to those affected by health issues is a powerful way to raise awareness and improve people's understanding, and this will ultimately help to ensure that everyone has access to quality healthcare."

translations.source: ከይፋዊው ምርጫ አንድ አንድ "የመወዳደሪያ ርእስ"(“GRAND PRIX” ) ለእያንዳንዱ ሶስት ዋና የውድድር ምድቦች ተሰጥቷል። እኚህም 'ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን'፣ 'የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች'፣ እና 'የተሻለ ጤና እና ደህንነት' ሲሆኑ፣ ከ ዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከቢሊዮን ሶስት እጥፍ ከሚሆኑ (WHO's Triple Billion) ዒላማዎች ጋር የሚጣጣሙ ናቸው። የጎልደን ግሎብ እና የኤሚ ሽልማት አሸናፊዋ የአሜሪካዊቷ ተዋናይ፣ የጤና እና የሰብአዊ ጉዳዮች አዘጋጅ እና ተሟጋች የሆነችው 'ሳሮን ስቶን' እንደተናገረችውም፣ “ጤና ለሁሉም የተሰኘው የ ፊልም ፌስቲቫል አካል በመሆኔ ደስተኛ ነኝ። ይህም፣ በአለም ዙሪያ ጤናማ የኑሮ ሁኔታዎችን ለማድረስ በሚያስፈልጉ ወሳኝ እርምጃዎች ላይ የተሻለ ግንዛቤን ስለ መፍጠር ነው። የተመረጡት ታሪኮች ስለ ጥሩ የጤና ሁኔታ እና ስለ ተደራሽነቱም ውስጣዊ ጠቀሜታ ይነግሩናል እናም የአለም አቀፍ የጤና ሽፋንንም ይደግፋሉ።
translations.reference: From the official selection one “GRAND PRIX” is attributed for each of the three main competition categories: Universal Health Coverage, Health emergencies, and Better health and well-being, which align with WHO’s Triple Billion Targets. Sharon Stone, Golden Globe and Emmy Award-winning actress from the United States of America, producer and activist for health and humanitarian causes said: "I am delighted to be part of the Health for All Film Festival. This is about creating better awareness on crucial actions needed for reaching healthier living conditions around the world. The stories selected talk to us about the intrinsic value of good health and its access, and they advocate for universal health coverage.
translations.hypothesis: A "Grand Prix" award was given for each of the three main competition categories. These categories were "Universal Health Coverage," "Health Emergencies," and "Better Health and Safety," and they align with the World Health Organization's (WHO) goal of achieving three billion (Triple Billion) targets. As Sarah Stone, the winner of the Golden Globe and Emmy Award, stated, "I am delighted to be part of a film festival that promotes the idea of 'health for all.' This festival will help to raise awareness of the important steps needed to achieve healthy living conditions around the world. The selected films highlight the importance of good health and accessibility, and they also support global health coverage."

translations.source: ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን በጣም አስፈላጊ መብት ነው፣ በዓለም ዙሪያ ላሉ ሰዎች ሁሉ ሰብአዊ መብት ነው።" በአየር ንብረት ለውጥ እና ጤና ላይ የተሰራ ፊልም፣ የወሲብ እና የስነ ተዋልዶ ጤና እና መብቶች ላይ የተሰራ ፊልም እና አንድ በጣም አጭር ፊልም፤ በተማሪ የተሰሩ ፊልም ምድብ ውስጥ፣ አራት ልዩ ሽልማቶች ተሰጥተዋል። ከስዊድን ስለ ጭንቀት እና ድብርት ላይ የተሰራን ኃይለኛ እና ልብ ቀስቃሽ አጭር ፊልምን ጨምሮ ፤ የአእምሮ ጤና ጭብጥ በዚህ አመት አሸናፊ በሆኑ የፊልም መልካዊ ይዘቶች ውስጥ ጎልቶ ታይቷል ። ፊልሙ፣ ቀስቃሽ የሆነ ማጀቢያ ሙዚቃን በመጠቀም የተቀናበረው ፊልም ተዋናዮችን የተለያዩ የጭንቀት ደረጃዎችን ሲያሳዩ እና ጭንቀቱም እንዴት እራሱን ማዳበርና ስር መስደድ እንደሚችል አሳይቷል። አጠቃላይ መልዕክቱም፣ እርስዎ ብቻዎን አይደሉም አሉታዊ ሀሳቦችን እና እንደዚህ አይነት ስሜቶችን ማስተናገድ ምንም እንደልሆነ ሲሆን፤ ከባንግላዲሽም ሌላ አሸናፊ ፊልም ፣ በአካባቢው ከሚገኝ ፋብሪካ ሊድ (lead) በተሰኘው ንጥረ ነገር ስለ ተጋለጠ ወጣት ልጅ ያሳያል።
translations.reference: Universal Health Coverage is a very important right, it’s a human right for everyone around the world. "Four special prizes were also given for a Student-produced film, a film on Climate Change and Health, a film on Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights and a Very Short Film. The theme of mental health featured heavily in this year’s winning entries, including a powerful and moving short film from Sweden about anxiety and depression. The film, set to a stirring soundtrack, featured a series of actors expressing different stages of anxiety and how it can manifest itself. The overall message was that you are not alone in experiencing negative thoughts and that it is okay to acknowledge such feelings.Another winning film, from Bangladesh, features a young boy who was exposed to lead from a local factory.
translations.hypothesis: "Universal health coverage is a fundamental human right, and it is essential for all people around the world." Four films were awarded in the student film category, including a powerful and moving short film about anxiety and depression from Sweden. The film, which featured a compelling soundtrack, showed the actors portraying different levels of anxiety and how it can affect a person's life. The overall message of the film was that it is important to seek help for negative thoughts and feelings, and that you are not alone. Another notable film from the competition was a short film from Bangladesh that highlighted the issue of lead contamination in the local environment.

translations.source: ልብ ቀሳቃሹ ፊልም፣ ወጣቱ ልጅ በዚህ ችግር መጋለጡ በትምህርት እድገቱ ላይ ያስከተለውን ተጽእኖ እና ጉዳዩንም ለማጉላት እና በተጎዳው ማህበረሰብ ላይ የሊድ(lead) መመረዝን ለመከላከል አንድ የሀገር ውስጥ መንግሥታዊ ያልሆነ ድርጅት ያከናወናቸውን ተግባራት በዝርዝር አስቀምጧል። የተሸለሙት ፊልሞች ዝርዝር፡- ለዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ሽፋን ከፍተኛ ሽልማት፦ “የጆናታን ተአምረኛ እግሮች” (“Jonathan's Miracle Feet”) በሚል ርዕስ ከሴራሊዮን በአካል ጉዳተኝነት እና በእግር አፈጣጠር ችግር ላይ ያተኮረ፤ በ'ማሚሃሲና ራሚኖሶአ' እና 'ናንቴኒያና ራኮቶንድራኒቮ' መሪነት የተዘጋጀው ከማዳጋስካር ተአምረኛ እግሮች ለተሰኘው መንግስታዊ ያልሆነ ድርጅት የተዘጋጀ / ዘጋቢ ፊልም - ቆይታ 3 ደቂቃ ከ19'ኳ ሴኮንድ የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች' በተሰኘው፣ "የመወዳደሪያ ርእስ"(“GRAND PRIX” ) : "ኮቪድን ፊት ለፊት የተጋፈጡት የህክምና እንክብካቤ ሰጪ ባለሙያዎች/Nurses facing Covid / Na Lihna De Frente " - ብራዚል / ኮቪድ-19 እና የእንክብካቤ ተደራሽነት
translations.reference: The moving film details the effects this has had on his educational development and the work that a local non-governmental organization has done to both highlight the issue and take action to prevent lead poisoning in the affected community. List of films awarded: UHC "Grand Prix": “Jonathan’s Miracle Feet” – Sierra Leone / Disability, clubfoot Directed by Mamihasina Raminosoa and Nantenaina Rakotondranivo from Madagascar for the NGO Miracle Feet / Documentary – Duration 3’19’’ Health Emergencies "Grand Prix": “Nurses facing Covid / Na Lihna de Frente” – Brazil / COVID-19 and access to care
translations.hypothesis:
The documentary film, "Heart of the Matter," highlights the impact of this crisis on the young people affected, particularly in their educational development, and details the efforts of a local, non-governmental organization to address the issue and prevent lead poisoning in the affected community. The list of featured films includes:

*   "Jonathan's Miracle Feet," a documentary film from Sierra Leone that focuses on disability and foot reconstruction, and was nominated for the International Health Coverage Award. The film was produced by "Mamaheshi na Raminoso" and "Nanteni na Rakotondranaivo," and features a 3-minute excerpt from "Health Emergencies" ("GRAND PRIX").

*   "Nurses facing Covid," a documentary film from Brazil that focuses on the challenges faced by healthcare workers during the Covid-19 pandemic.

translations.source: በክሊምት ፐብሊሲዳዴ የተመራ እና በፌደራል የነርሶች ምክር ቤት ተቋም በብራዚል የተዘጋጀ የ 8 ደቂቃ ቆይታ ያለው ዘጋቢ ፊልም ነው። ለተሻለ ጤና እና ደህንነት ከፍተኛ ሽልማት፦ "ከ 36 ሚሊዮን አንዱ፣ በባንግላዲሽ በልጆች ላይ የሊድ (lead) መመረዝ ታሪክ" በአካባቢ ጤና ላይ ያተኮር ፊልም፤ በ'አሪፉር ራህማን' እና 'ሚታሊ ዳስ' (ባንግላዴሽ) ዳይሬክት ተደረጎ፣ 'ንጹህ ምድር' ለተሰኘው መንግስታዊ ያልሆነ ድርጅት የተዘጋጀ / ባንግላዲሽ /ዘጋቢ ፊልም- ቆይታ 6 ደቂቃ ከ32 ሰከንድ ልዩ ሽልማት የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ እና የጤና ፊልም፡ "የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ ወደ ሁከት ሲቀየር ( When climate change turns violet)" - ዓለም አቀፍ / ጾታን መሰረት ያደረገ ጥቃት እና የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ በ'ቫንዲታ ሳሪያ' (ህንድ) ዳይሬክት ተደርጓል / ዘጋቢ ፊልም - ቆይታ 4 ደቂቃ 32 ሴኮንድ
translations.reference: Directed by Klimt Publicidade and the institution Conselho Federal de Enfermagem – Cofen – From Brazil / Documentary – Duration 8’ Better Health and Well-being "Grand Prix": “One in 36 Million: Story of Childhood Lead Poisoning in Bangladesh” – Environmental health Directed by Arifur Rahman and Mitali Das (Bangladesh) for the NGO Pure Earth Bangladesh Documentary – Duration 6’32’’ Special Prize Climate Change and Health Film: "When climate change turns violent” – Global / Gender based violence and climate change Directed by Vandita Sariya (India) / Documentary - Duration 4’32’’
translations.hypothesis:
This 8-minute documentary film, produced by Climate Publicis and the Federal Nursing Council in Brazil, highlights the importance of better health and safety. The film, titled "One of 36 Million: The Lead Poisoning Crisis in Bangladesh," focuses on environmental health and was directed by Arif Rahman and Mitali Das (Bangladesh). It was produced for "Clean Earth," a non-governmental organization (NGO) based in Bangladesh. The film's runtime is 6 minutes and 32 seconds.

The film also features a special award for "Climate Change and Health Film": "When Climate Change Turns Violent" - an international, gender-focused film directed by Vandita Saria (India). The film's runtime is 4 minutes and 32 seconds.

translations.source: ልዩ ሽልማት ወሲባዊ እና የስነ ተዋልዶ ጤና እና መብቶች ፊልም: "ቩልቮ እና ዲንያ (Vulvo and Dynia)" - እስራኤል / ቩልቮዲኒያ በ'ዲና ስቴስኮቪች' (እስራኤል) ዳይሬክት ተደርጓል / ልብ ወለድ - ቆይታ 4 ደቂቃ 13 ሴኮንድ የተማሪ ፊልም ሽልማት፡-“ ለመኖር መመኘት” ከጀርመን እንደ የስልክ ሱስ፣ ጭንቀት እና ድብርት ያሉ የአእምሮ ጤና ርዕሶችን ይሸፍናል። በ'ሱ ህዩን ሆንግ' (ጀርመን) ዳይሬክት ተደርጓል / አኒሜሽን - ቆይታ 8 ደቂቃ ልዩ ሽልማት በጣም አጭር ፊልም: "መስተዋት/Mirror" - ስዊድን / የአእምሮ ጤና፣ የመንፈስ ጭንቀት
translations.reference: Special Prize Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights Film:“Vulvo and Dynia” – Israel / Vulvodynia Directed by Dina Stescovich (Israel) / Fiction – Duration 4’13’’ Student Film Prize:“Gasping for life” – Germany / Mental health, screens addiction, anxiety, depression Directed by Su Hyun Hong (Germany) / Animation – Duration 8’ Special Prize Very Short Film:“Mirrors” – Sweden / Mental health, depression
translations.hypothesis: Special Award: Film on Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights: "Vulvo and Dynia" - Israel / Vulvodynia, directed by Dina Stekovich (Israel) / Runtime: 4 minutes 13 seconds. Student Film Award: "The Desire to Live" from Germany addresses mental health issues such as addiction, anxiety, and depression. Directed by "Su Hyun Hong" (Germany) / Animation - Runtime: 8 minutes. Special Award for Short Film: "Mirror" - Sweden / Mental health, spiritual distress.

translations.source: በ'ፖል ጄርዳል' (ስዊድን) ዳይሬክት ተደርጓል / ልብ ወለድ - ቆይታ 3 ደቂቃ ከዳኞች ልዩ ሙገሳ የተቀበሉ ፊልሞች፡የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች በልዩነት በመጥቀስ፡ “ሥሮቼ፡ ማያዊያን በኮቪድ-19 ጊዜ/ My roots:Mayas during Covid-19” - ጓቲማላ/ኮቪድ-19 በ'አንጄላ ሉክሬሺያ ቺኩዊን' (ጓቴማላ) ዳይሬክት ተደርጓል / ዘጋቢ ፊልም - ቆይታ 3 ደቂቃ 11 ሴኮንድ ለተሻለ ጤና እና ደህንነት ልዩ እውቅና ፦ "የፍቅር ጥላዎች” (“Love Shades") ከስዊድን ስለአእምሮ ጤና፣ ድብርት እና ርህራሄ ላይ ያተኮረ፤ በ'ፕራቲክ ፓውዴል' (ስዊድን) ዳይሬክት ተደርጓል / ልብ ወለድ - ቆይታ 5 ደቂቃ 51 ሴኮንድ
translations.reference: Directed by Paul Jerndal (Sweden) / Fiction – Duration 3’ Films receiving a Special Mention from the Jury:Health Emergencies Special Mention: “My roots: Mayas during Covid-19” – Guatemala / COVID-19 Directed by Ángela Lucrecia Chiquin (Guatemala) / Documentary – Duration 3’11’’ Better Health and Well-being Special Mention: “Love Shades” – Sweden / Mental health, depression and empathy Directed by Pratick Paudel (Sweden) / Fiction – Duration 5’51’’
translations.hypothesis: "Paul Järdal" directed the film / Heartfelt - 3-minute excerpt featuring films selected by the judges, with a focus on health emergencies: "My Roots: Mayas during Covid-19" - directed by "Angela Lucresia Chiquín" (Guatemala) / News film - 3 minutes 11 seconds, highlighting the importance of better health and safety: "Love Shades" - a film from Sweden focusing on mental health, depression, and anxiety, directed by "Pratik Poude" / Heartfelt - 5 minutes 51 seconds.

translations.source: የአየር ንብረት ለውጥን እና ጤናን በልዩ መልኩ በመግለጽ፡ "የመተንፈስ ነፃነት፡ የሕፃን ንጹህ አየር የመተንፈስ መብት/Freedom to breath: a child's right to breath clean air" - ዓለም አቀፍ / አስም እና የአየር ብክለት በ'ጆርጅት ቶማስ' (ዩናይትድ ኪንግደም) ዳይሬክት ተደርጓል / ዘጋቢ ፊልም - ቆይታ 5 ደቂቃ 39 ሴኮንድ በጣም አጭር ፊልም በልዩ መልኩ በመጥቀስ: "እኔ ራቁቴን ነኝ / Je suis nue/I am Naked" - ፈረንሳይ / የግላዊነት መብት ጥሰት; የአዕምሮ ጤንነት; በሴቶች ላይ ስሜታዊ ጥቃት በ'አሌክሳንድራ ሚግኒየን' (ፈረንሳይ) ዳይሬክት ተደርጓል / ልብ ወለድ - ቆይታ 2 ደቂቃ 20 ሴኮንድ ስለ ይፋዊ ምርጫው ፣ ስለ ዳኞች ጥንቅር ለበለጠ መረጃ ለማግኘት እና ፊልሞቹን እና የሽልማት ሥነ ሥርዓቱን ለመመልከት https://www.who.int/film-festival ይጎብኙ
translations.reference: Climate Change and Health Special Mention: “Freedom to breathe: a child’s right to breath clean air” – Global / Asthma and air pollution Directed by Georgette Thomas (United Kingdom) / Documentary – Duration 5’39’’ Very Short Film Special Mention: “I am naked / Je suis nue” – France / Violation of privacy; Mental health; Emotional violence against women Directed by Alexandra Mignien (France) / Fiction – Duration 2’20’’ For more details on the official selection, the jury composition and to watch the films and awards ceremony, visit https://www.who.int/film-festival

A short film that specifically mentions: "I am naked / Je suis nue/I am Naked" - France / Violation of the right to privacy; Mental health; Directed by Alexandra Migien (France) / Heart - Runtime: 2 minutes 20 seconds. translations.source: አዲስ ዘገባ እንደሚያሳየው፣ መሠረታዊ የሃይል አቅርቦት በታዳሽ ሀይል እድሎች አንፃር ወደ ኋላ ቀርነት አሳይቷል የአለም የሀይል አቅም ተደራሽነት ክፍተት እንደቀጠለ ነው፡- 675 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች የኤሌክትሪክ አገልግሎት አይደርሳቸውም፣ 2.3 ቢሊዮን ሰዎችም ጎጂ በሆነ የምግብ ማብሰያ ነዳጆች ላይ ጥገኛ ናቸው። በአለም አቀፍ የሀይል አቅም ኤጀንሲ (AIEA)፣ በአለም አቀፍ ታዳሽ ሃይል ኤጀንሲ (IRENA)፣ በተባበሩት መንግስታት የስታትስቲክስ ክፍል (UNSD)፣ በአለም ባንክ እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) ዛሬ የወጡት አዲስ ዘገባዎች ፣ ዓለም እ.ኤ.አ በ 2030 ሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG) የኃይል አቅርቦትን ለማሳካት መንገድ ላይ አለመሆኗን አመላክተዋል። ይህ ዓመት እ.ኤ.አ በ 2030 የዘላቂ ልማት ግብን (SDG) ለማሳካት ከተጀመረው ጉዞ የግማሽ መንገድ መዳረሻ ነው። ሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG 7) ያለመው በተመጣጣኝ ዋጋ፣ አስተማማኝ፣ ዘላቂ እና ዘመናዊ የኃይል አቅርቦትን ማረጋገጥ ነው።
translations.reference: Basic energy access lags amid renewable opportunities, new report shows Global energy access gap persists: 675 million people without electricity, 2.3 billion people reliant on harmful cooking fuels A new report by the International Energy Agency (IEA), the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), the United Nations Statistics Division (UNSD), the World Bank, and the World Health Organization (WHO), released today, finds that the world is not on track to achieve the Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 7 for energy by 2030. This year marks the halfway point for achieving SDGs by 2030. SDG 7 is to ensure access to affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy.
translations.hypothesis: A new report indicates that basic energy access has stagnated, despite the growing opportunities in renewable energy. The report highlights that 675 million people still lack access to electricity, and 2.3 billion people rely on polluting fuels for cooking. The report, which was released today by the International Energy Agency (IEA), the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), the United Nations Statistics Division (UNSD), the World Bank, and the World Health Organization (WHO), concludes that the world is not on track to achieve Sustainable Development Goal 7 (SDG 7) by 2030. The report states that we are only halfway to achieving SDG 7, which aims to ensure access to affordable, reliable, sustainable, and modern energy for all.
