translations.source: ግቡ ሁለንተናዊ የኤሌክትሪክ አቅርቦት እና ንጹህ ምግብ ማብሰል፣ ታሪካዊ የውጤታማነት ማሻሻያዎችን በእጥፍ ማሳደግ እና በአለም አቀፍ የሀይል አቅም ድብልቅ ውስጥ የታዳሽ ሃይሎችን ድርሻ በከፍተኛ ሁኔታ ማሳደግን ያጠቃልላል። ይህንን ግብ ማሳካት በሰዎች ጤና እና ደህንነት ላይ ከፍተኛ ተጽዕኖ ያሳድራል፣እንደ የአየር ብክለት ካሉ ከአካባቢያዊ እና ማህበራዊ አደጋዎች በመጠበቅ እና የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ የጤና እንክብካቤ እና አገልግሎቶችን ተደራሽነትን በማስፈሰት እገዘሰ ያደርጋል። የሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG 7) እ.ኤ.አ በ 2023 የወጣው የክትትል እትም ፡ የሀይል አቅም ግስጋሴ ወቅታዊ ጥረቶቹ የሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብን (SDG 7) በወቅቱ ለማሳካት በቂ እንዳልሆኑ ዘገባው ያስጠነቅቃል። በሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG 7) አጀንዳዎች ውስጥ በተወሰኑ ነጥቦች ላይ የተወሰነ መሻሻል ታይቷል - ለምሳሌም በኃይል አቅም ዘርፍ ውስጥ የታዳሽ መሳሪያዎችን የመጠቀም መጠን መጨመር ሲሆን- ግን በሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG 7) ውስጥ የተቀመጡትን ግቦች ላይ ለመድረስ ይህ መሻሻል ብቻ በቂ አይደለም ። የዓለማቀፉ የሀይል አቅም ቀውስ ታዳሽ መገልገያዎችን እንዲዘረጉ እና የሀይል አቅም ውጤታማነትን እንዲያሻሽሉ በርካታ የመንግስት ፖሊሲዎች የመዋለ ንዋይ ፈሰስን እንደሚያሳድጉ ይጠበቃል።
translations.reference: The goal includes reaching universal access to electricity and clean cooking, doubling historic levels of efficiency improvements, and substantially increasing the share of renewables in the global energy mix. Attaining this goal will have a deep impact on people’s health and well-being, helping to protect them from environmental and social risks such as air pollution, and expanding access to primary health care and services. The 2023 edition of Tracking SDG 7: The Energy Progress Report warns that current efforts are not enough to achieve the SDG 7 on time. There has been some progress on specific elements of the SDG 7 agenda – for example, the increased rate of using renewables in the power sector – but progress is insufficient to reach the targets set forth in the SDGs. The global energy crisis is expected to stimulate the deployment of renewables and improve energy efficiency with several government policies pointing to increasing investment.
translations.hypothesis: This encompasses universal access to electricity and clean cooking, which aims to double global energy productivity and significantly increase the share of renewable energy in the global energy mix. Achieving this goal will have a profound impact on human health and well-being, by reducing exposure to environmental and social hazards such as air pollution, and by improving access to primary healthcare and essential services. However, a recent report indicates that current efforts to achieve SDG 7 are falling short. While there have been some positive developments in the area of renewable energy – for example, an increase in the adoption of renewable energy technologies – these are not sufficient to meet the targets set for SDG 7. Several national policies are also being implemented to promote the adoption of renewable energy and improve energy efficiency, but their impact remains to be seen.

translations.source: ይሁን እንጂ፣ የ የዓለማቀፉ የታዳሽ የሀይል አቅም ኤጀንሲ (IRENA) ግምቶች እንደገመተው፣ ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው አገሮች ውስጥ ለንጹህ የሀይል አቅም ድጋፍ የሚሰጡ የአለም አቀፍ የህዝብ የገንዘብ ነክ ፍሰቶች ከኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ በፊት እየቀነሱ እና የገንዘብ ድጋፉ በጥቂቱ ሀገራት ብቻ የተወሰነ ሁኗል። የሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግቦች (SDG 7) ኢላማዎችን ለማሳካት እና ወደ ዘላቂ የሀይል አቅም በሚደረገው ሽግግር ወቅት ሰዎች የማህበራዊውና የኢኮኖሚያዊው ትርፍ ሙሉ በሙሉ ተጠቃሚ እንዲሆኑ ለማድረግ ዓለም አቀፍ የመንግስት የገንዘብ ነክ መዋቅራዊ ማሻሻያ ማድረግ እና ኢንቨስትመንቶችን ለመክፈት አዳዲስ እድሎችን መወሰን ያስፈልጋል። ዘገባው፣ በተጨማሪም እየጨመረ ያለውን ዕዳ እና የኃይል አቅም ዋጋ : ንጹሕ ማብሰያ እና ኤሌክትሪክ ሁሉን አቀፍ ተደራሽነት ለማረጋገጥ ያለውን አመለካከትም እያሽቆለቆለ ነው። አሁን ያለው ትንበያም እ.ኤ.አ በ2030 ተጨማሪ ርምጃ ካልወሰድን 1.9 ቢሊዮን ሰዎች ንፁህ ምግብ የማያገኙ እና 660ሚሊዮኑ ደግሞ ከኤሌክትሪ ውጭ ይሆናሉ። እነዚህ ክፍተቶች በጣም ተጋላጭ በሆኑ ህዝቦቻችን ጤና ላይ አሉታዊ ተጽእኖ ያሳድራሉ እናም የአየር ንብረት ለውጥን ያፋጥኑታል።
translations.reference: However, IRENA estimates show that international public financial flows in support of clean energy in low- and middle-income countries have been decreasing since before the COVID-19 pandemic and funding is limited to a small number of countries. To meet SDG 7 targets and to ensure that people fully benefit from the socio-economic gains of the shift to sustainable energy, it is necessary to structurally reform international public finance and define new opportunities to unlock investments. The report also finds that mounting debt and rising energy prices are worsening the outlook for reaching universal access to clean cooking and electricity. Current projections estimate that 1.9 billion people will be without clean cooking and 660 million without electricity access in 2030 if we do not take further action and continue with current efforts. These gaps will negatively impact the health of our most vulnerable populations and accelerate climate change.
translations.hypothesis: However, according to the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), global public finance flows for renewable energy have been declining in low- and middle-income countries before the COVID-19 pandemic, and the financial support is now concentrated in a few countries. To achieve the goals of the seventh Sustainable Development Goal (SDG 7) and ensure that people benefit fully from the transition to sustainable energy, international governments need to strengthen and reform their financial architecture, and create new opportunities for investment. The report also highlights the growing debt and the increasing cost of renewable energy, which is making it more difficult to achieve universal access to clean cooking and electricity. Current projections indicate that if no further action is taken, 1.9 billion people will be without access to clean cooking by 2030, and 660 million will be without access to electricity. These gaps will have a negative impact on the health of the most vulnerable populations, and will exacerbate climate change.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) እንደገለጸው በየዓመቱ 3.2 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች የሚሞቱት በነዳጆች መበከልን እና በቴክኖሎጅዎች እጥረት ምክንያት በሚከሰት በሽታ ሲሆን ፣ይህም በቤት ውስጥ የአየር ብክለት መመረዝ የመጋለጥ መጠንን ይጨምራል። የዘገባው ቁልፍ ግኝቶች እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2010 84% የሚሆነው የዓለም ህዝብ የኤሌክትሪክ አገልግሎት አግኝቷል። ይህ እ.ኤ.አ በ 2021 ወደ 91% አድጓል፣ ይህም ማለት በዚያ ጊዜ ውስጥ ከአንድ ቢሊዮን በላይ ሰዎች አቅርቦት አግኝተዋል። ነገር ግን እ.ኤ.አ በ2019-2021 የአገልግሎቱ መድረስ እድገት ፍጥነት ካለፉት አመታት ጋር ሲነጻጸር አዝጋሚ ነበር።
translations.reference: According to WHO, 3.2 million people die each year from illness caused by the use of polluting fuels and technologies, which increase exposure to toxic levels of household air pollution. Key findings of the report In 2010, 84% of the world’s population had access to electricity. This increased to 91% in 2021, meaning more than a billion people gained access over that period. However, the growth pace of access slowed in 2019–2021 compared to previous years.
translations.hypothesis: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), approximately 3.2 million people die each year from diseases caused by air pollution and lack of access to technology, which also contributes to indoor air pollution. Key findings indicate that by 2010, 84% of the world's population had access to electricity. This increased to 91% by 2021, meaning that over one billion people had access to power during that time. However, the rate of progress in expanding access to electricity between 2019 and 2021 was slower than in previous years.

translations.source: በገጠር የኤሌክትሪክ አቅርቦት ማድረስ ጥረቶች ለዚህ እድገት አስተዋፅዖ አድርገዋል፣ ነገር ግን በከተማ ውስጥ ትልቅ ክፍተት አለ። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2021 ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ የአፍሪካ 567 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች የኤሌክትሪክ አገልግሎት አላገኙም ፣ ይህም ተደምሮ ከ 80% በላይ የሚሆነው የዓለም ህዝብ ተደራሽነት አላገኘም። በክልሉም ያለው የኤሌክትሪክ አገልግሎት ተደራሽነት ጉድለት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2010 ከሞላ ጎደል ተመሳሳይ ሆኖ ቆይቷል። እ.ኤ.አ በ2030 ንፁህ ምግብ የማብሰል ሁለንተናዊ ተደራሽነትን ለማዳረስ አለም ከመንገድ ውጪ ሆና ቆይታለች። አሁንም ከሰሃራ በታች ባሉ አፍሪካ እና እስያ ውስጥ ለምግብ ማብሰያነት የሚበከሉ ነዳጆችን እና ቴክኖሎጂዎችን ፣እስከ 2.3 ቢሊዮን የሚደርሱ ሰዎች ይጠቀማሉ።
translations.reference: Rural electrification efforts contributed to this progress, but a large gap within urban areas remains. In 2021, 567 million people in sub-Saharan Africa did not have access to electricity, accounting for more than 80% of the global population without access. The access deficit in the region stayed almost the same as in 2010. The world remains off track to achieve universal access to clean cooking by 2030. Up to 2.3 billion people still use polluting fuels and technologies for cooking, largely in sub-Saharan Africa and Asia.
translations.hypothesis: Efforts to expand electricity access in rural areas have contributed to this progress, but there is a significant gap in urban areas. In 2021, an estimated 567 million people in sub-Saharan Africa lacked access to electricity, representing over 80% of the world's unreached population. This lack of access to electricity in urban areas has persisted, with similar figures reported in 2010. The goal of achieving universal access to clean cooking solutions by 2030 remains a significant challenge, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa and Asia, where an estimated 2.3 billion people still rely on traditional fuels and technologies for cooking.

translations.source: በባህላዊ መንገድ ባዮማስን (ከእፅዋት የሚገኝ አቅም) መጠቀም ቤተሰቦች በሳምንት እስከ 40 ሰአታት ማገዶን በመሰብሰብ እና በማብሰል እንዲያሳልፉ ያደርጋል፤ ይህም ሴቶች ወደ ስራ እንዳይሰማሩ ወይም በአካባቢው ውሳኔ ሰጪ አካላት እንዳይሆኑ እንዲሁም ህጻናት ደግሞ ወደ ትምህርት ቤት እንዳይሄዱ ያግዳቸዋል። እ.ኤ.አ በ2019 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት(WHO) ስልታዊ ግምት፣ በየአመቱ 3.2 ሚሊዮን ያለጊዜው ለሚሞቱት ሰዎች፣ በቤት ውስጥ የአየር ብክለትን በሚፈጥሩ ነዳጆችን እና ለምግብ ማብሰያ የሚጠቀሙበት ቴክኖሎጂዎች ምክንያት ነው። ታዳሽ የኤሌክትሪክ ፍጆታ በአለም አቀፍ ፍጆታ እ.ኤ.አ በ2019 ከነበረው ከ26.3% ወደ እ.ኤ.አ በ2020 28.2% አድጓል፣ ይህም በሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግቦች (SDGs) መሻሻል ክትትል ከተጀመረበት ጊዜ አንስቶ የአንድ አመት ከፍተኛ ጭማሪ ነው። እሳት ማቀጣጠል እና የተሽከርካሪ ኃይል ፈጆታዎች ፣ከሶስት አራተኛ በላይ የአለም የኃይል ፍጆታን የሚወክሉ ሲሆኑ እኚህ በታዳሾች በመቀየር ላይ ያላቸውን ድርሻ ለመጨመር የሚደረጉ ጥረቶች፣ በ 1.5oC የአየር ንብረት አላማዎችን ለማሳካት ከታቀደው ውጪ ሆነው ቆይተዋል። የአለም ኢኮኖሚ ምን ያህል ሃይል እንደሚጠቀም በዶላር የሀገር ውስጥ ምርት(GDP) የሚለካው - የሀይል አቅም ጥንካሬ - እ.ኤ.አ ከ2010-2020 በ1.8% በየዓመቱ ተሻሽሏል።
translations.reference: The use of traditional biomass also means households spend up to 40 hours a week gathering firewood and cooking, which prohibits women from pursuing employment or participating in local decision-making bodies and children from going to school. According to the 2019 WHO estimates, 3.2 million premature deaths each year were attributable to household air pollution created by using polluting fuels and technologies for cooking. Renewable electricity use in global consumption has grown from 26.3% in 2019 to 28.2% in 2020, the largest single-year increase since the start of tracking progress for the SDGs. Efforts to increase renewables’ share in heating and transport, which represent more than three quarters of global energy consumption, remain off target to achieve 1.5oC climate objectives. Energy intensity – the measure of how much energy the global economy uses per dollar of GDP – improved from 2010–2020 by 1.8% annually.
translations.hypothesis: Using traditional biomass (fuel derived from vegetation) for cooking requires families to spend up to 40 hours per week collecting and preparing fuel, which can prevent women from participating in other activities, such as going to work or engaging in decision-making within their communities. It also prevents children from attending school. According to the World Health Organization (WHO) in 2019, household air pollution, caused by the burning of solid fuels for cooking and heating, is a major contributor to premature deaths, accounting for 3.2 million deaths annually. Global electricity consumption increased from 26.3% of total final energy consumption in 2019 to 28.2% in 2020, the highest annual increase since the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) were adopted. The use of solid fuels for cooking and heating accounts for over three-quarters of global energy consumption, and efforts to shift to cleaner energy sources are falling short of the 1.5°C climate target. The world's economy uses a significant amount of energy, measured in terms of domestic product (GDP) - energy intensity - which increased by 1.8% per year from 2010 to 2020.

translations.source: ይህ ካለፉት አስርት አመታት የ1.2% መሻሻል ጋር ሲነጻጸር ከፍ ያለም ነው። ይሁን እንጂ በቅርብ ዓመታት ውስጥ የኃይል ጥንካሬ መሻሻል መጠን እየቀነሰ መጥቶ እናም እ.ኤ.አ በ 2020 ወደ 0.6% ወርዷል። ይህ ከዓለም አቀፍ የገንዘብ ቀውስ ወዲህ፣ ለኃይል መጠን መሻሻል በጣም መጥፎው ዓመት ያደርገዋል ፣ ምንም እንኳን በአብዛኛው ከወረርሽኙ (ኮቪድ 19) ጋር በተያያዙ ገደቦች ምክንያት በመሆኑ ፣ ይህ ምናልባትም ጊዜያዊ ውድቀትን ብቻ ሊያመለክት ይችላል። እ.ኤ.አ እስከ 2030 ድረስ ያለው አመታዊ መሻሻል አሁን በአማካይ 3.4% መሆን አለበት፣ የዘላቂ ልማት ግብን (SDG ) ንዑስ አንቀፅ 7.3 ን ለማሳካት፤ በማደግ ላይ ባሉ ሀገራት ለንፁህ የሀይል አቅም ድጋፍ የሚደረጉ አለማቀፋዊ የህዝብ የገንዘብ ነክ ፍሰቶች እ.ኤ.አ በ2021 10.8 ቢሊዮን የአሜሪካ ዶላር፣ እ.ኤ.አ ከ2010–2019 አማካኝ 35% ያነሰ እና እ.ኤ.አ ከ2017ቱ 40 በመቶው ብቻ 26.4 ቢሊዮን ዶላር ደርሷል። እ.ኤ.አ በ 2021 19 አገሮች 80% ሀላፊነቱን ተቀብለዋል፣ ዘገባውም እ.ኤ.አ በመስከረም 2023 በኒው ዮርክ ከሚካሄደው ከሁለተኛው የዘላቂ ልማት ግቦች ስብሰባ በፊት እ.ኤ.አ በሀምሌ 11 ቀን 2023፣በዘላቂ ልማት ከፍተኛ የፖለቲካ መድረክ ላይ ለከፍተኛ ውሳኔ ሰጪዎች ይቀርባል።
translations.reference: This is higher than the 1.2% improvement from the previous decades. However, the rate of energy intensity improvement has slowed in recent years and dropped to 0.6% in 2020. This makes it the worst year for energy intensity improvement since the global financial crisis, albeit largely due to pandemic-related restrictions, which may indicate only a temporary setback. Annual improvements through 2030 must now average 3.4% to meet the SDG target 7.3. International public financial flows in support of clean energy in developing countries stand at US$ 10.8 billion in 2021, 35% less than the 2010–2019 average and only about 40% of the 2017 peak of US$ 26.4 billion. In 2021, 19 countries received 80% of the commitments.The report will be presented to top decision-makers at a special launch event on 11 July 2023 at the High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) on Sustainable Development, ahead of the second SDG Summit in September 2023 in New York.
translations.hypothesis: This represents a significant increase compared to the 1.2% improvement seen in the previous decade. However, the rate of improvement in energy intensity has been slowing in recent years, falling to 0.6% in 2020. This makes 2020 the worst year on record for energy intensity, although this may simply reflect temporary setbacks due to the global economic crisis and the associated restrictions, particularly those related to the COVID-19 pandemic. Annual improvements are projected to average 3.4% between 2021 and 2030, in order to achieve Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 7.3, which focuses on increasing access to affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy. Global public finance flows for clean energy in developing countries increased from $10.8 billion in 2021, down from an average of 35% of 2010-2019, to just 26.4 billion in 2017. In 2021, 19 countries accounted for 80% of these flows, and the report will be presented at the second Sustainable Development Goals Conference in New York on November 11, 2023, before the High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development.

translations.source: አለም አቀፉ ማህበረሰብ እና ፖሊሲ አውጪዎች በሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG 7)ን ለማሳካት የተገኙትን ድሎች ጠብቀው እንዲቀጥሉ፣ መዋቅራዊ ማሻሻያዎችን እንዲያካሂዱ እና ከፍተኛ ድጋፍ በሚያስፈልጋቸው ተጋላጭ ሀገራት ላይ ስልታዊ ትኩረትን እንዲያደርጉ ደራሲዎቹ አሳስበዋል። ጥቅሶቹ: "ሩሲያ በዩክሬን ወረራ ወቅት የተከተለው የኃይል ቀውስ በዓለም ዙሪያ ባሉ ሰዎች ላይ ከፍተኛ ተጽዕኖ ማሳደሩን ቀጥሏል። ከፍተኛ የሀይል አቅም ዋጋ በጣም ተጋላጭ የሆኑትን በተለይም በማደግ ላይ ባሉ ኢኮኖሚዎች ላይ ከፍተኛ ተጽዕኖ አሳድሯል። የንፁህ የሀይል አቅም ሽግግር ብዙ ሰዎች ከሚያስቡት በላይ በፍጥነት እየተንቀሳቀሰ ቢሆንም ያለሱ ለሚኖሩ በቢሊዮን ለሚቆጠሩ ሰዎች ዘመናዊ የሀይል አቅም አገልግሎትን ዘላቂ፣ አስተማማኝ እና በተመጣጣኝ ዋጋ ተደራሽ ለማድረግ አሁንም ትልቅ ስራ ያስፈልጋል። የተሳካ የሀይል አቅም ሽግግሮች፣ ውጤታማ ፖሊሲዎች እና የቴክኖሎጂ ፈጠራዎች ከትልቅ የመዋእለ ንዋይ ፈሰስ አቅም ማሰባሰብ ጋር ተደምሮ አስፈላጊ ናቸው።
translations.reference: The authors urge the international community and policymakers to safeguard the gains made toward achieving SDG 7, to advance structural reforms, and to maintain a strategic focus on the vulnerable countries needing the most support. Quotes: “The energy crisis sparked by Russia’s invasion of Ukraine continues to have a profound impact on people all around the world. High energy prices have hit the most vulnerable hard, particularly those in developing economies. While the clean energy transition is moving faster than many think, there is still a great deal of work needed to deliver sustainable, secure and affordable access to modern energy services for the billions of people who live without it. Successful energy transitions rely on effective policies and technological innovation combined with large-scale mobilisation of investment capital.
translations.hypothesis: The authors urge the international community and policymakers to continue building on the successes achieved in achieving Sustainable Development Goal 7 (SDG 7), to implement structural reforms, and to focus strategically on countries most in need of support. They highlight that the ongoing energy crisis, particularly in Ukraine, has had a significant impact on people around the world. "The ongoing energy crisis, particularly in Ukraine, has had a significant impact on people around the world. The crisis has also had a significant impact on the prices of energy, particularly for those who are most vulnerable. While the transition to clean energy is progressing faster than many people thought, it is still a major challenge to ensure that everyone has access to affordable, reliable, and clean energy. Successful energy transitions require a combination of policy reforms, technological innovation, and financial support."

translations.source: የአለም አቀፉ ማህበረሰብ እነዚህን ሁሉ መሳሪያዎችን፣ በዚህ አስርት አመታት መጨረሻ ላይ የዘላቂ ልማቱን ግቦች(SDG) ለማሳካት መጠቀም ይኖርበታል፤" ሲል 'ፋቲህ ቢሮል' ፣የአለም አቀፍ የሀይል አቅም ኤጀንሲ ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዘግቧል። "በዋጋ-ተፎካካሪ የሆነው ታዳሽ ኃይል አሁንም አስደናቂ ጥንካሬን አሳይቷል፣ ነገር ግን በዓለም ላይ በጣም ድሃ የሆኑት ክልሎች አሁንም ከእሱ ሙሉ በሙሉ ተጠቃሚ ሊሆኑ አልቻሉም። የአየር ንብረት ግቦችን ሳንነካ የሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG 7) እውን ለማድረግ በአለም አቀፍ ህብረታዊ አሰራር ላይ የስርዓት ለውጥ ማምጣት አለብን። የብዝሀ-ቀውስ ደጋፊ የገንዘብ ነክ ተቋማት የገንዘብ ፍሰቶችን ይበልጥ ፍትሃዊ በሆነ መልኩ የታዳሽ ሃይሎችን በዓለም ዙሪያ ለማዳረስ እና ተዛማጅ የአካላዊ መሠረተ ልማት ግንባታዎችን መደገፍ ወሳኝ ነው፤ ሲል 'ፍራንቸስኮ ላ ካሜራ'፣ የዓለም አቀፍ ታዳሽ ኃይል ኤጀንሲ ዋና ዳይሬክተር ተናግሯል።" "በአጀንዳው እ.ኤ.አ በ2030 አጋማሽ ላይ የሰባተኛው የዘላቂ የኃይል ኢላማዎችን ለማስፈፀም መሻሻሎች ቢደረጉም፣ ግብ 7 ግን እ.ኤ.አ በ 2015 ከነበረው የበለጠ አስቸጋሪ ይመስላል እናም ተመጣጣኝ፣ አስተማማኝ፣ ዘላቂ እና ዘመናዊ የሀይል አቅምን እ.ኤ.አ በ 2030 ለሁሉም ያለውን ተደራሽነትን ለማረጋገጥ ከተፈለገ የተጠናከረ እርምጃ አስፈላጊ ነው።
translations.reference: The international community must leverage all these tools to meet the Sustainable Development Goals by the end of this decade.” Fatih Birol, Executive Director, International Energy Agency “Cost-competitive renewable energy has yet again demonstrated remarkable resilience, but the poorest in the world are still largely unable to fully benefit from it. To realise SDG7 without compromising climate goals, we must bring about systemic change in the way international cooperation works. It is crucial that multilateral financial institutions direct financial flows more equitably around the world to support renewables deployment and related physical infrastructure development.” Francesco La Camera, Director-General, International Renewable Energy Agency “Despite advances towards sustainable energy targets at the mid-point of Agenda 2030, Goal 7 seems harder to reach than it was in 2015 and scaled-up action is necessary if we are to ensure access to affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy for all by 2030.
translations.hypothesis: "The international community must utilize these tools to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in the coming decades," said Fateh Bilal, Director of the International Renewable Energy Agency. "While renewable energy has demonstrated remarkable strength in a competitive market, it has not yet fully benefited the world's poorest regions. To achieve SDG 7, which aims to ensure access to affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy for all, we need to bring about systemic change in international cooperation. Support for financial mechanisms that promote the equitable distribution of renewable energy resources around the world, and the development of complementary physical infrastructure, is crucial," said Francisco La Camara, Director of the International Renewable Energy Agency. "While progress has been made towards achieving the SDG 7 targets by mid-2030, the goal remains challenging and requires intensified efforts to ensure that affordable, reliable, sustainable and modern energy is accessible to all by 2030."

translations.source: የኤሌክትሪክ አቅርቦት እና ንፁህ ምግብ ማብሰያ አሁንም ትልቅ ክልላዊ ልዩነቶችን ስለሚያሳዩ ማንም ሰው ወደ ኋላ እንዳይቀር የተግባር ስራ ትኩረት መሆን አለበት። ወደ ግብ 7 የበለጠ ፍትሃዊ የሆነ እድገትን ለማረጋገጥ የመዋዕለ ንዋይ ፍሰቱ ባላደጉ ሀገራት እና ከሰሃራ በታች ላሉ የአፍሪካ ሀገራት መድረስ አለበት፤ ሲል 'ስቴፋን ስሽዌንፌስት'፣ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ስታቲስቲክስ ክፍል ሀላፊ ተናግሯል።" "በቅርብ ጊዜ በአለም አቀፍ የኤሌትሪክ ሀይል ማዳረስ ፍጥነት ላይ መቀዛቀዝ ቢኖርም፣ ባለፉት አስርት አመታት ውስጥ የኤሌክትሪክ ሃይል የሌላቸው ሰዎች ቁጥር በግማሽ ያህል ቀንሷል፣ እ.ኤ.አ በ 2010 ከ 1.1 ቢሊዮን እ.ኤ.አ በ 2021 ወደ 675 ሚሊዮን ዝቅ ብሏል። ቢሆንም፣ በጣም ድሆችንና እና ለመድረስ አስቸጋሪ የሆኑ ሰዎች እንዲደርሳቸው ለማድረግ ተጨማሪ ጥረቶችን እና እርምጃዎችን በአስቸኳይ መተግበር አለበት። እ.ኤ.አ በ 2030 ሁለንተናዊ ተደራሽነት ላይ ለመድረስ የልማቱ ማህበረሰብ የንፁህ የሀይል አቅም የመዋዕለ ንዋይ ፍሰቶችን እና የፖሊሲ ድጋፍን ማሳደግ አለበት ፤ ሲሉ የዓለም ባንክ የመሠረተ ልማት ምክትል ፕሬዚዳንት 'ጓንግሄ ቼን' ተናግረዋል።"
translations.reference: Access to electricity and clean cooking still display great regional disparities and should be the focus of action to ensure that no one is left behind. Investment needs to reach the least-developed countries and sub-Saharan Africa to ensure more equitable progress toward Goal 7.” Stefan Schweinfest, United Nations Statistics Division “Despite a recent slowdown in the global pace of electrification, the number of people without electricity almost halved over the past decade, from 1.1 billion in 2010 down to 675 million in 2021. Nonetheless, additional efforts and measures must urgently be put in place to ensure that the poorest and hardest-to-reach people are not left behind. To reach universal access by 2030, the development community must scale up clean energy investments and policy support.” Guangzhe Chen, Vice President for Infrastructure, World Bank
translations.hypothesis: Given that access to electricity and clean cooking remains a significant regional disparity, it is crucial to prioritize efforts to ensure that everyone has access. "According to Stefan Schweinfurster, a statistics officer at the United Nations, 'To achieve Goal 7, access to electricity must reach the least developed countries and those in sub-Saharan Africa'," he said. "While the global rate of electricity access has slowed in recent years, the number of people without electricity has fallen by more than half, from 1.1 billion in 2010 to 675 million in 2021. However, more efforts and action are needed to ensure that the poorest and most remote populations have access. 'The development community must scale up investments in clean energy and policy support to achieve universal access by 2030,' said Guang Chen, Deputy President of the World Bank for Infrastructure."

translations.source: "አሁን ወደ ተግባር በመግባት ቀጣዩን ትውልድ መጠበቅ አለብን። እውነተኛ ለውጥ ማምጣትንም የምንችለው፣ ሁለንተናዊ የኃይል አቅርቦትን ለመደገፍ ንጹህ እና ታዳሽ መፍትሄዎች ላይ የመዋዕለ ንዋይ ፈሰስን በማድረግ ነው። በቤት ውስጥ ንጹህ የምግብ ማብሰያ ቴክኖሎጂዎች እና በጤና ጥበቃ ተቋማት ውስጥ አስተማማኝ የኤሌክትሪክ ኃይልን ተደራሽ ማድረግ፣ በጣም ተጋላጭ የሆኑትን ህዝቦቻችንን ጤና ለመጠበቅ ወሳኝ ሚና ሊጫወቱ ይችላሉ፤ ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር የሆኑት 'ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ' ተናግረዋል።" ስለ ዘገባው ይህ ዘገባ፣ የሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG 7) ጠባቂ ኤጀንሲዎች፣ በአለም አቀፍ የሀይል አቅም ኤጀንሲ (AEA)፣ በአለምአቀፍ ታዳሽ የሀይል አቅም ኤጀንሲ (IRENA)፣ በተባበሩት መንግስታት የስታቲስቲክስ ክፍል (UNSD)፣ በአለም ባንክ እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) የታተመ ሲሆን እናም ለዓለም አቀፉ ማህበረሰብ በኃይል አቅርቦት፣ በሃይል ቆጣቢነት፣ በታዳሽ ሃይል እና በአለም አቀፍ ትብብር በሰባተኛውን የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ (SDG 7) ላይ እድገትን ለማስመዝገብ በአለም አቀፍ መዝገብ ላይ ለማቅረብ ያለመ ነው።
translations.reference: “We must protect the next generation by acting now. Investing in clean and renewable solutions to support universal energy access is how we can make real change. Clean cooking technologies in homes and reliable electricity in health-care facilities can play a crucial role in protecting the health of our most vulnerable populations.” Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General, World Health Organization About the report This report is published by the SDG 7 custodian agencies, the International Energy Agency (IEA), the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), the United Nations Statistics Division (UNSD), the World Bank, and the World Health Organization (WHO) and aims to provide the international community with a global dashboard to register progress on energy access, energy efficiency, renewable energy and international cooperation to advance SDG 7.
translations.hypothesis:
"Now, we must move towards action and work towards a sustainable future. We can achieve real change by investing in clean and renewable energy solutions to support universal energy access. By making clean cooking technologies and reliable electricity accessible in homes and healthcare facilities, we can play a crucial role in protecting the health of the most vulnerable populations," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization.

This report, published by the seventh Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) custodian agencies, the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), the United Nations Statistics Division (UNSD), the World Bank, and the World Health Organization (WHO), provides a comprehensive overview of progress on SDG 7, focusing on energy access, energy affordability, renewable energy, and international cooperation. The report is intended to inform the international community and to contribute to the global SDG 7 dashboard.

translations.source: ይህም የዘገባው የዘጠነኛው እትም ሲሆን፣ ከዚህ ቀደም የአለምአቀፍ መከታተያ ማዕቀፍ (GTF) በመባል ይታወቃል። የዚህ አመት እትምም በአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) መሪነት ተዘግቧል። ዘገባውን እዚህ ላይ ወደ ራስዎ ማውረድ ይቻላል። ለዘገባው የገንዘብ ድጋፍ የተደረገው በአለም ባንክ የሀይል አቅም መስክ አስተዳደር እርዳታ መርሀ ግብር (ESMAP) ነው።
translations.reference: This is the ninth edition of this report, formerly known as the Global Tracking Framework (GTF). This year’s edition was chaired by the World Health Organization (WHO). The report can be downloaded here Funding for the report was provided by the World Bank’s Energy Sector Management Assistance Program (ESMAP).
translations.hypothesis: This is the ninth edition of the report, which was previously known as the Global Tobacco Survey (GTS). This year's edition was published by the World Health Organization (WHO). You can download the report here. The report was supported by the World Bank's Enterprise Support for Healthier Economies (ESHA) program.

translations.source: የአውሮፓ ኮሚሽን እና የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ደህንነትን ለማጠናከር ጉልህ የሆነ የዲጂታል ጤና ተነሳሽነት ጀመሩ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) እና የአውሮፓ ኮሚሽን፣ ዛሬ ዲጂታል የጤና ሽርክና መጀመራቸውን አስታውቀዋል። እ.ኤ.አ በሰኔ 2023 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የአውሮፓ ህብረት (EU) የዲጂታል ኮቪድ-19 የምስክር ወረቀትን ስርዓት በመዘርጋት ዓለም አቀፋዊ እንቅስቃሴን ለማመቻቸት እና በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ ዜጎችን ወረርሽኞች ጨምሮ፣ ከሚከሰቱ እና ወደፊት ከሚመጡ የጤና ስጋቶች ለመከላከል የሚረዳ ዓለም አቀፍ ስርዓት ለመመስረት ያስችላል። ይህ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) አለምአቀፋዊ የዲጂታል የኮቪድ-19 የምስክር ወረቀት መረብን( WHO Global Digital Health Certification Network (GDHCN) የመጀመሪያው የግንባታ መሰረት ሲሆን ይህም ሰፋ ያለ ዲጂታል ምርቶችን በማዘጋጀት ለሁሉም የተሻለ ጤናን ይሰጣል። "በአውሮፓ ህብረት እጅግ ስኬታማ በሆነው የዲጂታል ሰርተፍኬት መረብ አሰራር ላይ በመነሳት፡ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ለሁሉም የአለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት በፍትሃዊነት ፣በፈጠራ ፣በግልጽነት እና በመረጃ ጥበቃ እንዲሁም በግላዊነት መርሆዎች ላይ የተመሰረተ ለተጠቃሚዎች ክፍት የሆነ ዲጂታል የጤና መሳሪያ እንዲያገኙ ለማድረግ ያለመ ነው።" ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ ተናግረዋል።
translations.reference: The European Commission and WHO launch landmark digital health initiative to strengthen global health security The World Health Organization (WHO) and European Commission have announced today the launch of a landmark digital health partnership. In June 2023, WHO will take up the European Union (EU) system of digital COVID-19 certification to establish a global system that will help facilitate global mobility and protect citizens across the world from on-going and future health threats, including pandemics. This is the first building block of the WHO Global Digital Health Certification Network (GDHCN) that will develop a wide range of digital products to deliver better health for all. “Building on the EU’s highly successful digital certification network, WHO aims to offer all WHO Member States access to an open-source digital health tool, which is based on the principles of equity, innovation, transparency and data protection and privacy,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General.
translations.hypothesis: The European Commission and the World Health Organization (WHO) have launched a major initiative to strengthen global health security through digital health. The WHO and the European Commission have announced the launch of a new global initiative to create a digital health certification system that will help to facilitate international travel and protect people from health risks, including those caused by COVID-19. The WHO's Global Digital Health Certification Network (GDHCN) is the first step in this initiative, and it will provide a standardized way for countries to verify the vaccination status of travelers. "Based on the successful digital certificate system in the European Union, the WHO aims to provide all WHO member states with access to a user-friendly digital health tool that is based on the principles of fairness, transparency, data protection, and privacy," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO.

translations.source: "አዳዲስ ዲጂታል የጤና ምርቶች የሚዘጋጁት በየትኛውም ቦታ ያሉ ሰዎች ጥራቱን የጠበቀ የጤና አገልግሎትን በፍጥነት እና በብቃት እንዲያገኙ ለመርዳት ነው።" ይህ ተነሳሽነት በአውሮፓ ህብረት አለም አቀፍ የጤና ስትራቴጂ እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት የዲጂታል ጤና ስትራቴጂ ላይ በመመስረት፤ በኮሚሽነር ኪርያኪደስ እና በዶ/ር ቴድሮስ መካከል እ.ኤ.አ. ህዳር 30 ቀን 2022 በተደረገው ስምምነት መሰረት በአለም የጤና ጉዳዮች ላይ ትብብርን ለማሳደግ ያለመ ነው። ይህ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) በሚጫወተው ማዕከላዊ ሚና እንዲሁም ጠንካራ በሆነው የአውሮፓ ህብረት ድጋፍ፤ ሁለገብ የሆነ ስርዓትን የበለጠ ያጠናክራል። “ይህ አጋርነት ለአውሮፓ ህብረት የአለም አቀፍ የጤና ስትራቴጂ ዲጂታል የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር አስፈላጊ እርምጃ ነው። የአውሮፓን ምርጥ ልምዶች በመጠቀም ለአለምአቀፍ ዲጂታል የጤና ደረጃዎች እና መስተጋብር መሻሻል አስተዋፅዖ እናደርጋለን፤ ይህም የተቸገሩት ለመጥቀም ነው። በአውሮፓ ህብረት እና በአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) መካከል ያለው ትብብር፤ በአውሮፓ ህብረት ሃገራት እንዲሁም በዓለም ዙሪያ ላሉ ሃገራት ሁሉ የተሻለ ጤናን እንዴት ማቅረብ እንደሚቻል የሚያሳይ ጠንካራ ምሳሌ ነው።
translations.reference: “New digital health products in development aim to help people everywhere receive quality health services quickly and more effectively”. Based on the EU Global Health Strategy and WHO Global strategy on digital health, the initiative follows the 30 November 2022 agreement between Commissioner Kyriakides and Dr Tedros to enhance strategic cooperation on global health issues. This further bolsters a robust multilateral system with WHO at its core, powered by a strong EU.“This partnership is an important step for the digital action plan of the EU Global Health Strategy. By using European best practices we contribute to digital health standards and interoperability globally—to the benefit of those most in need. It is also a powerful example of how alignment between the EU and the WHO can deliver better health for all, in the EU and across the world.
translations.hypothesis: "The initiative aims to help people in any location access high-quality healthcare services quickly and efficiently, enabling the development and deployment of new digital health products." This collaboration, based on the European Union's global health strategy and the World Health Organization's digital health strategy, was formalized in an agreement signed on November 30, 2022, between Commissioner Kyriakides and Dr. Tedros. It seeks to strengthen cooperation in global health, leveraging the central role played by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the strong support of the European Union. "This partnership is a key step in the European Union's global health strategy, specifically its digital action plan. By leveraging the best practices of the European Union, we can contribute to improved global digital health standards and cooperation, ultimately saving lives." The collaboration between the European Union and the World Health Organization (WHO) is a powerful example of how to improve healthcare for all, including European countries and countries around the world.

translations.source: የአለም አቀፍ የጤና ስራን የመምራት እና የማስተባበር ባለስልጣን እንደመሆኑ መጠን፤ በአውሮፓ ህብረት የጀመርነውን ስራ ለማራመድ እና ተጨማሪ አለምአቀፍ ዲጂታል የጤና መፍትሄዎችን ለማዳበር፤ ከአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) የተሻለ አጋር የለም።” ያሉት የጤና እና የምግብ ደህንነት ኮሚሽነር ስቴላ ኪሪያኪደስ ናቸው። ይህ አጋርነት የአለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO)፤ የአለምአቀፍ ዲጂታል የጤና ማረጋገጫ መረብ ስርዓት አዘገጃጀት፣ አስተዳደር እና አተገባበር ሂደት ውስጥ የአውሮፓዊያን ኮሚሽን በዘርፉ ያለውን በቂ የቴክኒክ እውቀት መጠቀም እንዲችል ሰፊ ትብብርን ይፈጥራል።. የመጀመሪያው እርምጃ የአሁኑ የአውሮፓ ህብረት ዲጂታል የምስክር ወረቀቶች በብቃት መስራታቸውን ማረጋገጥ ነው። የአውሮፓ ህብረት በዲጂታል የኮቪድ-19 ሰርተፍኬት 80 አገሮችን እና ግዛቶችን በማገናኘት የዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ መለኪያን ከፍ አድርጓል። ይህ የምስክር ወረቀት ወረርሽኙን ለመቆጣጠር በምናደርገው ትግል ጠቃሚ መሳሪያ ብቻ ሳይሆን አለም አቀፍ ጉዞ እና ቱሪዝም ላይም ከፍተኛ አስተዋጶ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: As the directing and coordinating authority on international health work, there is no better partner than the WHO to advance the work we started at the EU and further develop global digital health solutions,” said Stella Kyriakides, Commissioner for Health and Food Safety. This partnership will include close collaboration in the development, management and implementation of the WHO GDHCN system, benefitting from the European Commission’s ample technical expertise in the field. A first step is to ensure that the current EU digital certificates continue to function effectively.“ With 80 countries and territories connected to the EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate, the EU has set a global standard. The EU certificate has not only been an important tool in our fight against the pandemic, but has also facilitated international travel and tourism.
translations.hypothesis: "As the leading international body for health and food safety, the European Union has a unique opportunity to build on its pioneering work and develop further global digital health solutions. The World Health Organization (WHO) is the ideal partner for this endeavor." According to Stella Kyriakides, Commissioner for Health and Food Safety. This partnership will enable the WHO to leverage the European Commission's extensive technical expertise in the development, implementation, and management of the Global Digital Health Certification System. The first step will be to ensure that the current European Union digital certificates are effective. The European Union's digital COVID-19 certificate has already connected 80 countries and territories, setting a new global standard. This certificate has not only been a valuable tool in our fight against the pandemic, but it has also had a significant impact on global travel and tourism.

translations.source: የአገር ውስጥ ገበያ ኮሚሽነር ቲዬሪ ብሬተን “የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ከአውሮፓ ህብረት የምስክር ወረቀት፤ ግላዊነትን ያማከሉ መርሆዎች እና ዘመናዊ ቴክኖሎጂን በመጠቀም፣ ለወደፊት ወረርሽኞች አለም አቀፍ መሳሪያ በማዘጋጀቱ ደስተኛ ነኝ።” ብለዋል። በአውሮፓ ህብረት ባገኛቸው ስኬቶች ላይ የሚገነባ አለም አቀፍ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስርዓት፤ .የአውሮፓ ህብረት ወረርሽኙን ለመከላከል በሚያደርገው ጥረት ዲጂታል የኮቪድ-19 የምስክር ወረቀቶችን ማዘጋጀት እንደ ቁልፍ ነገር ሆኖ ቆይቶል። ሰዎች በድንበር አካባቢዎች ላይ በነፃነት እንዲንቀሳቀሱ ለማገዝ የአውሮፓ ህብረት የኮቪድ-19 ሰርተፊኬቶችን ('EU Digital COVID-19 certificate') ወይም በአጭሩ ('EU DCC') በፍጥነት አዘጋጅቶል። ለሁሉም በይፋ በሚገኙ ቴክኖሎጂዎች እና መመዘኛዎች ላይ በመመስረት፤ የአውሮፓ ህብረት አባል ያልሆኑ ሃገራት ዲጂታል የኮቪድ 19 ሰርተፊኬት (Digital COVID-19 certificate) ዝርዝሮችን መሰረት አድርገው የምስክር ወረቀቱን እንዲሰጡ አስችሎል፤ ይህም በአለምአቀፍ ደረጃ በብዛት ጥቅም ላይ እንዲውል አድርጎታል።
translations.reference: I am pleased that the WHO will build on the privacy-preserving principles and cutting-edge technology of the EU certificate to create a global tool against future pandemics,” added Thierry Breton, Commissioner for Internal Market. A global WHO system building on EU legacy One of the key elements in the European Union’s work against the COVID-19 pandemic has been digital COVID-19 certificates. To facilitate free movement within its borders, the EU swiftly established interoperable COVID-19 certificates (entitled ‘EU Digital COVID-19 Certificate’ or ‘EU DCC’). Based on open-source technologies and standards it allowed also for the connection of non-EU countries that issue certificates according to EU DCC specifications, becoming the most widely used solution around the world.
translations.hypothesis:
"As the Commissioner for Domestic Market, Thierry Breton, stated, "I am pleased that the World Health Organization is using the European Union's model, which incorporates privacy principles and modern technology, to create a global system for future pandemics. The success of the EU in combating the pandemic has been a key factor in developing this system."

The EU's digital COVID-19 certificate (EU DCC) has been instrumental in the WHO's efforts to create a global system for future pandemics. The EU's certificate, which was developed quickly and easily, allows people to travel freely within the EU and to other countries that recognize the certificate. The EU DCC is based on a common set of standards and technologies, which makes it easy for other countries to adopt.

translations.source: ወረርሽኙ ከጀመረበት ጊዜ ጀምሮ፣ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከሁሉም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ክልሎች ጋር በመተባበር ለእነዚህ ዓይነቶቹ የምስክር ወረቀቶች አጠቃላይ መመሪያዎችን አዘጋጅቷል። ከጊዜ ወደ ጊዜ እየጨመሩ የሚሄዱትን የጤና ስጋቶችን ለመጋፈጥ እና ዝግጁነትን ለማጠናከር እንዲረዳ፤ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት አለም አቀፍ ዲጂታል የጤና የምስክር ወረቀት መረብ እየዘረጋ ነው። ይህ የአውታር መረብ በአውሮፓ ህብረት በዲጂታል የኮቪድ 19 ሰርተፊኬት ማዕቀፍ፣ መርሆች እና ለሁሉም ይፋ የሆኑ ቴክኖሎጂዎች ላይ በጠንካራ መሰረት ይገነባል። በዚህም ትብብር የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የራሱን መዋቅር በመጠቀም የዲጂታል ሰርተፍኬቶችን በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ማስተባበርን ዓላማ ያደረገ ሲሆን ይህም ሰዎች ዓለም አቀፍ ጥቅሞቻቸውን እንዲያገኙ ለማስቻል ነው። ይህም የማጭበርበር ድርጊቶችን ለመከላከል መመሪያዎችን ማስቀመጥ እና ዲጂታል ፊርማን ማረጋገጥን ያካትታል። በዚህ ሂደት ውስጥ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ምንም አይነት የሰዎችን የግል መረጃ የማግኘት መብት አይኖረውም፤ ይህም የመንግስት ብቸኛ ኃላፊነት ሆኖ ይቆያል።
translations.reference: From the onset of the pandemic, WHO engaged with all WHO Regions to define overall guidelines for such certificates. To help strengthen global health preparedness in the face of growing health threats, WHO is establishing a global digital health certification network which builds upon the solid foundations of the EU DCC framework, principles and open technologies. With this collaboration, WHO will facilitate this process globally under its own structure with the aim to allow the world to benefit from convergence of digital certificates. This includes standard-setting and validation of digital signatures to prevent fraud. In doing so, WHO will not have access to any underlying personal data, which would continue to be the exclusive domain of governments.
translations.hypothesis: Since the pandemic began, the World Health Organization has been working with all its regional offices to develop comprehensive guidelines for these types of certificates. The WHO is also establishing a global digital health certificate network to help countries address and prepare for future health challenges. This network will be built on a strong foundation of interoperability, with a focus on common standards and technologies. The goal of this initiative is to enable countries to cooperate and share health certificate data, making it easier for people to travel and access essential services. The WHO will also provide guidance on how to implement and use digital certificates, as well as how to ensure their security and privacy. Importantly, the WHO will not collect any personal data from individuals, as this will be the sole responsibility of each country.

translations.source: የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የመጀመሪያው አለም አቀፍ ስርዓት እ.ኤ.አ በሰኔ 2023 ተግባራዊ ይሆናል፣ በሚቀጥሉት ወራትም ለቀጣይ እየሰሩ ለመቀጠል እቅድ ይዘዋል። ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ውጤቶችን ለማሻሻል ዘላቂ ዲጂታል ሽርክና መፍጠር፤ የአውሮፓ ህብረት ዲጂታል የኮቪድ 19 ሰርተፊኬት (Digital COVID-19 certificate) ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት (WHO) ስራዎች ጋር እንዲዋሃድ እና እየታየ ያለውን እድገት ለመደገፍ፤ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የአውሮፓ ኮሚሽን ለመተባበር ተስማምተዋል። ይህ ትብብር የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ቴክኒካል ማሻሻያ ላይ የሚያተኩር ሲሆን ቀስ በቀስ አቅሙን በማስፋፋት እንደ ዓለም አቀፍ የክትባት የምስክር ወረቀት እና ይህንን የመሳሰሉ ተጨማሪ ሁኔታዎችን ለመሸፈን ያስችላል። የአለም አቀፍ የጤና አጠባበቅ ውጤቶችን ለማሻሻል የዲጂታል መፍትሄዎችን ወሰን ማስፋፋት ወሳኝ ይሆናል።
translations.reference: The first building block of the global WHO system becomes operational in June 2023 and aims to be progressively developed in the coming months. A long-term digital partnership to deliver better health for all To facilitate the uptake of the EU DCC by WHO and contribute to its operation and further development, WHO and the European Commission have agreed to partner in digital health. This partnership will work to technically develop the WHO system with a staged approach to cover additional use cases, which may include, for example, the digitisation of the International Certificate of Vaccination or Prophylaxis. Expanding such digital solutions will be essential to deliver better health for citizens across the globe.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization's first global digital health system will be implemented in June 2023, with plans to continue working on it in the following months. The goal is to improve global health outcomes through the creation of sustainable digital infrastructure; to integrate the European Union's digital COVID-19 certificate with WHO's work; and to support the progress being made. The WHO and the European Commission have agreed to cooperate on this initiative, which will focus on technical improvements for the WHO, with the aim of gradually expanding its capacity to support the global vaccine certificate and other similar initiatives. Expanding the use of digital solutions to improve global health outcomes will be crucial.

translations.source: ይህ ሽርክና የተመሰረተው ግልጽነት፣ አካታችነት፣ ተጠያቂነት፣ የመረጃ ጥበቃ፣ ደህንነት፣ ልኬታማነት እና ፍትሃዊነትን ጨምሮ በጋራ እሴቶች እና መርሆዎች ላይ ነው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የአውሮፓ ኮሚሽን ሰፊውን ዓለም አቀፍ ተሳትፎ ለማበረታታት ይተባበራሉ። ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ያላቸው ሀገራት የመሳተፍ ፍትሃዊ እድሎች እንዲኖራቸው ይደረጋል።
translations.reference: This cooperation is based on the shared values and principles of transparency and openness, inclusiveness, accountability, data protection and privacy, security, scalability at a global level, and equity. The WHO and the European Commission will work together to encourage maximum global uptake and participation. Particular attention will be paid to equitable opportunities for the participation by those most in need: low and middle-income countries.
translations.hypothesis: This partnership is founded on shared values and principles, including transparency, accountability, data protection, security, scalability, and fairness. The World Health Organization and the European Commission are working together to promote this global partnership. They aim to ensure that low- and middle-income countries have fair opportunities to participate.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በግንቦት 30፣ 2023 የተካሄደው ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ማጠቃለያ። የአለም ጤና ድርጅት አመታዊ ጉባኤ በገንዘብ ድጋፍ እና በተለያዩ የጤና ጉዳዮች ላይ ስምምነት በማድረግ ተጠናቀቀ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 76ኛው የጤና ጉባኤ የሰዎችን ባህሪ የሚያጠና ሳይንስን (behavioral sciences) ጨምሮ በተለያዩ ጉዳዮች ላይ ከተወያየ በኋላ ዛሬ ተጠናቀቀ። ሌሎች የተካተቱት ርዕሰ ጉዳዮችም፦ ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችን ለመቆጣጠር ስልቶች፣ ምርመራዎች፣ የአካል ጉዳቶች፣ በውሃ መስጠምን መከላከል፣ ድንገተኛ እና ወሳኝ እንክብካቤ እና ሌሎችም ይገኙበታል። ጉባኤው በምግብ ውስጥ የሚገኙ ንጥረነገሮች (micro-nutrients)፣ በአገር በቀል ጤና፣ በኢንፌክሽን መከላከልና መቆጣጠር፣ በእናቶችና ህጻናት ጤና፣ በህክምና ኦክሲጅን፣ በመጀመሪያ ደረጃ ጤና አጠባበቅ፣ በስደተኞች ጤና፣
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 30 May 2023 WHO’s annual assembly ends with agreement on funding, and array of health topics WHO’s 76th health assembly closed today, having addressed a vast array of issues, including behavioural sciences; best buys for non-communicable diseases; diagnostics; disabilities; drowning prevention; emergency, critical and operative care; food micronutrients; indigenous health; infection prevention and control; maternal and child health; medical oxygen; primary health care; refugee and migrant health;
translations.hypothesis: The 76th World Health Assembly, held on May 30, 2023, concluded with a focus on global health issues, including pandemic preparedness, health equity, and non-communicable diseases. The 76th World Health Assembly concluded today after discussions on a range of topics, including behavioral sciences, disease surveillance, disability, water safety, emergency and humanitarian health, and other key areas. Other topics discussed included: strategies for controlling non-communicable diseases, diagnostics, disability, water safety, emergency and humanitarian health, and other key areas.

translations.source: የመልሶ ማቋቋም እና የባህላዊ ህክምና ጉዳዮች ላይ ትኩረት ሰጥቷል። በተጨማሪም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ለሌሎች ብዙ ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች የሚሰጠውን ምላሽ እና ዝግጁነትን ለማሻሻል የሚደረገውን ጥረት አጉልቶ አሳይቷል። በዕለቱ ቀደም ብለው የኮሚቴ ኤ (Committee A) ተወካዮች ለዓለም አቀፍ ጤና እና ሰላም ተነሳሽነት ፍኖተ ካርታ እውቅና ሰጥተው ከዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የሂደት ሪፖርት ጠይቀዋል። በምልአተ ጉባኤው ተወካዮች የሁለቱንም ኮሚቴዎች ውሳኔዎችን በማጽደቅ የጉባኤውን ሂደት አጠናቀዋል። ይህ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2024-25 ዓመት በጀት ማጽደቅን እና የተገመገሙ መዋጮዎች በ20 በመቶ መጨመርን ያካትታል። የኮሚቴው ሰብሳቢዎች እና የሁለት ክልሎች ተወካዮች ለዚሁ ምክር ቤት ስራ እና ሂደት እውቅና ሰጥተዋል።
translations.reference: rehabilitation; traditional medicine, and WHO’s work in responding to dozens of emergencies while working with Member States to be better prepared to face new ones. Earlier in the day, delegates in Committee A agreed to note the roadmap towards the Global Health and Peace Initiative (along with a slight change in name from “Global Health for Peace Initiative”), and requested that the Director-General report on progress in strengthening the roadmap. In plenary, as the final approval step of the assembly, delegates adopted the resolutions and decisions of the two committees, and adopted their reports. This included approval of the budget for 2024-25, and a 20% increase in assessed contributions. The committee chairs and representatives from two regions spoke to recognize the work and progress of this Assembly.
translations.hypothesis: The meeting focused on disaster response and traditional medicine. It also highlighted the World Health Organization's efforts to improve its response and preparedness for other emergencies. During the meeting, representatives from Committee A presented a report to the Director-General on the international health and peace initiative. The delegates from both committees then voted on the report. The vote approved the report, as well as the budget for the 2024-25 fiscal year and a 20% increase in allocated funds. The delegates from Committee A and the two regions also commended the committee's work and process.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ በመዝጊያ ንግግራቸው “የተገመገሙ መዋጮዎች መጨመር እና ኢንቨስትመንቶች ታሪካዊ ስኬቶች መሆናቸውን አፅንዖት ሰጥተዋል።” በዚህ አመት በተካሄደው የተባበሩት መንግስታት ጠቅላላ ጉባኤ ላይ በአለም አቀፍ የጤና ሽፋን፣ በሳንባ ነቀርሳ እና በወረርሽኝ መከላከል እና ምላሽ መስጠት ላይ በቅርቡ የሚደረጉ ከፍተኛ ስብሰባዎችን ጠቅሰዋል። ዶ/ር ቴድሮስ በወረርሽኝ ስምምነቱ ላይ እየተካሄደ ያለውን ድርድር እና በአለም አቀፍ የጤና ደንቦች ላይ የተደረጉ ማሻሻያዎችን፤ በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ወቅት የተከሰቱ ስህተቶች እንዳይደገሙ ለመማር ጠቃሚ እድሎች መሆናቸውን ጠቁመዋል። በዚህ አመት 100ኛ ዓመት ልደቷን ካከበረችው የቀድሞ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ሰራተኛ ግዌን ካርኔሊ ጋር ልዑካኑ ተገናኝተዋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከተመሠረተ ከአንድ ዓመት በኋላ እ.ኤ.አ. በ1949 በድርጅቱ መሥራት ጀመረች። የ76ኛው የአለም ጤና ጉባኤ መዝጊያ ቪዲዮውን ይመልከቱ።
translations.reference: In his closing remarks, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO’s Director-General, noted that “the increase in assessed contributions and the investment round are historic and a huge milestone.” He spoke about the year ahead, with high-level meetings on universal health coverage, tuberculosis and pandemic preparedness and response at this year’s UN General Assembly. He pointed to the continuing negotiations on the pandemic accord and amendments to the International Health Regulations as unprecedented—“generational”—opportunities to learn from the mistakes of the COVID-19 pandemic and ensure they are not repeated. Delegates also had the chance to hear from former WHO staff Gwen Carnelley, who turned 100 this year, who began working with WHO in 1949, just a year after WHO was founded 75 years ago. Video of Closing of the 76th World Health Assembly.
translations.hypothesis: In his closing remarks, WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus emphasized the importance of "increased funding and investments in historical achievements." He highlighted recent high-level meetings at the United Nations General Assembly on global health coverage, pandemic preparedness, and response, as well as the upcoming World Health Assembly. Dr. Tedros also noted the ongoing negotiations on the International Health Regulations and the recent revisions to the Pandemic Treaty, emphasizing that these events provide valuable opportunities to learn from the COVID-19 pandemic and prevent future outbreaks. The delegation also met with Gwen Carnell, a former WHO employee who celebrated the organization's 100th anniversary this year. The World Health Organization was founded in 1948, and Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus became its Director-General in 2019. Watch the closing video from the 76th World Health Assembly.

translations.source: ግዌን ካርኔሊን በቪዲዮው 54፡37 ደቂቃ ላይ አስተዋወቋት እና ብዙም ሳይቆይ ንግግር አደረገች። ከነገው እለት ጀምሮ 153ኛው የስራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርድ ስብሰባ ይጀመራል ፣ የጉባዔው ውጤት እና ሌሎችም ርዕሰ ጉዳዮች ውይይት የሚደረግባቸው ይሆናል።
translations.reference: Gwen Carnelley is introduced at 54:37 and speaks shortly afterwards. Starting tomorrow, the 153rd meeting of Executive Board begins, where the outcomes of the Assembly will be discussed, among other things.
translations.hypothesis: I introduced Gwen Carnell in the video at 54:37 minutes, and she quickly began speaking. The 153rd meeting of the executive board will begin tomorrow, and discussions will be held on the meeting's outcomes and other important issues.

translations.source: ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ስብሰባ - ዕለታዊ ዜና፡ እ.ኤ.አ. ግንቦት 29፣ 2023 በዓለም ዙሪያ የአገሬው ተወላጆችን (Indigenous people) ጤና ለማሻሻል ታሪካዊ ውሳኔ የአለም ጤና ጥበቃ ጉባኤ ዛሬ የአገሬው ተወላጆችን ጤና የሚመለከት ታሪካዊ ውሳኔ አስተላለፈ። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡም፤ ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ለአገሬው ተወላጆች ጤና ዓለም አቀፍ የድርጊት መርሃ ግብር እንዲያዘጋጁ እና እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2026 በሰባ ዘጠነኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ላይ እንዲያቀርቡ ይጠይቃል። የአገሬው ተወላጆች፤ የተለያዩ የህዝብ ቡድኖችን እና ማህበረሰቦችን ያቀፉ ቢሆኑም በአጠቃላይ የመኖር ዕድላቸው የአገሬው ተወላጅ ካልሆኑ ሰዎች ጋር ሲታይ በእጅጉ ያነሰ ነው። እንደ ስኳር በሽታ፣ የእናቶች እና የጨቅላ ህጻናት ሞት እና የተመጣጠነ ምግብ እጥረት ያሉ በርካታ በሽታዎች እና የጤና ችግሮች በከፍተኛ ደረጃ ላይ ያጠቃቸዋል።
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 29 May 2023 Landmark resolution for strengthening Indigenous Peoples’ health globally The World Health Assembly today approved an unprecedented resolution on the health of Indigenous Peoples, which requests the Director-General to develop a global action plan for the health of indigenous peoples and to present it to the Seventy-ninth World Health Assembly in 2026. Indigenous Peoples, although representing diverse population groups and communities, in general have considerably lower life expectancy than non-indigenous populations. They also have a higher prevalence of many diseases and adverse health conditions, including diabetes, maternal and infant mortality and malnutrition.
translations.hypothesis: Sixty-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily News: May 29, 2023 – The World Health Assembly today made a historic decision to improve the health of indigenous peoples around the world. The decision, which calls for the Director-General to develop a global action plan for the health of indigenous peoples and to present it at the seventy-seventh World Health Assembly in 2026, is a landmark achievement. Indigenous peoples, despite their diverse cultures and communities, face significant health challenges. They are disproportionately affected by diseases such as diabetes, maternal and child mortality, and malnutrition.

translations.source: ጉባኤው የድርጊት መርሃ ግብሩ ከተወላጆች ጋር በመመካከር እንዲዘጋጅ ጠይቋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አስፈላጊ ሆኖ በተገኘበት ጊዜ የአገሬው ተወላጆችን ጤና ለማሻሻል፤ ለአባል ሀገራት ድጋፍ የመስጠት ኃላፊነት ተሰጥቶታል። አስራ አራተኛው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አጠቃላይ የሥራ መርሃ ግብር በሚዘጋጅበት ወቅት በውስጡ የአገሬው ተወላጆችን ጤና ማሻሻል ይካተታል ። በተጨማሪም፣ የጤና ጉባኤው አባል ሀገራት፣ ከሌሎች ተግባራት መካከል፣ ስለ የአገሬው ተወላጆች የጤና ሁኔታ መረጃ ሲሰበስቡ በነጻ፣ አስቀድመው ጠይቀው እና በፍቃደኝነታቸው ላይ የተመሰረተ እንዲሆን አሳስቧል። አባል ሀገራት ብሄራዊ የጤና ዕቅዶችን፣ ስልቶችን ወይም ሌሎች እርምጃዎችን ለተወላጆች እንዲፈጥሩ፣ ገንዘብ እንዲለግሱ እና እንዲተገብሩ ይበረታታሉ።
translations.reference: The Assembly requested the action plan be developed in consultation with Indigenous Peoples; that WHO provide support to Member States, upon request, for improving indigenous health; and that the improvement of Indigenous Peoples’ health be included in the development of the Fourteenth WHO General Programme of Work. In the same resolution, the Health Assembly urged Member States to, among other tasks, develop knowledge about the health situation of Indigenous Peoples, with their free, prior and informed consent; develop, fund and implement national health plans, strategies or other measures for Indigenous Peoples;
translations.hypothesis: The meeting called for the development of an action plan in consultation with local residents. The World Health Organization was tasked with improving the health of its member states, and specifically, the health of the local population, when it deemed it necessary. During the preparation of the 14th World Health Assembly, the health of the local population will be a key focus. Furthermore, the health committee urged member states to collect data on the health of the local population, and to do so in a free and voluntary manner. Member states are encouraged to develop, fund, and implement national health plans, strategies, or other measures to benefit the local population.

translations.source: አባል ሀገራት ተወላጆች ያላቸውን ባህላዊ እውቀት እና ተግባር ከግምት ውስጥ በማስገባት፤ በጤና ሰራተኛነት ምልመላ፣ ስልጠና እና በስራ እንዲቆዩ እንዲበረታቱ አሳስበዋል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ ፦ A76 / A / CONF./1 “የሃገሩ ተወላጆች ጤና” ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ የመስጠምን መከላከል ጥረቶችን ለማፋጠን የመጀመሪያውን ውሳኔ አሳለፈ። አባል ሀገራት ብሄራዊ የመስጠም ሁኔታቸውን በመገምገም ሁሉን አቀፍና ዘርፈ ብዙ የመስጠምን መከላከል መርሃ ግብሮች እንዲተገብሩ አሳስበዋል። የውሃ መስጠም የ236,000 ሰዎች ህይወትን በየዓመቱ ይቀጥፋል።
translations.reference: encourage the attraction, training, recruitment and retention of Indigenous Peoples as health workers taking into account the traditional knowledge and practices. Related document: A76/A/CONF./1 - Health of Indigenous Peoples First-ever resolution to accelerate action on drowning preventionThe delegates at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly agreed a resolution to accelerate action on global drowning prevention. The resolution requests Member States to assess their national drowning situation and to develop and implement multisectoral drowning prevention programmes. Drowning causes 236 000 deaths every year.
translations.hypothesis: Taking into account the traditional knowledge and practices of the indigenous populations in member states, the WHO encourages member states to integrate this knowledge into healthcare, training, and workforce development programs. Relevant document: A76/A/CONF.1 "Health of Indigenous Peoples" The 66th World Health Assembly adopted a resolution to accelerate global efforts to prevent and control communicable diseases. The resolution encourages member states to develop comprehensive and multi-sectoral programs to address communicable diseases, taking into account the specific national contexts. Waterborne diseases claim the lives of 236,000 people each year.

translations.source: በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ከጉዳት ጋር በተያያዘ የህጻናትን ሞት ከሚያስከትሉ ሁኔታዎች ግንባር ቀደም መንስኤ ነው። ባለፉት አስርት አመታት 2.5 ሚሊዮን ሰዎች በውሃ ሰጥመው ህይወታቸውን ያጡ ሲሆን ከእነዚህ ውስጥ ከ90% በላይ የሚሆኑት የሞት አደጋዎች የተከሰቱት ዝቅተኛ እና መካከለኛ ገቢ ባላቸው ሀገራት ነው። በተባበሩት መንግስታት ድርጅት (ተመድ) ጠቅላላ ጉባኤ ግብዣ መሰረት፤ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመስጠም መከላከል ስራዎችን ያስተባብራል እናም በየዓመቱ ሃምሌ 25 የአለም የውሃ መስጠም መከላከል ቀንን ያከብራል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከተባበሩት መንግስታት ድርጅት፣ ከአለም አቀፍ የልማት አጋሮች እና መንግሥታዊ ካልሆኑ ድርጅቶች ጋር በመተባበር ዓለም አቀፍ የውሃ መስጠም አደጋን የመከላከል ህብረትን (Global Alliance for Drowning Prevention) ያቋቁማል። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የውሃ መስጠምን መከላከልን በተመለከተ አለም አቀፍ ሪፖርት እንዲያዘጋጅም ያሳስባል።
translations.reference: It is a leading global cause of injury-related child deaths. Over the past decade, 2.5 million people died from drowning, and over 90% of those occurred in low- and middle-income countries. At the invitation of the United Nations (UN) General Assembly, WHO will coordinate actions within the UN system on drowning prevention and facilitate the observance of World Drowning Prevention Day on 25 July each year. WHO will also set up a Global Alliance for Drowning Prevention with organizations of the UN system, international development partners and nongovernmental organizations. To better understand the true burden and impact of drowning, the resolution further requests WHO to prepare a global status report on drowning prevention.
translations.hypothesis: Drowning is a leading cause of death among children worldwide. Over the past two decades, more than 2.5 million children have died from drowning, with the vast majority of these deaths occurring in low- and middle-income countries. According to a call to action from the United Nations, the World Health Organization (WHO) leads efforts to prevent drowning, and the WHO observes World Drowning Prevention Day on November 25 each year. The WHO, in collaboration with the United Nations, international development partners, and non-governmental organizations, has established the Global Alliance for Drowning Prevention (GLAP). The alliance aims to promote the WHO's drowning prevention work and to produce a global report on drowning prevention.

translations.source: ተዛማጅ ሰነድ: EB152/CONF./2 - በአለምአቀፍ የውሃ መስጠም መከላከል ላይ እርምጃን ማፋጠን ስለ መፍትሄው የበለጠ ይረዱ። የአለም የውሃ መስጠም መከላከል ቀን እ.ኤ.አ. 2023። አባል ሀገራት ኬሚካሎችን፣ ብክነትን እና የአካባቢ ብክለትን ለመከላከል የውሳኔ ሃሳቡን በደስታ ተቀብለዋል። እነዚህ ጥረቶች ራስን ከማጥፋት ጋር የተያያዙ ሞትን፤ በተለይም በአደገኛ ፀረ ተባይ መድሃኒቶች ምክንያት የሚሞቱትን ሞት እስከ አንድ አምስተኛ ለመቀነስ ያለመ ነው።
translations.reference: Related document: EB152/CONF./2 - Accelerating action on global drowning prevention Read more about the resolution World Drowning Prevention Day 2023 Member States mobilize behind resolution to tackle chemicals, waste and pollution Member States welcomed the resolution addressing environmental determinants, including management of chemicals and waste. The efforts can help prevent up to one fifth of all suicide-related deaths from highly hazardous pesticides.
translations.hypothesis: Related document: EB152/CONF./2 - Learn more about accelerating action to combat global water contamination. World Water Contamination Day, 2023. Member states have welcomed the proposal to combat chemicals, pollution, and environmental contamination. These efforts are aimed at reducing deaths from self-harm, particularly those caused by dangerous anti-depressant medications, by as much as one-fifth.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት፤ የጤና ሴክተሩን ቁልፍ ሚና የሚገልጸውን የኬሚካል ፍኖተ ካርታ ጨምሮ፤ አባል ሀገራት ያላቸውን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ስትራቴጂዎች ትግበራ እንዲያጠናክሩ ይበረታታሉ። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ የጤና ሚኒስቴሮች፤ መንግስታት የሳይንስ ፖሊሲን ለማቅረብ በሚያደርጉት ጥረት ላይ እንዲሳተፉ እና የፕላስቲክ ብክለትን ለማስቆም በሚደረግ ስምምነት ላይ እንዲደራደሩ አሳስቧል። ውሳኔው ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የሚከተሉትን እንዲያደርጉ ጠይቋል። ኬሚካሎች፣ ብክነት እና ብክለት እንዴት ጤናን እንደሚጎዱ ከ"አንድ ጤና" (“One Health”) እይታ አንጻር ሪፖርት እንዲያሳትሙ። ከተባበሩት መንግስታት የአካባቢ ጥበቃ ፕሮግራም ጋር በመተባበር፤ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2012 የወጣውን ሰነድ፤ ይህም ስለ “Endocrine”ን (በደም ውስጥ የሚዘዋወሩ ሆርሞኖችን የሚያመርቱ እና የሚያሰራጩ የአካል ክፍሎችን የሚቆጣጠር የሰውነት ስርዓት) የሚጎዱ ኬሚካሎች ማዘመን እንዲሁም
translations.reference: Member States were urged to bolster the implementation of existing WHO strategies including implementation of the WHO Chemicals Roadmap which outlines key roles of the health sector in implementing the Strategic Approach to International Chemicals Management. The resolution also encourages the Ministries of Health to engage in efforts to prepare proposals for an intergovernmental science policy panel and negotiations for a treaty to end plastic pollution. The resolution called upon the Director-General to undertake several actions, including: publishing a report on the health implications of chemicals, waste and pollution from a "One Health" perspective; updating the 2012 document on the State of the Science of Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals jointly with the United Nations Environment Programme; and
translations.hypothesis:
The World Health Organization (WHO) encourages its member states to strengthen their national health strategies, including the use of a chemical risk assessment tool, to better protect public health. The decision calls on health ministers to participate in efforts to develop national science policies and to engage in negotiations on an international agreement to reduce plastic pollution. The decision also directs the Director-General to:

*   Report on the health impacts of chemicals, pollution, and waste from a "One Health" perspective.
*   Work with the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) to update the 2012 document on chemicals that affect the endocrine system (the body's system of organs that produce and regulate hormones).

translations.source: በብሔራዊ ወይም በክልል ደረጃ ለሰው ልጅ ጤና አሳሳቢ የሆኑ ኬሚካሎችን ለመቆጣጠር አገሮችን መርዳት ናቸው። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ፡ A76/A/CONF./2 - የኬሚካል፣ ብክነት እና ብክለት በሰው ጤና ላይ የሚያሳድረው ተጽዕኖ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በኬሚካል ደህንነት እና የጤና ደህንነት ላይ የሚሰራው ስራ፡- ሁሉም ዜጎች ሙሉ አካላዊ እና አእምሯዊ ጤናቸውን በህይወት ዘመናቸው እና በትውልዳቸው ሁሉ እንዲያሳኩ ለማስቻል ያለመውን "የጤና ማስተዋወቅ ዘዴን በመጠቀም ደህንነትን ከህብረተሰብ ጤና ጋር ለማዋሃድ አለምአቀፍ መዋቅር"ን ለመቀበል አባል ሀገራት ተስማምተዋል። የአለም አቀፍ መዋቅሩ ትኩረት ሊሰጣቸው የሚገቡ ስድስት ቁልፍ ስትራቴጂካዊ አቅጣጫዎችን ይመክራል፡- ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን፣ ፍትሃዊ ኢኮኖሚ፣ አለማችንን መጠበቅ፣ የማህበራዊ ጥበቃ ስርዓቶች፣ የጤና ዲጂታል ስርዓቶች እና ደህንነትን መለካት እና መከታተል ናቸው።
translations.reference: supporting countries in developing national or regional human biomonitoring programmes for chemicals of concern. Related document: A76/A/CONF./2 - The impact of chemicals, waste and pollution on human health WHO’s work on chemical safety and health Achieving well-being: a global frameworkMember States agreed to adopt the "Global framework for integrating well-being into public health utilizing a health promotion approach", which strives to enable all people to flourish and achieve their full physical and mental health potential throughout their lives and across generations. The Global framework recommends six key strategic directions that focus on: universal health coverage, equitable economies, protecting the planet, social protection systems, digital systems to enable health, and measuring and monitoring well-being.
translations.hypothesis: Countries play a crucial role in helping to regulate chemicals that pose a threat to human health, both at the national and regional levels. Relevant document: A76/A/CONF./2 - The Impact of Chemicals, Pollution and Contamination on Human Health. The World Health Organization (WHO) is working on chemical safety and health protection, and has adopted a "global framework" to integrate health with societal well-being, using a "health promotion strategy" to ensure that all citizens can achieve optimal physical and mental health throughout their lives. The framework recommends six key strategic priorities: universal health coverage, a fair economy, protecting the environment, social protection systems, digital health systems, and monitoring and evaluating safety.

translations.source: ጤናማነትን ለማስተዋወቅ እና ጤናን ለመጠበቅ፤ ከጤና ውጭ ካሉ ሴክተሮች ጋር የቅርብ ትብብር ማድረግን መዋቅሩ ይጠቁማል። ሁሉም ባለድርሻ አካላት ወደ አንድ የጋራ ግብ፤ በአንድነት እና በትብብር እንዲሳተፉ መመሪያ ሆኖ ይሰራል። ጤናን ለሰዎች እና ለአለም ዘላቂ እና ፍትሃዊ በሆነ መንገድ ለማሳደግ ይጠቅማል። ለሰዎች ሁሉ እና ለዓለማችን ጤናን በዘላቂነት እና በፍትሃዊነት ለማሳደግ ያለመ ነው። ጉባኤው እ.ኤ.አ. በ2024፣ 2026 እና 2031 የአለምአቀፍ መዋቅሩን አፈፃፀም ሪፖርት እንዲያቀርብ ለዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ጠይቋል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ: A76/7 Add.2 - ደህንነት እና ጤናን ማስተዋወቅ (ሰዎች የጤና ሁኔታቸውን እንዲቆጣጠሩ እና ጤንነታቸውን እንዲያሻሽሉ የማድረግ ሂደት።)
translations.reference: The framework proposes close collaboration with sectors outside the health sector to promote and protect health. It serves as a guide for all stakeholders to engage in a coherent and coordinated manner around a common purpose: promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. The Assembly requests the Director-General to report on implementation of the global framework in 2024, 2026 and 2031. Related document: A76/7 Add.2 - Well-being and health promotion
translations.hypothesis: It emphasizes the importance of close collaboration with non-health sectors to promote health and protect well-being. It serves as a guiding principle for all stakeholders to work together towards a common goal, ultimately leading to improved health for people and the world. The committee has requested the Director-General to submit a report on the implementation of the Global Strategy for Health and Development in 2024, 2026, and 2031. Relevant document: A76/7 Add.2 - Promoting Health and Well-being (The process of enabling individuals to monitor and improve their health status).

translations.source: ምግብን በጥቃቅን ንጥረ-ነገሮች ማበልጸግ ላይ ጥረቶችን ለማፋጠን አዲስ መፍትሄ። ልዑካኑ የምግብ ጥቃቅን ንጥረ-ነገር እጥረቶችን (micronutrient deficiencies) በአስተማማኝ እና ውጤታማ በሆነ ምግብን በማበልጸግ ለመከላከል የሚደረገውን ጥረት ለማፋጠን ያለመ አዲስ የውሳኔ ሃሳብ አጽድቀዋል። በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ፤ ሃምሳ በመቶ የሚሆኑ እድሜያቸው ከ5 ዓመት በታች የሆኑ ልጆች እንዲሁም ሁለት ሶስተኛ የሚሆኑ እድሜያቸው ከ15 እስከ 49 የሆኑ ሴቶች እንደ ፎሌት (folate)፣ አይረን (Iron)፣ ቫይታሚን ኤ (vitamin A) እና ዚንክ (Zinc) ባሉ ጠቃሚ ንጥረ ነገሮች እጥረት ይሰቃያሉ። እነዚህ የንጥረነገር እጥረቶች እንደ spina bifida (ከውልደት ጀምሮ የሚከሰት የነርቭ በሽታ) እና ሌሎች የነርቭ ቱቦ ጉድለቶች ያሉ ከባድ የጤና ችግሮችን ሊያስከትሉ ይችላሉ። እነዚህን ችግሮች ለመፍታት መጠነ ሰፊ የምግብ ማበልጸግ እንደ የመፍትሄ አካል ሆኖ ይታያል። ሃገራቱ እንደየሃገር አቀፍ የፍጆታ ዘይቤ እና የንጥረነረሮች እጥረት መሰረት፤ እንደ ስንዴ እና የበቆሎ ዱቄት፣ ሩዝ፣ የምግብ ዘይት እና ጨው የመሳሰሉት ዋና ዋና ምግቦች እንዲሁም ማጣፈጫዎች ላይ አስፈላጊ የሆኑትን ቫታሚኖች እና ማዕድኖች በመጨመር የሚታየውን የጥቃቅን ንጥረነገሮች እጥረት ማስተካከል እና የበለጠ መከላከል ይችላሉ።
translations.reference: New resolution to accelerate efforts on food micronutrient fortification The delegates approved a resolution on accelerating efforts to prevent micronutrient deficiencies through safe and effective food fortification. Deficiencies in vitamin and mineral status, particularly of folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc, affect 50% of all preschool aged children and 67% of all women of reproductive age worldwide. Micronutrient deficiencies can have serious consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects.Large scale food fortification is part of the solution. By adding essential vitamins and minerals to staple foods and condiments, such as wheat and maize flours, rice, cooking oil and salt in accordance with national consumption patterns and deficiencies, countries can correct and further prevent a demonstrated micronutrient deficiency.
translations.hypothesis: A new solution to accelerate efforts to fortify food with essential micronutrients. This innovative approach focuses on rapidly improving the fortification of food with essential micronutrients to combat micronutrient deficiencies in a reliable and effective manner. Globally, approximately 50% of children under the age of 5 and two-thirds of women aged 15-49 suffer from deficiencies in essential nutrients such as folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc. These deficiencies can lead to serious health problems, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects. Food fortification is seen as a key solution to address these issues. By incorporating essential vitamins and minerals into staple foods such as rice, wheat flour, cooking oil, and beverages, countries can effectively address and prevent micronutrient deficiencies, taking into account local dietary habits and nutrient needs.

translations.source: አገሮች በምግብ ማበልጸግ ላይ ፖሊሲዎችን እንዲያዘጋጁ እና የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እና የክትትል ስርዓቶችን የሚያጠናክሩበትን መንገድ እንዲያስቡ ይበረታታሉ። ይህ የውሳኔ ሃሳብ ከተባበሩት መንግስታት የአስር ዓመት የተመጣጠነ ምግብ የማቅረብ ውሳኔ (United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition) (እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2016 እስክ 2025) ጋር ይጣጣማል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ፡ EB152/CONF./5 የጥቃቅን ንጥረነገሮችን እጥረትና የሚያመጡትን ችግሮች ለምሳሌ የነርቭ ጉድለቶችን ለመከላከል፤ አስተማማኝ እና ውጤታማ የምግብ ማበልጸጊያዎችን በመጠቀም የሚደረገውን ጥረት ማፋጠን። ተዛማጅ አገናኞች (Related links)
translations.reference: The resolution urges Member States to develop policies on food fortification with micronutrients and/or supplementation, and to consider ways of strengthening financing and monitoring mechanisms. The resolution was agreed under the umbrella of the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025) report. Related document: EB152/CONF./5 Accelerating efforts for preventing micronutrient deficiencies and their consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects, through safe and effective food fortification Related links
translations.hypothesis: The countries are encouraged to develop policies and strengthen systems for food security, as well as to provide financial and technical support. This aligns with the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025), which aims to achieve a balanced and nutritious food supply for all. Related document: EB152/CONF./5. This document focuses on accelerating efforts to combat malnutrition, including neurological disorders, by utilizing reliable and effective food fortification strategies. Related links

translations.source: ስለ መፍትሄው የበለጠ ያንብቡ። ቫይታሚኖችን እና ማዕድኖችን በምግብ ላይ ለመጨመር የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እየሰራ ያለው ስራ። ሀገራት ሀሰተኛ እና ጥራት የሌላቸው መድሃኒቶችን ማስቆም በሚቻልበት መንገድ ላይ ተስማምተዋል። በግንቦት 27፣ ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ሃሰተኛ እና ጥራት የሌላቸው መድሃኒቶችን ለመቆጣጠር “ሜምበር ስቴት መካኒዝም” (Member State Mechanism (MSM)) ለመገምገም ተስማምቷል። ይህ እ.ኤ.አ. በጥር 2023 የተደረገውን የዋና ዳይሬክተሩን ጥሪ ይደግፋል። ግምገማው እ.ኤ.አ. በ2023 በሚቀጥለው የ“ሜምበር ስቴት መካኒዝም” (Member State Mechanism (MSM)) መሪ ኮሚቴ ስብሰባ ይጀምራል።
translations.reference: Read more about the resolution WHO's work on food fortification Member States approve actions to counter substandard and falsified medicines On 27 May, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly approved a resolution to conduct an independent review of the Member State Mechanism (MSM) for incidents, substandard and falsified medicines, reinforcing the Director-General’s January 2023 call for increased vigilance and action in this important area. The review will be initiated at the next MSM steering committee meeting later in 2023.
translations.hypothesis: Read more about the solution. The World Health Organization is working to increase the amount of vitamins and minerals in food. Countries have agreed on a way to stop the sale of counterfeit and substandard medicines. On May 27, the 76th World Health Assembly adopted a resolution to establish a "Member State Mechanism" (MSM) to monitor and combat counterfeit and substandard medicines. This supports the call made by the Director-General in January 2023. The assessment will begin at the next "Member State Mechanism" (MSM) steering committee meeting in 2023.

translations.source: የ“ሜምበር ስቴት መካኒዝም” (Member State Mechanism (MSM)) እ.ኤ.አ. በ2012 የተቋቋመው፤ ሃሰተኛ እና ጥራት የሌላቸውን የህክምና ምርቶች፤ ፍትሃዊ እና ግልጥ በሆነ መንገድ ለመቋቋም ነው። አላማውም የህብረተሰብን ጤና መጠበቅ እና በተመጣጣኝ ዋጋ፣ ጥራት ያላቸውን የህክምና ምርቶች ተደራሽ ማድረግ ነው። አባል ሀገራቱ ደህንነታቸውን ያልተጠበቁ እና ሀሰተኛ የሆኑ የህክምና ምርቶችን የማስቆም፣ የመለየት እና ምላሽ የመስጠት አቅማቸውን ለማጠናከር ድጋፍ አስፈላጊ መሆኑን ጠቁመዋል። በተበከሉ መድኃኒቶች ምክንያት የሚደርሱ አሳዛኝ ሁኔታዎችን ለመከላከል እንደ የላብራቶሪ ምርመራዎችን ማጠናከር እና መደበኛ ያልሆኑ ገበያዎች እንዲሁም የመስመር ላይ ሽያጮችን ጠንከር ባለ መንገድ መቆጣጠር ያስፈልጋል። አባል ሀገራቱ እነዚህን ገበያዎች ለመቆጣጠር የክትትል ቴክኖሎጂዎችን መቀበል እና መተግበር እንዳለባቸው አሳስበዋል፤ በተጨማሪም እንደ ህግ አስከባሪ እና የጉምሩክ ኤጀንሲዎች ካሉ የሀገር ውስጥ እና አለምአቀፍ አጋሮች ጋር ትብብር እንዲኖር ጠይቀዋል ። አባል ሀገራቱ ህጎችን ለማሻሻል የሚደረጉ ስምምነቶችን እንዲያከብሩ እንዲሁም ሀሰተኛ እና ደህንነታቸው ያልተጠበቁ የህክምና ምርቶች ላይ ጥብቅ ቅጣቶችን እንዲያጠናክሩ አሳስበዋል።
translations.reference: The Assembly established the MSM in 2012 to tackle substandard and falsified medical products from a public health perspective and in a transparent and inclusive way. The goal of the MSM is to protect public health and promote access to affordable, safe, efficacious and quality medical products. Noting recent events of contaminated medicines found on various markets and related preventable deaths, Member States emphasized the support needed to improve their capacity to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products through access to appropriate technologies, including laboratory testing facilities, along with improved oversight on informal markets and advertising, and the online sale of medical products. Member States called for embracing and implementing track and trace technologies for market control and surveillance, as well as the need for formal collaboration by relevant local and global partners such as law enforcement and customs administrations. Member States were encouraged to align with conventions aimed at improvement of legislation and imposition of prohibitive sanctions for wrongful dealing in substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis: Established in 2012, the "Member State Mechanism" (MSM) is designed to combat the circulation of counterfeit and substandard medical products. Its goal is to protect public health and ensure that people have access to safe, high-quality medical products at affordable prices. The member states emphasized the importance of strengthening their capacity to detect, prevent, and respond to the threat of counterfeit medical products. This includes enhancing laboratory testing capabilities, strengthening controls on informal markets, and effectively regulating online sales. The member states encouraged the adoption and implementation of monitoring technologies to control these markets, as well as collaboration with domestic and international partners such as law enforcement and customs agencies. They also urged member states to strengthen their legal frameworks and impose stricter penalties on those involved in the production and distribution of counterfeit and substandard medical products.

translations.source: ሴክሬተሪያቱ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2025 የስራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርዱን የማዘመን ኃላፊነት ተሰጥቶታል። ተዛማጅ ሰነድ፡ EB152/9 - ደህንነታቸው ያልተጠበቁ የህክምና ምርቶች ተዛማጅ አገናኞች (Related links) የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት አደጋዎችን፣ ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች መቆጣጠሪያ መንገድ፤ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ እና የተጭበረበሩ የህክምና ምርቶች ላይ እየሰራ ነው።
translations.reference: The Secretariat is requested to report on progress in this area to the Executive Board in 2025. Related document: EB152/9 - Substandard and falsified medical products Related links WHO Member State Mechanism on incidents, substandard and falsified medical products WHO's work on substandard and falsified medical products
translations.hypothesis: The Secretary was assigned the responsibility of updating the Board of Directors in 2025. Related document: EB152/9 - Control of substandard medical products. Related links: The World Health Organization (WHO) is working with member states to control substandard and counterfeit medical products.

translations.source: ለተሻለ ጤና ሲባል ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ በባህሪ ሳይንስ (Behavioral Science) ላይ ውሳኔ ተወሰደ። ዛሬ አባል ሀገራቱ የባህሪ ሳይንስ ንድፈ ሃሳቦችን እና ዘዴዎችን ከጤና ርእሶች እና የህዝብ ጤና ስልቶች ጋር ማቀናጀትን የሚያስተዋውቅ ውሳኔ አሳለፉ። የውሳኔ ሃሳቡ አባል ሀገራት የጤና ውጤቶችን በማሻሻል ሂደት ውስጥ የባህሪ ሳይንስን አስፈላጊነት እንዲገነዘቡ እንዲሁም የባህሪ ሳይንስ ማስረጃዎችን በመጠቀም ፖሊሲዎችንና ፕሮግራሞችን የሚያግዙ ቡድኖች እንዲያዋቅሩ አሳስቦል። በተጨማሪም ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ የባህሪ ሳይንስን በአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስራ ውስጥ እንዲያካትቱ እና ለአባል ሀገራት መመሪያ እና ድጋፍ እንዲሰጡ ጥሪውን ያቀርባል። አባል ሀገራቱ በሴክሬታሪያት የሚመራውን “የባህሪ ሳይንስ ለተሻለ ጤና” ተነሳሽነት ስኬት እውቅና ሰተዋል፤ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ላደረገችው ማሌዢያ እንዲሁም ሌሎች የተቀላቀሉ 19 ሃገራትን እና ለዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ምስጋናቸውን አቅርበዋል ።
translations.reference: First ever resolution on behavioural sciences for better health is adopted Today Member States adopted the behavioural sciences for better health resolution with Member States showing broad consensus towards the need for integrating systematically behavioural science theory, methods and approaches across health topics and public health functions. The resolution urges Member States to acknowledge the role of behavioural science in achieving better health outcomes, to identify opportunities for increased use and, to establish functions and units for generating and translating evidence to inform policies and programmes. It also requests the Director-General to mainstream the use of behavioural science within the organization and to provide support to Member States through the development of guidance and the provision of technical assistance. Member States recognized the achievement of the Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative led by the secretariat, congratulated Malaysia, sponsor of the resolution, as well as the other 19 countries that joined as co-sponsors, and thanked the Director-General for the report.
translations.hypothesis: For the first time, behavioral science has been explicitly recognized as a key factor in promoting better health. Today, member states have adopted a resolution to integrate behavioral science principles and methods into health topics and public health strategies. The resolution encourages member states to recognize the importance of behavioral science in improving health outcomes and to establish teams that can use behavioral science evidence to support policies and programs. Furthermore, the Director-General calls on member states to incorporate behavioral science into the work of the World Health Organization and to provide guidance and support to member states in this area. Member states have praised the success of the "Behavioral Science for Better Health" initiative, led by the Secretariat, and have expressed their gratitude to Malaysia and other participating countries, as well as to the Director-General.

translations.source: በዚሁ ዘርፍ በተለይም በክልል መሥሪያ ቤቶች ክህሎትን ማሳደግ እንዲሁም የመረጃ ቋትን በማቋቋም በተለያዩ ዘርፎች ማለትም በትምህርት እና በግሉ ሴክተር ላይ ትብብር ማድረግ እንደሚያስፈልግ አስገንዝበዋል። ተዛማጅ ሰነዶች (Related documents) : EB152 (23) - ለተሻሻለ ጤና የባህሪ ሳይንስ EB152/25 ፦ ለተሻለ ጤና የባህሪ ሳይንስ፣ የዋናው ዳይሬክተር ሪፖርት “የባህሪ ሳይንስ ለተሻለ ጤና” ተነሳሽነትን በተመለከተ የተጠቀሱትን ጉዳዮች በሰነድ A76/7 ራዕ.1 ላይ ተዘርዝረዋል። - የጄኔራል ዳይሬክተር አጠቃላይ ሪፖርት ቀርበዋል።
translations.reference: They stressed the importance of building capacity in this area, particularly in regional offices, and of creating a repository of evidence and synergies between sectors, including with academia and the private sector. Related documents: EB152(23) - Behavioural sciences for better health EB152/25 - Behavioural sciences for better health, Report by the Director-General Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative The above items were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General.
translations.hypothesis: They have recognized the need to enhance skills within regional offices, particularly in areas such as public health and education, and to establish information systems across various sectors. Related documents: EB152 (23) - Enhancing Public Health through Behavioral Science; EB152/25: Enhancing Public Health through Behavioral Science. The main director's report, "Behavioral Science for Better Health," details the issues mentioned in document A76/7, Vision 1. The General Director's overall report is also available.

translations.source: ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ስብሰባ - ዕለታዊ መረጃ፡ እ.ኤ.አ. ግንቦት 27፣ 2023 በኢንፌክሽን (ሰውነት ጎጂ በሆኑ ረቂቅ ተዋህሲያን መጠቃት) መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር ላይ የመጀመሪያው ዓለም አቀፍ ስልት፦ የዓለም ጤና ጥበቃ ጉባኤ ወደ ሁለት አስርት ዓመታት በሚጠጋው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና አጋሮቹ መሪነት ጥረት የተዘጋጀውን የመጀመሪያውን ዓለም አቀፍ የኢንፌክሽን መከላከል እና ቁጥጥር ስልት ዛሬ አጽድቋል። ይህ ስትራቴጂ ፀረ ተሕዋስያን መድሃኒቶችን የሚቋቋሙትን ጨምሮ ከጤና አጠባበቅ ጋር የተገናኙ ኢንፌክሽኖችን (health care-associated infections (HAIs)) አደጋ በከፍተኛ ሁኔታ ለመቀነስ ለአባላት ሀገራት ስትራቴጂካዊ መመሪያ ይሰጣል። ከጤና አጠባበቅ ጋር የተገናኙ ኢንፌክሽኖች (HAI) በጤና እንክብካቤ አሰጣጥ ወቅት በጣም ከተለመዱት አሉታዊ ክስተቶች መካከል አንዱ ነው። ኮቪድ-19 እና በቅርብ ጊዜ የተከሰቱ ወረርሽኞች እንደ ኢቦላ ፣ የመካከለኛው ምስራቅ የመተንፈሻ አካላት በሽታ (the Middle East respiratory syndrome) እና የሱዳን ቫይረስ በሽታዎች በዓለም ዙሪያ በኢንፌክሽን መከላከል ፕሮግራሞች ላይ ያሉ ክፍተቶችን በግልፅ አሳይተዋል።
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 27 May 2023 First global strategy on infection prevention and controlThe World Health Assembly agreed today on the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), which builds on almost two decades of efforts led by WHO and partners. The strategy provides Member States with strategic directions to substantially reduce the ongoing risk of health care-associated infections (HAIs), including those that exhibit antimicrobial resistance. HAIs are among the most frequent adverse events occurring in the context of health service delivery. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent major disease outbreaks such as Ebola virus disease, the Middle East respiratory syndrome and the Sudan virus disease have clearly exposed the existing gaps in IPC programmes in all countries.
translations.hypothesis: Sixty-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily Briefing: June 27, 2023 – Launch of the First Global Strategy on Preventing and Controlling Infections: The World Health Organization (WHO) and its partners today launched the first global strategy on preventing and controlling infections, a landmark initiative designed to address the growing threat of health care-associated infections (HAIs). This strategy provides member states with strategic guidance to significantly reduce the risk of HAIs, including those caused by antimicrobial-resistant bacteria. HAIs are one of the most common adverse events in health care settings. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent outbreaks of diseases such as Ebola, Middle East respiratory syndrome (MERS), and Sudanese virus have highlighted the gaps in global infection control programs.
