All share a common goal: to detect and respond to disease threats before they become epidemics and pandemics, and to optimize routine disease surveillance. The goal of this new network is ambitious, but it can also play a vital role in health security: to give every country access to pathogen genomic sequencing and analytics as part of its public health system,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “As was so clearly demonstrated to us during the COVID-19 pandemic, the world is stronger when it stands together to fight shared health threats.”
translations.hypothesis: Scientists and public health officials can use this data to develop medicines and vaccines that can be used to prevent and respond to outbreaks. The World Health Organization is working with governments, non-governmental organizations, the private sector, and other stakeholders to develop a global surveillance system for outbreaks. All of these stakeholders share a common goal: to detect and respond to outbreaks before they occur, and to strengthen routine disease surveillance. "This new partnership has the potential to play a vital role in protecting public health," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization. "As we have seen with the COVID-19 pandemic, when we work together, we can be stronger."

translations.source: ኮቪድ-19 በሽታ አምጪ ዘረመልን ማጥናት ለወረርሽኝ ምላሽ መስጠት ያለውን ወሳኝ ሚና ጎላ አድርጎ አሳይቷል። የSARS-COV-2 (የኮሮና ቫይረስ ዝርያ ሲሆን ከባድ እና አጣዳፊ የመተንፈሻ አካላት ችግር የሚያመጣ ነው) ዘረመል ባይጠና ኖሮ ውጤታማ ክትባቶች በፍጥነት አይገኙም ነበር። አዳዲስ እና የበለጠ ተላለፊ የሆኑ የቫይረሱ ዓይነቶችም በፍጥነት ተለይተው ባልታወቁ ነበር። የዘረመል ጥናት ለውጤታማ የወረርሽኝ ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽ መስጠት ቁልፍ ነው፤ እንዲሁም ለተለያዩ በሽታዎች እነዚህም ከምግብ ወለድ በሽታዎች ጀምሮ የመተፈሻ አካል በሽታ፣ ቲቢ እና ኤችአይቪ ጨምሮ ለሚደረጉ የምርምር ስራዎች ቀጣይነት ያለው ነው። ለምሳሌ የኤችአይቪ መድሀኒትን ውጤታማነት ለመከታተል ጥቅም ላይ መዋሉ ለቁጥር የሚያታክቱ ህይወቶችን ታድጓል።
translations.reference: COVID-19 highlighted the critical role pathogen genomics plays in responding to pandemic threats. Without the rapid sequencing of the SARS-COV-2 genome, vaccines would not have been as effective, or have been made available so quickly. New, more transmissible variants of the virus would not have been as quickly identified. Genomics lies at the heart of effective epidemic and pandemic preparedness and response, as well as part of the ongoing surveillance of a vast range of diseases, from foodborne diseases and influenza to tuberculosis and HIV. Its use in monitoring the spread of HIV drug resistance, for example, has led to antiretroviral regimes that have saved countless lives.
translations.hypothesis: The study of the COVID-19 virus has highlighted its crucial role in responding to the pandemic. Without understanding the SARS-CoV-2 virus (the virus responsible for the coronavirus, which causes severe and potentially fatal respiratory illness), effective treatments would not have been developed as quickly. Furthermore, new and more transmissible variants of the virus would have emerged undetected. Research into the virus is essential for effective pandemic preparedness and response, and it also supports ongoing research into a wide range of diseases, including foodborne illnesses, respiratory infections, tuberculosis, and HIV. For example, research into HIV has led to the development of effective treatments that have saved countless lives.

translations.source: “ሮክፌለር” የተባለው ማህበር ፕሬዝዳንት ዶ/ር ራጂቭ ጄ ሻህ እንዳሉት “ዓለም ኮቪድ-19ን በጋራ በሚዋጋበት ወቅት በበሽታ አምጪ ተውሳክ ዘረመል ክትትል ላይ የነበረው ዓለም አቀፍ ትብብር ወሳኝ ነበር።” "የአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት በዘርፉ ያሉ አጋሮችን ዕውቀት፣ መሳሪያዎች እና ልምዶች ለመለዋወጥ የሚያስችል ጠንካራ መድረክ በመፍጠር ወረርሽኝን ለመከላከል እና ምላሽ ለመስጠት ለወደፊቱ በዚህ ልምድ ላይ ይገነባል።" በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ምክንያት የአገሮች የዘረመል ጥናት አቅም በቅርብ ጊዜ ቢጨምርም ፣ ብዙዎች አሁንም ናሙናዎችን ለመሰብሰብ እና ለማጥናት እንዲሁም በመረጃዎች ተደግፎ የህዝብ ጤና ውሳኔዎችን ለማገዝ የሚችል ውጤታማ ስርዓት የላቸውም ። የተጠናከረ የአለም ጤና ጥበቃ ስርዓትን ለመገንባት በቂ የመረጃ፣ የአሰራር እና የፈጠራዎች መጋራት የለም። ወረርሽኙ በተከሰተበት ወቅት ለዘርፉ ተመድቦ የነበረው የገንዘብ መጠን አሁን ላይ ሀብታም በሆኑ አገሮች ውስጥ እንኳን እየቀነሰ ነው።
translations.reference: “Global collaboration in pathogen genomic surveillance has been critical as the world fights COVID-19 together,” said Dr Rajiv J. Shah, President of The Rockefeller Foundation. “IPSN builds upon this experience by creating a strong platform for partners across sectors and borders to share knowledge, tools, and practices to ensure that pandemic prevention and response is innovative and robust in the future.” Despite recent scale-up in genomics capacity in countries as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic, many still lack effective systems for collecting and analyzing samples or using those data to drive public health decision-making. There is not enough sharing of data, practices, and innovations to build a robust global health surveillance architecture. Budgets that soared during the pandemic, allowing a rapid build-up of capabilities, are now being slashed, even in the wealthiest countries.
translations.hypothesis: According to Dr. Rajiv J. Shah, the president of the "Rockefeller" foundation, "International collaboration was crucial in the global fight against COVID-19, particularly in the development and deployment of vaccines." "The International Vaccine Alliance has created a strong platform for partners in the field to share knowledge, tools, and expertise, which will be essential for preparing for and responding to future outbreaks." While countries have significantly increased their capacity for vaccine research in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, many still lack the resources and infrastructure to collect and analyze samples, and to develop effective systems for making evidence-based public health decisions. There is a need for greater sharing of data, expertise, and innovation to build a stronger global health system.

translations.source: "አርጀንቲና የራሳችንን ሀገር እና የሌሎች ሀገራትንም የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመል ጥናት አቅም ለመገንባት የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ታደርጋለች።" ሲሉ የአርጀንቲና ብሄራዊ ዘረመል ጥናት ማዕከል ዳይሬክተር ጆሴፊና ካምፖስ ተናግረዋል ። "በሽታዎች ድንበር አያከብሩም፤ በአንድ ሀገር ውስጥ ያለ የበሽታ ስጋት ለሌሎችም ስጋት ነው። በሽታን ለመከላከል እና ህይወትን ለማዳን እንዲሁም የጋራ ግባችንን ለማሳካት ከአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አባላት ጋር ለመተባበር ዝግጁ ነን። የአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት እነዚህን ተግዳሮቶች በአለምአቀፍ ደረጃ ሃገራትን እና በሽታ-ተኮር ህብረቶችን በማገናኘት፤ አደጋዎችን በተሻለ ለመለየት፣ ለመከላከል እና ምላሽ ለመስጠት የትብብር ስርዓትን በመገንባት ይሰራል። በአለም አቀፍ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት በኩል በገንዘብ በመደገፍ፤ ሀሳቦችን እና ፕሮጀክቶችን ለማሳደግ አባላቱ በልዩ ልዩ ተግዳሮቶች ላይ በማተኮር በተዘጋጁ ቡድኖች በጋራ ይሰራሉ።
translations.reference: “Argentina is deeply invested in building our own country capacity in pathogen genomics and the capacity of other countries,” said Josefina Campos, Director of the National Genomics and Bioinformatics Center at ANLIS Malbrán, Argentina. “Diseases do not respect borders: a disease threat in one country is also a threat to others. We look forward to collaborating with IPSN members to achieve our common goal of preventing illness and saving lives.” The IPSN will tackle these challenges through a global network, connecting geographies and disease-specific networks, to build a collaborative system to better detect, prevent and respond to disease threats. Members will work together in dedicated groups focusing on specific challenges, supported by funding through the IPSN to scale-up ideas and projects in pathogen genomics.
translations.hypothesis: "Argentina is committed to building its own research capacity and supporting research in other countries to combat infectious diseases," said Josefina Campos, director of the National Institute of Biological Research. "Diseases do not respect borders; a threat in one country is a threat to all. We are ready to collaborate with members of the International Association for Research in Biological Sciences to prevent and control diseases, save lives, and achieve our common goals. The International Association for Research in Biological Sciences works to address these challenges by connecting countries and disease-prone regions through a collaborative system, enabling better detection, prevention, and response to outbreaks. Through its network of research institutions and experts, the International Association for Research in Biological Sciences fosters innovation and collaboration, allowing its members to address specific challenges and develop effective solutions."

translations.source: ሀገሮችን፣ ክልሎችን እና ሰፊ ባለድርሻ አካላትን በማገናኘት ህብረቱ ወሳኝ አቅምን ለመጨመር፣ ክልላዊ እና ሀገር አቀፍ ድምጽን ለማጉላት እና ቅድሚያ የሚሰጧቸውን ነገሮች ለማጠናከር ይረዳል። ስለ አለም አቀፉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አጀማመር፤ ህብረቱ በጄኔቫ የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ በሚካሄድበት ጊዜ በይፋ ይጀምራል። የማስጀመሪያው ዝግጅት እ.ኤ.አ. በግንቦት 20 ቀን 2023 ከቀኑ 17፡00 - 19፡30 ሰአት በጄኔቫ ኢንተር ኮንቲኔንታል ውስጥ ይካሄዳል እናም ለመገናኛ ብዙሃን ክፍት ነው። የሚከተሉት ዋና ዋና ተናጋሪዎችም ይገኙበታል፤
translations.reference: By connecting countries, regions, and wider stakeholders, the IPSN will help to increase critical capacity, amplify regional and country-level voices, and strengthen their priorities. About the IPSN launch The IPSN will be formally launched on the sidelines of the World Health Assembly in Geneva. The launch event takes place on 20 May 2023, 17:00 – 19:30h (CET) in the InterContinental Geneva, and is open to media. It includes keynote speeches from:
translations.hypothesis: The network plays a crucial role in connecting countries, regions, and major stakeholders, thereby enhancing its capacity to address global health challenges, amplify regional and national voices, and strengthen priorities. The network will officially launch its activities at the World Health Assembly in Geneva, where it will be held. The initial meeting will take place on May 20, 2023, from 5:00 PM to 7:30 PM at the Geneva InterContinental Hotel, and will be open to the media. The following key speakers will be present:

translations.source: ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር፤ ዶ/ር ማይክ ራያን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የድንገተኛ አደጋ ፕሮግራም ዋና ዳይሬክተር እና ዶ/ር ቺክዌ ኢኽክዌዙ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ረዳት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ከወረርሽኝ ጥናት ማዕከል ናቸው። በተጨማሪም ከአባል ሃገሮች እና ከገንዘብ ድጋፍ ሰጪዎች ከፍተኛ ደረጃ ተናጋሪዎች የሚኖሩበት ውይይት ይኖራል። እነርሱም፤ ዴም ጄኒ ሃሪስ የተባበሩት መንግስታት የጤና ጥበቃ ኤጀንሲ ዋና ሥራ አስፈፃሚ፤
translations.reference: Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General, World Health Organization; Dr Mike Ryan, Executive Director, WHO Health Emergencies Programme; and Dr Chikwe Ihekweazu, Assistant Director-General, World Health Organization, Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence. There will also be a panel with high-level speakers from Member State and IPSN funders, including; Dame Jenny Harries, Chief Executive, UK Health Security Agency;
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization; Dr. Michael Ryan, Director of the WHO's Emergency Programme; and Dr. Chikwe Ihekweazu, Deputy Director-General of the WHO, are all leading experts in pandemic research and response. The meeting will also bring together high-level representatives from member states and funding agencies. Key participants include: Demis Jean-Harris, Director-General of the World Health Organization;

translations.source: ዶ/ር ዣን ካሴያ የአፍሪካ የበሽታ መቆጣጠርና መከላከል ማዕከል ዋና ዳይሬክተር፤ ዶ / ር ናቪን ራኦ የ”ሮክፌለር” ድርጅት ምክትል ፕሬዚዳንት፤ ዶ / ር አሌክሳንደር ፒም የተላላፊ በሽታዎች ዳይሬክተር፤ ፕሮፌሰር ቱሊዮ ዴ ኦሊቬራ በደቡብ አፍሪካ ስቴለንቦሽ ዩኒቨርሲቲ የበሽታ ስርጭት ጥናት ማዕከል ዳይሬክተር፤ ቦጅሮን ኩሜል በጀርመን የፌዴራል ጤና ጥበቃ ሚኒስቴር ምክትል ኃላፊ፤
translations.reference: Dr Jean Kaseya, Director-General, Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention; Dr Naveen Rao, Senior Vice President, Rockefeller Foundation; Dr Alexander Pym, Director of Infectious Disease, Wellcome Trust; Professor Tulio de Oliveira, Director, Centre for Epidemiology at Stellenbosch University, South Africa; Björn Kümmel, Deputy Head of Unit, Global Health, Federal Ministry of Health, Germany;
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Jean Cassel, Director of the African Center for Disease Control and Prevention; Dr. Navin Rao, Vice President of the Rockefeller Foundation; Dr. Alexander Pimm, Director of Infectious Diseases; Professor Tulio de Oliveira, Director of the Center for Infectious Disease Research at Stellenbosch University in South Africa; Mr. Bojorn Kume, Deputy Director of the German Federal Ministry of Health;

translations.source: ፕሮፌሰር ሊዮ ኢይ ሲን የስንጋፖር ብሔራዊ ተላላፊ በሽታዎች ማዕከል ዋና ዳይሬክተር፤ ዶክተር ማሪዮ ሞሬራ የፊዮክሩዝ ብራዚል ፕሬዚዳንት እና ዶ/ር ጆሴፊና ካምፖስ በአርጀንቲና የብሔራዊ ዘረመል ጥናት ማዕከል ዳይሬክተር ናቸው። ዝግጅቱ የሚከናወነው በድብልቅ ቅርጸት ነው፣ ይህም ለአለም አቀፉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አባላት እና ለሰፊ ታዳሚዎች በመረጡት አማራጭ እንዲሳተፉ እድል ይሰጣል። ስለ አለም አቀፉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት፤ ህብረቱ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝን ጥናት ማዕከል ስር የተመሰረተ ሲሆን የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመል ጥናት ሂደትን ለማፋጠን እና የህዝብ ጤና ውሳኔ አሰጣጥን ለማሻሻል የተቋቋመ ነው።
translations.reference: Professor Leo Yee Sin, Executive Director of the National Centre for Infectious Diseases; Singapore; Dr Mario Moreira, President, Fiocruz, Brazil; and Dr Josefina Campos, Director of the National Genomics and Bioinformatics Center at ANLIS Malbrán, Argentina. The event will take place in a hybrid format, providing an opportunity for IPSN members and broader audiences to engage with the IPSN and its activities. About the IPSNThe IPSN is a new global network of pathogen genomic actors, hosted by the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, to accelerate progress on the deployment of pathogen genomics and improve public health decision-making.
translations.hypothesis: Professor Liio Ey Sin is the Director of the National Institute of Infectious Diseases in Sinagapore; Dr. Mario Moreira is the President of Fiocruz Brazil, and Dr. Josefine Campos is the Director of the National Center for Viral Research in Argentina. The event will be held in a hybrid format, allowing members of the International Society for Infectious Diseases and a wider audience to participate. The International Society for Infectious Diseases is an organization founded under the auspices of the World Health Organization, with the aim of accelerating viral research and improving public health decision-making.

translations.source: ህብረቱ እያንዳንዱ ሀገር የህዝብ ጤና ክትትል ስርአት አካል ሆኖ የዘረመል ጥናት ዘላቂ አቅም እና ፍትሃዊ ተደራሽነት ያለባትን ዓለም ማየት ግቡ ነው ። ተደራሽነትን እና ፍትሃዊነትን ለማሻሻል የአባላቱን ስራ የሚያጎላ እና የሚያፋጥን እርስ በርስ የሚደጋገፍ አለምአቀፍ የክትትል መረብ ለመፍጠር አቅዷል። ህብረቱ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝ ጥናት ማዕከል ጽህፈት ቤት የሚደገፉ የተለያዩ ባለድርሻ አካላትን የሚያሰባስቡ ሶስት ዋና የስራ አስፈፃሚ አካላትን ያቀፈ ነው። የመጀመሪያው አጋሮቹ በCommunities of Practice (COPs) በጋራ ተግዳሮቶችን ለመፍታት እንዲተባበሩ ያደርጋል፤ ይህም በበሽታ አምጪ የዘረመል ጥናት ውስጥ መስማማትን እና ፈጠራን ለመጨመር ነው። በሃገር ደረጃ ስኬል አፕ አክስሌሬተር (Country Scale-Up Accelerator) ውስጥ ባለድርሻ አካላቱ ጥረቶችን በማጣጣም እና የደቡብ-ደቡብ ልውውጡን (South-South exchange) በመደገፍ የሀገርን አቅም ግንባታ ለማሳደግ በጋራ እንዲሰሩ ያደርጋል።
translations.reference: The IPSN envisions a world where every country has equitable access to sustained capacity for genomic sequencing and analytics as part of its public health surveillance system. It sets out to create a mutually supportive global network of genomic surveillance actors that amplifies and accelerates the work of its members to improve access and equity. The IPSN consists of three main operational bodies that bring together different sets of stakeholders, supported by a Secretariat at the WHO Pandemic Hub. Partners collaborate in Communities of Practice (COPs) to solve common challenges, aiming to increase harmonization and innovation in pathogen genomics. In the Country Scale-Up Accelerator (CSUA), stakeholders work together to align efforts and enable South-South exchange to scale-up country capacity building.
translations.hypothesis: The Initiative aims to create a sustainable and equitable global system for neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) by integrating each country's national health program. It seeks to improve access and equity by establishing a global network of partners that will support and strengthen national NTD programs. The Initiative is comprised of three key partner organizations, all of which are supported by the World Health Organization's neglected tropical diseases research center. The first partner, the Communities of Practice (COPs), will bring together partners to share best practices and develop innovative solutions to challenges in NTD control. The second partner, the Country Scale-Up Accelerator, will work with partners to scale up successful interventions and promote South-South exchange.

translations.source: እነዚህ ሁለቱ ለአለም አቀፍ ትብብር እና ፍትሃዊነት እንዲሁም ጥልቅ እውቀት ከተለያዩ ዘርፎች፣ የገቢ ደረጃዎች እና አካባቢዎች የተውጣጡ ድርጅቶችን በአንድነት ያሰባስባሉ። ሦስተኛው አካል የበጎ አድራጎት ድርጅቶች፣ የመንግስት ለጋሾችን እና ሌሎችን በማስተባበር የፓለቲካ ትኩረት እና የበሽታ አምጪ ዘረመል ክትትል ላይ የገንዘብ አያያዝ ውጤታማነትን ለማስተባበር የሚያግዘው “Funders Forum” ነው። ይህ አካል ለአለም አቀፉ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ጥናት ህብረት አባላት ስራዎች ተጨማሪ የገንዘብ ድጋፍን ያዘጋጃል። የህብረቱ ሲቋቋም የጀርመን መንግስት ለአለም ጤና ድርጅት በሰጠው የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ተደግፏል። ስለ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝ ጥናት ማዕከል ፤
translations.reference: The COP and CSUA bring together organizations from across sectors, income levels, and geographies, with a commitment to international cooperation and equity, and deep expertise in either genomics or country scale-up of surveillance systems. The third body is the Funders Forum to coordinate philanthropic, multi-lateral and governmental donors around increased political attention and financing efficiency of pathogen genomic surveillance. The Funders Forum also catalyses additional grant funding for projects of IPSN members. The establishment of the IPSN has been supported by German government funding to the WHO Pandemic Hub. About the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence
translations.hypothesis: These two entities work together to promote international cooperation and fairness, and to bring together organizations from diverse fields, income levels, and regions. The third entity, the "Funders Forum," brings together philanthropic organizations, government agencies, and others to coordinate efforts on political advocacy and disease eradication. This entity provides additional financial support to the work of the International Coalition on Epidemic Preparedness, which was established with the support of the German government and the World Health Organization.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የድንገተኛ አደጋ ፕሮግራም አካል የሆነው የወረርሽኝ ጥናት ማዕከሉ ሃገራትን የሚደግፉ ከበርካታ ዘርፎች የተወጣጡ አጋሮች መካከል ዓለም አቀፍ ትብብርን ያመቻቻል፤ ይህ ትብብር ለቀጣይ የወረርሽኝ ስጋቶችን ለመቅረፍ አገሮችን እና ባለድርሻ አካላትን በተሻለ የመረጃ ተደራሽነት፣ የተሻሻለ የጥናት አቅም እና የውሳኔ አሰጣጥ ግንዛቤዎችን ይደግፋል። ከጀርመን ፌዴራላዊ ሪፐብሊክ መንግስት በተገኘ ድጋፍ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝ ማዕከል እ.ኤ.አ. በመስከረም 2021 በበርሊን ለኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ምላሽ ለመስጠት ተቋቁሟል፤ ወረርሽኙ ሀገሮች የህዝብ ጤና ስጋቶችን እንዴት እንደሚለዩ፣ እንደሚቆጣጠሩ እና እንደሚያስተዳድሩ በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ ድክመቶችን አጋልጧል። የአለም ጤና ድርጅት የወረርሽኝ ማዕከል መረጃን የመለዋወጥ አቅማቸውን ለማጠናከር ከአባል ሀገራት እና ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ክልላዊ እና ሀገር አቀፍ ቢሮዎች ጋር በቅርበት ይሰራል፤ ለቅድመ ዝግጅት መረጃን ለመሰብሰብ እና ለማጥናት በዓለም ዙሪያ ያሉ አጋሮች እንዲተባበሩ እና መሳሪያዎችን እንዲያዳብሩ ያስችላቸዋል። በ150 አገሮች፣ በስድስት የክልል ቢሮዎች እና በጄኔቫ ዋና መሥሪያ ቤቱ የሚገኘው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ማዕከል በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ወረርሽኞችን እና የጤና አደጋዎችን በእኩል አጣዳፊነት እና በትጋት ለመፍታት አቅምን ይሰጣል። የአካባቢ፣ ክልላዊ እና ዓለም አቀፋዊ ተነሳሽነቶችን በማቀራረብ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ወረርሽኝ ማዕከል ለአዲስ ነገር ፈጣሪዎች፣ ለተመራማሪዎች እና ከተለያዩ ዘርፎች ለተውጣጡ ባለሙያዎች የትብብር መድረክን ያሳድጋል፤ ይህም ቴክኖሎጂን በመጠቀም ስራን በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለባለድርሻ ለማቅረብ ያስችላል።
translations.reference: Forming part of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence (the WHO Pandemic Hub), facilitates a global collaboration of partners from multiple sectors that supports countries and stakeholders to address future pandemic and epidemic risks with better access to data, better analytical capacities, and better tools and insights for decision-making. With support from the Government of the Federal Republic of Germany, the WHO Pandemic Hub was established in September 2021 in Berlin, in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, which demonstrated weaknesses around the world in how countries detect, monitor and manage public health threats. The WHO Pandemic Hub works closely with Member States and WHO regional and country offices to strengthen their data-sharing capacities and enable partners from around the world to collaborate and co-create tools to gather and analyse data for early warning surveillance. With a presence in more than 150 countries, six regional offices, and its Geneva headquarters, WHO’s reach gives us the ability to treat pandemic, epidemic and public health risks with equal urgency and diligence around the globe. By linking local, regional, and global initiatives, the WHO Pandemic Hub fosters a collaborative environment for innovators, scientists and experts from across a wide spectrum of disciplines, allowing us to leverage and share cutting-edge technology and anchoring our work in the needs of stakeholders around the world.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization's (WHO) Global Outbreak Alert and Response Program (GOAARP) includes the Influenza Centre, which partners with organizations from various fields to promote international collaboration. This collaboration supports countries and stakeholders in better preparedness, improved surveillance, and enhanced response capabilities to influenza outbreaks. The WHO Influenza Centre, supported by the German Federal Republic, was established in Berlin in September 2021 to respond to the COVID-19 pandemic. It has provided valuable insights into how countries can detect, control, and manage influenza outbreaks. The WHO Influenza Centre works closely with member states and WHO regional and country offices to strengthen their capacity for data sharing and analysis. It also facilitates collaboration and resource mobilization among partners around the world to support preparedness and response efforts. With its headquarters in Geneva, the WHO Influenza Centre has a global network of 150 countries, six regional offices, and numerous collaborating institutions. It is committed to addressing influenza and other health emergencies in a timely and effective manner. By fostering collaboration among researchers, policymakers, and other stakeholders, the WHO Influenza Centre provides a platform for innovation and knowledge sharing.

translations.source: በተለያዩ ዘርፎች እና ክልሎች ያሉ ባለሙያዎችን በመጠቀም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ወረርሽኝ ማዕከል በአለም አቀፍ ትብብር እና እምነት ማዕቀፍ ላይ የተመሰረቱ ዓለም አቀፍ መፍትሄዎችን ለማዳበር ያስችላል።
translations.reference: Building on expertise across disciplines, sectors, and regions, it will leverage WHO’s convening power to foster global solutions built on an architecture of global collaboration and trust.
translations.hypothesis: By leveraging experts from various fields and regions, the World Health Organization's pandemic center can achieve global solutions based on international cooperation and trust.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመቱን ሲያከብር፤ ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ “ህይወትን በማዳን፤ ጤናን ለሁሉም ማድረስ” በሚል ርዕስ ትኩረት አድርጓል። ዓለማችን የጤና እና የሰብዓዊ ድንገተኛ አደጋ እያጋጠማት ባለበት በዚህ ጊዜ፤ ሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ጤናን ለሁሉም ማድረስ ላይ ያተኩራል። የዘንድሮው ክፍለ ጊዜ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅትን የቅርብ እና የረጅም ጊዜ የወደፊት እጣ ፈንታን የሚወስን ሲሆን ይህም ለቀጣዮቹ ሁለት ዓመታት የሚመደበውን የፕሮግራሙን በጀት፣ የድርጅቱን ዘላቂ የገንዘብ አጠቃቀም እና የድርጅቱን ሂደቶች እና ተጠያቂነት ለማሳደግ ማሻሻያዎችን ጨምሮ ሌሎች ውሳኔዎችን ያካትታል። ልዑካኑ በድንገተኛ አደጋ ስራዓት ውስጥ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስላለው ወሳኝ ሚናም ይወያያሉ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ የምስረታ በዓሉን ሲያከብር እ.ኤ.አ. በ1948 ከተመሠረተበት ጊዜ ጀምሮ ያስመዘገበውን ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ስኬቶችን ያቀርባል።
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly to focus on “saving lives, driving health for all” as WHO turns 75 As the world faces ongoing health and humanitarian emergencies, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly will focus on driving forward health for all. This year’s session of the World Health Assembly will determine the immediate and longer-term future of WHO, starting with the program budget for the next two years, key decisions about the sustainable financing of the Organization and changes put in place to improve WHO’s processes and accountability. Delegates will also deliberate about the critical role that WHO has in the Global Health Emergency Architecture. The backdrop to these issues is the organization’s 75th anniversary, with the highlights of its collective global health achievements since WHO’s creation in 1948.
translations.hypothesis: As the World Health Organization celebrates its 75th anniversary, the 76th World Health Assembly has focused on the theme "Health for all, everyone healthy". In a world facing increasing health and humanitarian crises, the 76th World Health Assembly has prioritized ensuring health for all. A key focus of this year's assembly is the World Health Organization's future, which will include decisions on the organization's strategic direction for the next two years, including budget allocations, institutional reforms, and measures to enhance transparency and accountability. The assembly will also discuss the WHO's crucial role in emergency response. The 76th World Health Assembly will also celebrate the WHO's 75th anniversary, highlighting the organization's global health achievements since its founding in 1948.

translations.source: መጪው 76ኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ካለፈው ዓመት የተከናወኑ ሂደቶችን፣ ስኬቶችን እና ተግዳሮቶችን እንዲሁም የወደፊት ቅድሚያ የሚሰጣቸውን ቁልፍ ስራዎች በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዋና መለኪያዎች እነዚህም ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን፣ ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች እንዲሁም ጤናን ማስተዋወቅ ይገመግማል። 76ኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 21 እስከ 30 በጄኔቫ ፓሌይስ ዴ ኔሽን ይካሄዳል። ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረየሱስ “የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመት የምስረታ በዓልን ስናከብር ከኮቪድ-19 ድንገተኛ አደጋ ምዕራፍ ተላቀን ሲሆን ካለፈው ጊዜ ተምረን ሁሉም ሰው አስፈላጊ የጤና አገልግሎቶችን የሚያገኝበት ነገን ለመፍጠር እየጣርን መሆን አለበት።” ብለዋል። "ይህም ማለት ለድንገተኛ የጤና ችግሮች ዝግጁነት፣ ምላሽ መስጠት እና መቋቋም መቻል ዓለም አቀፉን ስርዓት ማጠናከር፣ ሀገራት ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋን እንዲያገኙ መርዳት እንዲሁም ጠንካራ እና ዘላቂ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ያለው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት መገንባትን ያካትታል።" "እ.ኤ.አ. እሁድ ግንቦት 21 ቀን 76ኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤን ለማስጀመር አራተኛው ወክ ዘ ቶክ፡ ጤና ለሁሉም (Walk the Talk: Health for All) ቻሌንጅ በዴስ ኔሽን ከጠዋቱ በ8:30 ላይ ይጀምራል፤ (ማማሞቂያው ከጠዋቱ 7:30 ጀምሮ ይኖራል) ይህም በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት 75ኛ ዓመት የምስረታ በዓል ላይ የአካል እና የአእምሮ ጤናን ለማስተዋወቅ ነው።
translations.reference: It will review last year’s progress, accomplishments and challenges along with future priorities across the key pillars of WHO’s work: Universal Health Coverage, Emergencies, Promoting Health and Well-being. WHA76 will convene 21-30 May at the Palais des Nations in Geneva. “As we mark WHO’s 75th anniversary, we can be proud of our past achievements, but we must be mindful of lessons learned, as we transition from the emergency phase of COVID-19, and create a future where every person has access to the health services they need,” said Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “This means strengthening the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response and resilience, supporting countries on the road to universal health coverage, and building a stronger, sustainably-funded and accountable WHO. "To kickstart WHA76 on Sunday 21 May, the fourth edition of Walk the Talk: Health for All challenge will start at Place des Nations at 8:30 am CEST (warm-up starts at 7:30 am CEST), to promote physical and mental health and celebrate the importance of partnership during the WHO’s 75th anniversary.
translations.hypothesis: The 76th World Health Assembly will focus on key priorities for the World Health Organization, including universal health coverage, pandemic preparedness, and health promotion. The 76th World Health Assembly will take place from May 21 to 30 at the Palais des Nations in Geneva. Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said, "As we celebrate the 75th anniversary of the World Health Organization, we have learned from the COVID-19 pandemic and must work together to ensure that everyone has access to the health services they need. This means strengthening the global health system, helping countries achieve universal health coverage, and building a strong and resilient WHO." The opening ceremony of the 76th World Health Assembly will take place on Monday, May 21, at 8:30 AM, with the "Walk the Talk: Health for All" challenge, which will promote physical and mental health as part of the WHO's 75th anniversary celebrations.

translations.source: ከምሽቱ 2፡00 ሰዓት አካባቢ የልዑካን መሪዎቹ የቡድን ፎቶ መርሃ ግብርን ተከትሎ ከፍተኛ የእንኳን ደህና መጣችሁ አቀባበል እንዲሁም የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር፣ የሀገር መሪዎች እና ልዩ እንግዶች ንግግሮች ይቀርባል። (ከታች የተናጋሪዎች ዝርዝር አለ።) በዚህ ክፍለ ጊዜ የመርሃ ግብሩ አስተባባሪ የህይወት ዘመን ስኬት ሽልማቶችን ለሁለት የአለም ጤና መሪዎች ያቀርባል። እ.ኤ.አ. ሰኞ ግንቦት 22 ቀን ዋና ዳይሬክተሩ ዋና አጀንዳቸውን በ10፡00 ሰዓት አካባቢ ያቀርባሉ። እ.ኤ.አ. አርብ ግንቦት 26 ቀን የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ከአምስት የአለም አቀፍ ማህበራት ተወካዮች ጋር በመሆን አምስት የህዝብ ጤና ሽልማቶችን ለሰባቱ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 ተሸላሚዎች ይሰጣሉ። ተሸላሚዎቹም የመጡት ከባህሬን፣ ቻይና፣ ጓቲማላ፣ ሊባኖስ፣ ኔፓል፣ ታይላንድ እና ፊሊፒንስ ነው።
translations.reference: At about 2:00 pm CEST, following a family photo with Heads of Delegations, the Assembly high-level welcome at Palais des Nations will feature speeches from the WHO Director-General, Heads of State and special guests. List of speakers below). During this session, the DG will present life-time achievement awards to two global health leaders. On Monday 22 May, the Director-General will give his main address at approximately 10:00 am. On Friday, 26 May, the WHO Director-General will be joined by representatives of five global foundations, to present five public health awards and prizes to the seven laureates for 2023. They come from Bahrain, China, Guatemala, Lebanon, Nepal, Thailand and the Philippines.
translations.hypothesis: Around 2:00 PM, the program will begin with a welcome address from the organizing committee, followed by speeches from the Director-General of the World Health Organization, national leaders, and special guests. (A list of speakers is below.) During this session, the program coordinator will present lifetime achievement awards to two prominent figures in global health. On Monday, June 22, the Director-General will present her main address at 10:00 AM. On Wednesday, June 26, the Director-General will present five global health awards to seven winners, in partnership with five international organizations. The winners are from Bhutan, China, Guatemala, Lebanon, Nepal, Thailand, and the Philippines.

translations.source: ቁልፍ ጉዳዮች፤ በሚከተሉት ላይ ቁልፍ ውይይቶች እና ውሳኔዎች ይጠበቃሉ፤ የአለም ጤና ጥበቃ ደንቦችን ጨምሮ የድንገተኛ የጤና አደጋዎችን በመቋቋም ዝግጁነትን እና ምላሽን በማጠናከር ላይ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት ስራ ግምገማ፤ እንደ የሴቶች፣ የሕፃናት እና የታዳጊ ወጣቶች ጤና፣ መልሶ ማቋቋም፣ ዓለም አቀፍ የጤና ሽፋን እና የመጀመሪያ ደረጃ ጤና አጠባበቅ፣ የባህል ህክምና፣ ተላላፊ በሽታዎችን መከላከልና መቆጣጠር፣ ደረጃቸውን ያልጠበቁ እና የተጭበረበሩ መድሃኒቶች፣ የስደተኞች ጤና፣ ተላላፊ ባልሆኑ በሽታዎች፣ የአዕምሮ ጤና፣ አመጋገብ እና የአካል ጉዳት ላይ ስልቶች እና አለም አቀፍ እርምጃዎች፤ የተገመገሙ መዋጮዎችን መጨመር እና ሌሎች ዘላቂ የገንዘብ ድጋፍ ጉዳዮች መገምገም ጨምሮ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2024-2025 የአለም ጤና ድርጅትን በጀት ማፅደቅ ናቸው።
translations.reference: Key issues Key discussions and decisions are expected on: A review of WHOs work in health emergencies, including the International Health Regulations and strengthening WHO preparedness for and response to health emergencies; Strategies and global action on areas such as women’s, children’s and adolescents’ health, rehabilitation, universal health coverage and primary health care, traditional medicine, infection prevention and control, substandard and falsified medicines, health of refugees and migrants, non-communicable diseases, mental health, social determinants, nutrition and disabilities; Approval of the WHO Programme Budget for 2024-2025, including the decision to increase assessed contributions and other matters emanating from the Working Group on Sustainable Financing.
translations.hypothesis: Key issues to be addressed include: strengthening preparedness and response to public health emergencies, including compliance with the International Health Regulations; assessing the World Health Organization's work in areas such as maternal, child, and adolescent health, health system strengthening, universal health coverage, primary health care, traditional medicine, the prevention and control of infectious diseases, substandard and counterfeit medicines, migrant health, non-communicable diseases, mental health, nutrition, and physical activity; and reviewing and approving the WHO's 2024-2025 budget, including measures to increase funding and address other sustainable financing issues.

translations.source: ዕለታዊ ስልታዊ ውይይቶች በምሳ ሰአት ከሰኞ እስከ ቅዳሜ በ18ኛው ክፍል ውስጥ ይካሄዳሉ። እነዚህ ዝግጅቶች በስብሰባ ተወካዮች፣ አጋር ድርጅቶች፣ የሲቪል ማህበረሰብ ተወካዮች እና የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ባለሙያዎች በሕዝብ ጤና ቅድሚያ የሚሰጧቸው ጉዳዮች ላይ ውይይት ለማድረግ ያስችላል። ክፍለ-ጊዜዎች እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 22 እስከ 27፣ ከ13፡00 እስከ 14፡15 ሰዓት የሚሄዱ ሲሆን በመስመር ላይ ሊከተሏቸው ይችላሉ። የጤና ጉባኤው የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ከፍተኛ ውሳኔ ሰጪ አካል እንደመሆኑ የድርጅቱን ፖሊሲ ያዘጋጃል እናም በጀቱን ያፀድቃል። በጤና ጉባኤው ከ194ቱ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት አባል ሀገራት የተውጣጡ ልዑካን ይሳተፋሉ።
translations.reference: Daily Strategic roundtables will take place at lunch time from Monday to Saturday in Room XVIII. These events will host discussions among assembly delegates, partner agencies, representatives of civil society and WHO experts on public health priorities. Sessions run from 22-27 May, from 13:00 to 14:15 CEST and can be followed online. As WHO’s highest decision-making body, the Health Assembly sets out the Organization’s policy and approves its budget. WHA is attended by delegations from all WHO 194 Member States.
translations.hypothesis: Daily strategic discussions take place at 18:00, from Monday to Friday, in Room 18. These meetings provide a forum for representatives from participating organizations, civil society representatives, and World Health Organization experts to discuss issues of public health priority. The sessions will be held from May 22 to 27, from 13:00 to 14:15, and can be attended online. As the World Health Organization's highest decision-making body, the Health Assembly sets the organization's policies and approves its budget. The Health Assembly is attended by delegates from the 194 member states of the World Health Organization.

translations.source: የጤና ጉባኤው አጀንዳ እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 17 እስከ 19 2023 በሚካሄደው የስራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርድ 30ኛው የፕሮግራም፣ በጀት እና አስተዳደር ኮሚቴ ስብሰባ ቀርቧል። ከጉባዔው በኋላ 153ኛው የሥራ አስፈፃሚ ቦርድ ስብሰባ እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 31 እስከ ሰኔ 1 ቀን ድረስ ይካሄዳል። የጤና ጉባኤው ለተመዘገቡ እና እውቅና ለተሰጣቸው አባል ሀገራት፣ አጋር አባላት፣ ታዛቢዎች፣ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ጥሪ የተደረገላቸው ተወካዮች እና ሌሎች ተሳታፊዎች እንዲሁም መንግስታዊ ያልሆኑ ድርጅቶች ክፍት ነው። 76ኛው የጤና ጉባኤ በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ድህረ ገጽ በቀጥታ ይለቀቃል። ጊዜያዊ አጀንዳውን፣ የመጀመሪያውን የጊዜ ሰሌዳ፣ የመሰብሰቢያ ሰነዶች እና ዕለታዊ ማስታወሻዎችን እዚህ ማግኘት ይቻላል።
translations.reference: The Health Assembly’s agenda is preceded by the 30th meeting of the Programme, Budget and Administration Committee of the Executive Board, which will meet on 17-19 May, 2023. After the Assembly, the 153th meeting of the Executive Board will take place from 31 May to 1 June. The Health Assembly is open to registered and accredited Member States, Associate Members, Observers, invited representatives of the UN and other participating inter-governmental organizations and non-State actors. WHA76 will be webcast live from the WHO website. The provisional agenda, preliminary timetable, Assembly documents and daily journal can be accessed here.Media accreditation and updates for the World Health Assembly
translations.hypothesis: The agenda for the Health Assembly includes a meeting of the 30th Program, Budget, and Administration Committee of the Executive Board, scheduled for June 17-19, 2023. The 153rd meeting of the Executive Board will also be held from June 31 to July 1. The Health Assembly is open to registered and accredited member states, partner organizations, observers, representatives of the United Nations, and other participants, as well as non-governmental organizations. The 76th Health Assembly will be broadcast live on the World Health Organization website. The provisional agenda, the first timetable, meeting documents, and daily notes can be found here.

translations.source: የሚዲያ እውቅና በጄኔቫ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ድርጅት ይሰጣል። በኢንዲኮ (Indico) በኩል ይመዝገቡ፤ ባጅዎን ለመውሰድ፡ እ.ኤ.አ. አርብ ግንቦት 19 (በስራ ሰዓት፡ ከ9፡00 እስከ 16፡30 ከሰዓት) በ “Villa Feuillantines, Place des Nations” ይገኙ። ዝርዝሮች እና አድራሻ ለማግኘት፡ https://unece.org/important-information-how-obtain-your-badge ይጎብኙ። እ.ኤ.አ. ቅዳሜ ግንቦት 20 በ”UN Palais des Nations at Pregny Gate” ከ13፡00 እስከ 17፡00 ሰዓት፤ እ.ኤ.አ. እሑድ ግንቦት 21 የሚዲያ ባጅዎን በ “UN Palais des Nations Pregny Gate” ከ9፡00 እስከ 16፡00 ሰዓት ድረስ ይሰብስቡ።
translations.reference: Media accreditation for WHA76 will be handled by the United Nations of Geneva. Register via Indico. To collect your badge: On Friday 19 May (working hours: 9:00 am to 16:30 pm CEST) at Villa Feuillantines, Place des Nations . Details and address : https://unece.org/important-information-how-obtain-your-badge On Saturday 20 May: UN Palais des Nations at Pregny Gate (opposite ICRC Building) from 13:00 to 17:00 CEST. On Sunday 21 May, you shall collect your media badge at the UN Palais des Nations at Pregny Gate (opposite ICRC Building) from 9:00am to 16:00 pm CEST
translations.hypothesis: Media accreditation is provided by the United Nations in Geneva. Register through Indico to obtain your badge. You can pick up your badge on **Wednesday, June 19 (working hours: 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM) at "Villa Feuillantines, Place des Nations."** For details and address, please visit: https://unece.org/important-information-how-obtain-your-badge. Please collect your media badge on **Friday, June 20 (1:00 PM to 5:00 PM) at "UN Palais des Nations at Pregny Gate,"** and on **Sunday, June 21 (9:00 AM to 4:00 PM) at "UN Palais des Nations Pregny Gate."**

translations.source: በሚቀጥለው ሳምንት እ.ኤ.አ. ከግንቦት 22 እስከ 26፤ የመገናኛ ብዙሃን ባጅ ለመሰብሰብ “Villa Les Feuillantines Place des Nations” ይገኙ። በጤናው ጉባኤ ላይ ያሉ ማሻሻያዎች በዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ድረ-ገጽ ላይ ይለጠፋሉ፤ እና ተዛማጅነት ያላቸው ጉዳዮች ወደ ዓለም አቀፍ ሚዲያ ይላካሉ። ዜናን ለማግኘት ይመዝገቡ። ፎቶግራፎች እና ቪዲዮዎች የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፎቶዎችን ለመገናኛ ብዙኃን አገልግሎት ከፈለጉ በፎቶ ቤተ መጻሕፍት በኩል ይገኛሉ።
translations.reference: Next week - 22-26 May- to collect the media badges: Villa Les Feuillantines at Place des Nations. Updates on the Health Assembly will be posted on the WHO website and relevant materials will be sent to the global media list. Register for updates. Photographs and videos WHO photos are available for media use via the WHO photo library.
translations.hypothesis: From June 22 to 26, the media center will be located at "Villa Les Feuillantines Place des Nations." Updates from the health conference will be posted on the World Health Organization website, and related issues will be disseminated to international media. Subscribe to receive news updates. For high-resolution photos and videos for media use, please visit the World Health Organization's image library.

translations.source: በዚህ ገጽ ላይ "የሚዲያ ምዝገባ" የሚለውን በመንካት ይመዝገቡ። ከፎቶዎች ጋር ለተያያዙ ጥያቄዎች ኢሜል ይጠቀሙ፤ የተቀዳ የስርጭት ቪዲዮ እና ሌሎች ይዘቶች ለመገናኛ ብዙሃን አገልግሎት ከፈለጉ በካንቶ “Canto” ላይ ይገኛሉ። ከቀጥታ ቪዲዮ ስርጭት ጋር ለተያያዙ ማናቸውም ጥያቄዎች፣ እባክዎን በኢሜል ይላኩ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት መደበኛ ዜናዎችን በትዊተር ያቀርባል፤ @WHO እና #WHA76 በማለት ይከተሉ። ከ#WHA76፡ Facebook፣ Instagram፣ LinkedIn፣ YouTube ዜናዎችን ለማግኘት የ WHOን ሌሎች የማህበራዊ ሚዲያ መለያዎችን ይከተሉ።
translations.reference: Register once for media access to the WHO photo library by clicking “media registration” on this page. For questions related to WHO photos email [email protected] Recorded broadcast video material and other press content will be available for media to download on Canto. For any questions related to live video transmission please email [email protected]. Social mediaWHO will provide regular updates from the World Health Assembly via Twitter –follow @WHO and hashtag #WHA76.Follow WHO’s other social media accounts for updates from #WHA76: Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube
translations.hypothesis: To register on this page, click on "Media Registration." For questions related to the images, please use email. If you are interested in using the recorded video and other content for media purposes, you can find them on "Canto." For any questions related to live video streaming, please email us. The World Health Organization provides regular news on Twitter; follow @WHO and #WHA76. To get news from #WHA76 on Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and YouTube, follow the WHO's other social media accounts.

translations.source: ከጤና ጋር የተያያዙ ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች እንዳይሳኩ ያስቆመውን ተግዳሮት ለመቅረፍ አስቸኳይ እርምጃ ያስፈልጋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት በኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ ተፅእኖ ላይ አዳዲስ አሃዞችን እንዲሁም ከጤና ጋር በተያያዘ ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦች ላይ ለመድረስ በሚደረገው እንቅስዋሴ ላይ አዳዲስ መረጃዎችን በመያዝ እ.ኤ.አ. የ2023 እትሙን እያወጣ ነው። እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2000-2015 ከታዩት እንቅስቃሴዎች ጋር ሲነጻጸር የአሁኑ እስከ እ.ኤ.አ. 2022 ያለው መረጃ በቅርብ ዓመታት ውስጥ ቁልፍ በሆኑ የጤና አመልካቾች ላይ የጤና መሻሻል መቀዛቀዝ እንዳለ ያሳያል። በተጨማሪም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ ያላቸውን ጉዳት በማጉላት የተቀናጀ እና ጠንካራ ምላሽ እንደሚያስፈልግ ያመለክታል። ኮቪድ-19 በጠፉ ህይወቶች እና በጤና መሻሻል ላይ ያስከፈለው ዋጋ፦ ዘገባው በአለም አቀፍ ጤና ላይ ወረርሽኙ ያስከተለውን አሃዝ አሻሽሏል፣ ይህም ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦችን ለማሳካት ያለው እድገት ማሽቆልቆል ላይ አስተዋፅዖ አድርጓል።
translations.reference: Urgent action needed to tackle stalled progress on health-related Sustainable Development Goals WHO is releasing the 2023 edition of its annual World Health Statistics report with new figures on the impact of COVID-19 pandemic and the latest statistics on progress towards the health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The report with data up to 2022 underscores a stagnation of health progress on key health indicators in recent years compared with trends seen during 2000-2015. It also alerts us to the growing threat of noncommunicable diseases (NCDs) and climate change, and calls for a coordinated and strengthened response. COVID-19 cost in lost lives and health progress The report documents updated statistics on the toll of the pandemic on global health, contributing to the ongoing decline in progress towards the SDGs.
translations.hypothesis: Urgent action is needed to address the challenge of preventing the achievement of long-term development goals related to health. The World Health Organization is releasing its 2023 report, which includes new data on the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and insights into the obstacles to achieving long-term health development goals. When compared to the trends observed from 2000 to 2015, the data from 2020 to 2022 indicates a setback in key health indicators in recent years. The report also highlights the need for a coordinated and robust response to the growing threat of non-communicable diseases and the impacts of climate change. The COVID-19 pandemic has had a significant impact on global health, and the report details the changes in health outcomes, which have contributed to the slowdown in progress towards long-term development goals.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2020-2021 ኮቪድ-19 በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ለ336.8 ሚሊዮን ሊኖሩ የሚችሉ አመታት መጥፋት ምክኒያት ሆኖል። ይህ ማለት ለእያንዳንዱ ሞት በአማካኝ ተጨማሪ 22 ሊኖሩ የሚችሉ አመታትን እንደማጣት ነው፤ ይህም በሚሊዮን የሚቆጠሩ ሰዎችን ህይወት ያሳጥራል። እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2000 ጀምሮ በእናቶች እና ህፃናት ጤና ላይ ከፍተኛ መሻሻሎችን አይተናል፤ በዚህም በቅደም ተከተል ሞት በአንድ ሶስተኛ እና በአንድ ሁለተኛ ቀንሷል። እንደ ኤች አይ ቪ፣ ሳንባ ነቀርሳ (ቲቢ) እና ወባ ያሉ ተላላፊ በሽታዎች የመከሰት አጋጣሚ እንዲሁም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች እና በጉዳት ምክንያት ያለጊዜው የሚመጣ የሞት መጠንም ቀንሷል። እነዚህ አንድ ላይ ሆነው፣ አማካኝ የእድሜ ጣሪያን እ.ኤ.አ. በ2000 67 ዓመታት ከነበረው እ.ኤ.አ. በ2019 ወደ 73 ዓመታት እንዲጨምር አስተዋፅዖ አድርገዋል።
translations.reference: During 2020-2021, COVID-19 resulted in a staggering 336.8 million years of life lost globally. This equates to an average of 22 years of life lost for every excess death, abruptly and tragically cutting short the lives of millions of people. Since 2000, we saw significant improvements in maternal and child health with deaths falling by one-third and one-half, respectively. The incidence of infectious diseases such as HIV, tuberculosis (TB) and malaria also declined, along with a lowered risk of premature deaths from NCDs and injuries. Together, these contributed to an increase in global life expectancy from 67 years in 2000 to 73 years in 2019.
translations.hypothesis:
The COVID-19 pandemic, which began in 2020, is estimated to have caused 336.8 million years of potential life lost globally. This means that, on average, each death resulted in the loss of 22 years of potential life. This represents a significant reduction in the average lifespan, particularly for younger populations.

Over the past two decades, we have seen significant improvements in the health of both adults and children. As a result, mortality rates have declined by one-third and one-half, respectively. The prevalence of infectious diseases such as HIV, tuberculosis, and malaria has also decreased, as has the incidence of non-communicable diseases and deaths due to injury.

These factors, taken together, have contributed to an increase in the average life expectancy from 67 years in 2000 to 73 years in 2019.

translations.source: ይሁን እንጂ ወረርሽኙ ከመጣ በኋላ ብዙ የጤና እርምጃዎችን ያባባሰ ሲሆን ጥራት ያለው የጤና እንክብካቤ፣ መደበኛ ክትባቶች እና የገንዘብ ድጋፍን ለማግኘት ፍትሃዊ ያልሆኑ ልዩነቶች ጨምረዋል። በዚህም ምክንያት የወባ እና የቲቢ ህክምና መሻሻል አዝማሚያዎች መልሰው ተባብሰዋል፣ እናም ጥቂት ሰዎች ብቻ ችላ ከተባሉ የሃሩሩ አካባቢ በሽታዎች ታክመዋል። “የዓለም ጤና አሃዝ ዘገባ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዓመታዊ የክትትል መንገድ ነው። ዘገባው ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎችን በተመለከተ ግልጽ ማስጠንቀቂያ ሰቷል፤ በሰው ህይወት፣ በኑሮ፣ በጤና ስርአት፣ በማህበረሰብ እና በኢኮኖሚ ላይ በከፍተኛ ጉዳትንም ያደርሳሉ።” ብለዋል የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ። "ዘገባው ዘላቂ የልማት ግቦችን ለማሳካት በጤና እና በጤና ስርዓቶች ላይ የሚደረጉ የገንዘብ ድጋፎች ላይ ከፍተኛ ጭማሪ እንዲያደርጉ ጠይቋል።"
translations.reference: However, the pandemic has put many health-related indicators further off-track and contributed to inequalities in access to high-quality health care, routine immunizations and financial protection. As a result, improving trends in malaria and TB have been reversed, and fewer people were treated for neglected tropical diseases (NTDs). “The World Health Statistics is WHO’s annual check-up on the state of the world’s health. The report sends a stark message on the threat of noncommunicable diseases, which take an immense and increasing toll on lives, livelihoods, health systems, communities, economies and societies,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “The report calls for a substantial increase in investments in health and health systems to get back on track towards the Sustainable Development Goals.”
translations.hypothesis: However, the war has disrupted many health initiatives, leading to disparities in access to quality healthcare, routine vaccinations, and essential medicines. As a result, progress in treating malaria and tuberculosis has stalled, and the incidence of neglected tropical diseases has increased in some areas. "The World Health Organization's annual report provides a clear warning about the impact of non-communicable diseases on global health," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO. "The report calls for increased investment in health and health systems to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals."

translations.source: ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ለመጪው ትውልድ ትልቅ የጤና ስጋት እየሆኑ ነው፤ ምንም እንኳን በአጠቃላይ ጤና ላይ መሻሻሎች ቢኖሩም ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ግን በየዓመቱ ያለማቋረጥ እያደጉ መተዋል እናም አሁን በየዓመቱ ሶስት አራተኛ የሚጠጋው ሞት ምክኒያት ናቸው። ይህ አዝማሚያ ከቀጠለ ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች በዚህ ክፍለ ዘመን አጋማሽ ላይ ከ90 ሚሊዮን አመታዊ ሞት ውስጥ 86 በመቶውን ይይዛሉ ይህም ማለት 77 ሚሊዮን ሞት ማለት ነው፤ እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2019 ጋር ሲነጽጸር በፍፁም ቁጥሮች ወደ 90% የሚጠጋ ጭማሪ ያሳያል። የዘገየውን እድገት ማፋጠንን ይጠይቃል፤ ተላላፊ ያልሆኑ በሽታዎች ብቻ ሳይሆን ሌሎች ብዙ አመላካቾች አመታዊ የመቀነስ ፍጥነት የመቀዛቀዝ ምልክቶች ያሳያሉ። ለምሳሌ የዓለም አቀፍ የእናቶች ሞት ጥምርታ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2021 እና 2030 መካከል በ11.6 በመቶ መቀነስ ይኖርበታል። በተመሳሳይ እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2015 እስከ 2021 የነበረው የቲቢ ክስተት መቀነስ የዓለም የጤና ድርጅት በሽታውን እ.ኤ.አ. በ2025 ለማጥፋት ካስቀመጠው ግብ ጋር ለመድረስ የተጓዘው አንድ አምስተኛ ብቻ ነበር።
translations.reference: NCDs ̶ an ever-increasing health threat for future generationsDespite overall health progress, the share of deaths caused annually by NCDs has grown consistently and is now claiming nearly three quarters of all lives lost each year. If this trend continues, NCDs are projected to account for about 86% of the 90 million annual deaths by mid-century; consequently, 77 million of these will be due to NCDs – a nearly 90% increase in absolute numbers since 2019. Stagnating progress calls for acceleration More recent trends show signs of slowdown in the annual rate of reduction (ARR) for many indicators. For example, the global maternal mortality ratio needs to decline by 11.6% per year between 2021 and 2030 to meet the SDG target. Similarly, the net reduction in TB incidence from 2015 to 2021 was only one-fifth of the way to the 2025 milestone of WHO’s End TB Strategy.
translations.hypothesis: Non-communicable diseases (NCDs) are a major public health concern for the coming generations. While overall health has improved, NCDs continue to rise each year, and now account for three-quarters of all deaths annually. If this trend continues, NCDs will be responsible for 86% of the 90 million annual deaths by mid-century, or 77 million deaths. This represents a staggering increase of nearly 90% compared to 2019. This highlights the urgent need to reverse this trend. Not only NCDs, but also other indicators, show a slowing rate of progress. For example, the Global Burden of Disease study projects a 11.6% reduction in maternal mortality between 2021 and 2030. Similarly, the World Health Organization estimates that only one in five of the 2025 goal to eliminate tuberculosis will be achieved.

translations.source: እንደ ትምባሆ መጠቀም፣ አልኮል መጠጣት፣ የወንጀል ጥቃት፣ የተበከለ ውሃ መጠቀም እና የህጻናት መቀንጨር አደጋዎችን በተመለከተ ተጋላጭነት ቢቀንስም በቂ ግን አልነበረም፤ አንዳንድ እንደ የአየር ብክለት ያሉ አደጋዎች ደግሞ ምንም ለውጥ ያላሳዩ ናቸው። በሚያስደነግጥ ሁኔታ ከመጠን ያለፈ ውፍረት በከፍተኛ ፍጥነት እየጨመረ ነው። በተጨማሪም የተሻለ የጤና አገልግሎቶች ተደራሽነት ከቅድመ እ.ኤ.አ. 2015 ግኝቶች ጋር ሲነፃፀር የቀነሰ ሲሆን በጤና አጠባበቅ ወጪዎች ምክንያት የገንዘብ ችግርን በመቀነስ ረገድ ምንም ጉልህ መሻሻል አላሳየም። ይህ እ.ኤ.አ. በ2030 ሁለንተናዊ የጤና ሽፋንን የማሳካት አቅማችንን በእጅጉ ይገድባል። “የኮቪድ-19 ወረርሽኝ እድገት ሁሌ የሚጨምር እንዳልሆነና ዋስትና እንደሌለው ማሳያ ነው።” ሲሉ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የመረጃና ጥናት ረዳት ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ሳሚራ አስማ አስጠንቅቀዋል።
translations.reference: Despite a reduction in exposure to many health risks – such as tobacco use, alcohol consumption, violence, unsafe water and sanitation, and child stunting – progress was inadequate and exposure to some risks such as air pollution remains high. Alarmingly, the prevalence of obesity is rising with no immediate sign of reversal. Furthermore, expanded access to essential health services has slowed compared to pre-2015 gains, coupled with no significant progress in reducing financial hardship due to health-care costs. This drastically limits our ability to achieve Universal Health Coverage by 2030. “The COVID-19 pandemic is an important reminder that progress is neither linear nor guaranteed,” warns Dr Samira Asma, WHO Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact.
translations.hypothesis: While some progress has been made in reducing the risks associated with tobacco use, alcohol consumption, violent crime, contaminated water, and child neglect, the overall picture remains inadequate. In fact, some risks, such as air pollution, have not shown any improvement. Alarmingly, obesity is increasing at an alarming rate. Furthermore, access to better healthcare has declined compared to the findings of the 2015 Sustainable Development Goals, and there has been no significant improvement in addressing financial barriers to healthcare. This significantly undermines our ability to achieve universal health coverage by 2030. "The COVID-19 pandemic has demonstrated that the risk of future outbreaks is not diminishing and that we must remain vigilant," warned Dr. Samira Asma, Director of Information and Research at the World Health Organization.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ. በ2030 የዘላቂ ልማት ግብ አጀንዳውን ለማሳካትና በሁሉም ሀገራት ሊለካ የሚችል ተፅእኖ ለማድረስ በቆራጥነት እና በጋራ መስራት አለብን። የዚህ አመት ዘገባ በአየር ንብረት ለውጥ እና በጤና ላይ ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ ራሱን የቻለ ክፍል ያካትታል፤ ይህም ወደፊት በሚወጣው ዘገባ ላይ የበለጠ ጠቃሚ እንደሚሆን እንጠብቃለን። ለዚህ እና ለሁሉም ሌሎች ጉዳዮች ወቅታዊ፣ አስተማማኝ እና ዝርዝር መረጃ እድገትን ለመከታተል እንዲሁም ብሄራዊ እና አለምአቀፍ የጤና ፖሊሲዎችን ለማሻሻል ወሳኝ ናቸው። የአርታዒ ማስታወሻ፡ የአለም ጤና አሃዝ ዘገባ የአለም ጤና ድርጅት አመታዊ በጤና እና ጤና ነክ አመላካቾች ላይ የቅርብ ጊዜ መረጃዎችን የሚያሰባስብበት መንገድ ነው። ለጥያቄዎች ኢሜል ይጠቀሙ።
translations.reference: “To stay on track towards the 2030 SDG agenda, we must act decisively and collectively to deliver a measurable impact in all countries.” This year’s report includes for the first time a dedicated section on climate change and health, and we anticipate that this will be of more relevance in the report going forward. For this issue and all other areas timely, reliable and disaggregated data are critical to track progress and improve national and global health policies. Editor’s note: The World Health Statistics report is the World Health Organization’s (WHO) annual compilation of the most recent available data on health and health-related indicators. For inquiries, contact [email protected]
translations.hypothesis: In order to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals by 2030 and have a lasting impact in all countries, we must work together with determination. This year's report includes a new section on climate change and health, which we believe will be even more valuable in future reports. Up-to-date, reliable, and detailed information is essential for tracking progress and improving national and international health policies. Note from the editor: The World Health Organization's annual report provides the latest data on health and health indicators. For questions, please email.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኃላፊ የአለም አቀፍ የጤና መሪዎች ሽልማቶችን አስታወቀ። ዣን ዣክ ሙዬምቤ-ታምፉም እና ፒተር ፒዮት በህይወት ዘመናቸው ለጤና ላደረጉት ትጋት ክብር ሰጥተዋል። በሰባ ስድስተኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኃላፊ ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ በዓለም ጤና ላይ ትልቅ ለውጥ ያመጡ ሰዎችን ለማክበር ሁለት ሽልማቶችን ይሰጣሉ። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2019 የተጀመረው የዳይሬክተሩ አለማቀፍ የመሪዎች ሽልማት ለዣን ዣክ ሙዬምቤ-ታምፉም እና ፒተር ፒዮት ይሰጣሉ። ሁለቱም የተከበሩ ተመራማሪዎች እና የህዝብ ጤና መሪዎች ናቸው፤ በአለም አቀፍ ጤና ውስጥ የመሪነት ቦታዎችን ከመያዛቸው በፊትም የኢቦላ ምርመራ አካል ነበሩ።
translations.reference: WHO Director-General announces Global Health Leaders Awards Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot awarded for lifelong commitment to health WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus will present two lifetime achievement awards to recognize outstanding contributions to advancing global health during the high-level opening session of the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly. The Director-General’s Global Leaders Awards, which were established in 2019, will go to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot. Both recipients are distinguished scientists and public health leaders who were closely involved in the discovery of Ebola virus disease before advancing to leadership positions in global health.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) Director announced the World Leaders in Global Health awards. Jean Jacquet Muyembe-Tama and Peter Piot were recognized for their contributions to global health during their careers. At the 70th World Health Assembly, WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus announced that two awards will be given to individuals who have made significant contributions to global health. The Director-General's Global Leaders in Global Health award, launched in 2019, will be presented to Jean Jacquet Muyembe-Tama and Peter Piot. Both are highly respected researchers and public health leaders, and they were both members of the Ebola research team before taking on leadership roles in global health.

translations.source: ሽልማቱ የሰዎችን ጤንነት በመጠበቅ የህይወት ዘመን ስኬቶቻቸውን ያከብራል። የዲሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ኮንጎ ፕሮፌሰር የሆኑት ሙዬምቤ-ታምፉም ይህንን ሽልማት የሚያገኙት ለፈጠራ ሃሳባቸው፣ ግንባር ቀደም መሪ በመሆናቸው እና እንደ ኢቦላ ያሉ በሽታዎችን ለማከም ትልቅ አስተዋፅዖ በማድረጋቸው ነው ። በዴሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ኮንጎ፣ በአፍሪካ እና በመላው አለም ጤና ላይ ትልቅ መሻሻል እንዲኖር ጥረቶችን አድርጓል። ፕሮፌሰር ሙዬምቤ-ታምፉም በኪንሻሳ የዲሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ብሔራዊ ምርምር ተቋም ኃላፊ ናቸው፤ በኪንሻሳ የሕክምና ትምህርት ቤት መምህር እና የኮንጎ ሳይንስ አካዳሚ የመጀመሪያ ፕሬዚዳንትም ናቸው። እ.ኤ.አ. በ1976 በኢኳተር ግዛት፣ በማዕከላዊ ዲሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ኮንጎ የመጀመሪያ የኢቦላ ወረርሽኝ ወቅት ዛየር ኢቦላቫይረስን ካገኙ ሰዎች አንዱ ነበር።
translations.reference: The award honours their lifetime achievements in public health. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is being recognized for his innovation, leadership and ground-breaking contributions to the management and treatment of diseases such as Ebola virus disease. He has played a critical role in improving health outcomes in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the African region, and the global community. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the Director General of the DRC’s National Institute of Biomedical Research in Kinshasa, full Professor of Microbiology at the University of Kinshasa Medical School and the inaugural president of the Congolese Academy of Science. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum was part of the team that discovered the Zaïre ebolavirus during the first recorded outbreak in 1976, in Equateur Province, Central DRC.
translations.hypothesis: The award recognizes his contributions to public health and his commitment to improving the lives of others. Professor Muyembe-Tamfu, a professor at the Democratic Republic of Congo, received the award for his innovative ideas, his leadership, and his significant contributions to combating diseases such as Ebola. He has made a significant impact on public health in the Democratic Republic of Congo, across Africa, and around the world. Professor Muyembe-Tamfu is the director of the National Research Institute in Kinshasa, a professor at the Faculty of Medicine in Kinshasa, and the first president of the Congolese Academy of Sciences. In 1976, he was one of the first people to identify the Zaire Ebola virus during the first outbreak of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

translations.source: በኋላ ቫይረሱን ለመከላከል ከረዱ የመጀመሪያ ሕክምናዎች እና ክትባቶች ምርምር ላይም ከፍተኛ አስተዋጾ አድርጓል። ዲሞክራቲክ ሪፐብሊክ ኮንጎን በሳይንስ የተሻለ ለማድረግ ጠንክሮ ሰርቷል፤ በሀገሪቱ ብዙ የምርምር ማዕከላትን አቋቁሞ በሽታዎችን ለመከላከል ረድቷል። የቤልጂየሙ ፕሮፌሰር ፒዮት ይህን ሽልማት ያገኘው ኤች አይ ቪ ኤድስን እና ሌሎች ትልልቅ የጤና ችግሮችን በመዋጋት ላሳዩት አስደናቂ አመራር ነው። እንደ የተባበሩት መንግስታት የኤድስ ፕሮግራም (UNAIDS) እና ሌሎች በመላው አለም ጤናን የተሻለ ያደረጉ ጠቃሚ የአለም ጤና መርሃ ግብሮች ትልቅ አካል ሆኖ ቆይቷል። የተባበሩት መንግስታት የኤድስ ፕሮግራምን በሚመራበት ጊዜ በዓለም ዙሪያ በኤድስ ላይ እርምጃ እንዲወስድ የሚገፋፉ መሪ ነበረ፤ በተጨማሪም 10 የተለያዩ የተባበሩት መንግስታት ድርጅት (ተመድ) ቡድኖችን በማሰባሰብ የተሻለ ትብብር ለማድረግ ረድቷል።
translations.reference: He later helped design one of the first effective treatments against the virus and the deployment of experimental Ebola virus disease vaccines. He has worked tirelessly to build the Democratic Republic of the Congo’s scientific capacity, establishing multiple research facilities in the country, and has played key roles with WHO in fighting infectious diseases. Professor Piot from Belgium is being recognized for his extraordinary leadership in the response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic and other significant global health challenges. He has played a vital role in launching major global health initiatives such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund, and others that have improved health outcomes around the world. Under his leadership, UNAIDS became the chief advocate for worldwide action against AIDS, also spearheading UN reform by bringing together 10 different UN organizations.
translations.hypothesis: He made significant contributions to the development of early treatments and vaccines to combat the virus, and also played a key role in research aimed at preventing the disease. The Democratic Republic of Congo has been committed to advancing science and research in the country, and has established numerous research centers to help combat diseases. Professor Piet from Belgium was awarded this prize for his outstanding leadership in fighting HIV/AIDS and other major health challenges. He has been a leading figure in global health initiatives, including the United Nations Programme on AIDS (UNAIDS), which has been instrumental in improving health outcomes around the world. As the director of UNAIDS, he has been a driving force in mobilizing action against AIDS globally, and has also helped to foster collaboration among a number of United Nations agencies.

translations.source: በተጨማሪም ፕሮፌሰር ፒዮት ቀደም ሲል የኢቦላ ቫይረስ እንዲገኝ እና ለመጀመሪያ ጊዜ የታወቀውን ወረርሽኝ በቁጥጥር ስር ለማዋል አስተዋፅኦ አድርገዋል፤ በመስክ የበሽት ስርጭት ምርመራዎች ውስጥ ይሳተፋል። በርካታ የከፍተኛ ደረጃ ተነሳሽነቶችን መርቷል፤ ኮቪድ-19ን ጨምሮ ብዙ ከፍተኛ ደረጃ የማማከር ሚናዎች ውስጥ አገልግሏል እና በርካታ ሽልማቶችንም አግኝቷል። ፕሮፌሰር ፒዮት በአሁኑ ጊዜ የአውሮፓ ሃገሮች ዓለማቀፍ ድርጅት ፕሬዝዳንት ልዩ አማካሪ ሲሆኑ የአለም ጤና ፕሮፌሰር እና የቀድሞ የለንደን የንፅህና እና የሃሩሩ አካባቢዎች በሽታ ህክምና ዳይሬክተር ሆነው አገልግለዋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ዋና ዳይሬክተር ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ “እነዚህ ድንቅ ግለሰቦች የዕድሜ ልክ ቁርጠኝነት፣ የማያቋርጥ የሰብዓዊ መብት ተሟጋችነት፣ ለፍትሃዊነት ቁርጠኝነት እና ከራስ ወዳድነት ነፃ የሆነ አገልግሎትን ያሳዩ ናቸው።” ብለዋል። “በብዙ ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች፣ የጤና ቀውሶች እና ግጭቶች ጊዜ በሕዝብ ጤና እና በሳይንስ ውስጥ ያለው አመራር ዓለም አቀፍ ጤናን ለማራመድ ከምንም በላይ ወሳኝ መሆኑን እንገነዘባለን።
translations.reference: Professor Piot also played a part in the earlier discovery of the Ebola virus, and the containment of the first known outbreak, participating in field epidemiological investigations. He has chaired a number of high-level initiatives, served in numerous high-level advisory roles, including on COVID-19, and has received multiple awards. Professor Piot is the Special Advisor to the EC President, a Professor of Global Health and former Director of the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. “These outstanding individuals embody lifelong dedication, relentless advocacy, a commitment to equity, and selfless service of humanity and people’s health,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “In a time of multiple emergencies, health crises, and conflict, we recognize that leadership in public health and science are more important than ever to advance global health.
translations.hypothesis: Professor Piot also played a key role in identifying and containing the Ebola virus outbreak, which was the first time the virus was discovered and brought under control. He has been involved in numerous high-level investigations and has served in advisory roles on a wide range of issues, including COVID-19. Professor Piot is currently the Special Advisor to the President of the World Health Organization and has previously served as the Director of the World Health Organization and the Director of the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine. Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the World Health Organization, said: "These remarkable individuals have demonstrated a lifelong commitment to public health and science, as well as a strong sense of social justice and a dedication to serving others. We recognize that in times of crisis, such as pandemics, natural disasters, and conflicts, leadership in public health and science is more important than ever in saving lives and promoting global health."

translations.source: የእኛ ተሸላሚዎች ዛሬ ያንን ትጋት ያሳያሉ።” "የእነሱ የጋራ ታሪኮቻቸው እና ስኬቶቻቸው በዓለም አቀፍ፣ በክልላዊ እና በብሔራዊ ደረጃ ትብብርን ማጎልበት የጤና ድንገተኛ አደጋዎች ዝግጁነት እና ምላሽ መስጠትን ለማሳደግ ምን ያህል መሻሻል እንዳመጣ ያሳያል። እነዚህ ልዩ ተመራማሪዎች በስራቸው በየቀኑ እኛን ማነሳሳታቸውን ይቀጥላሉ። ”ሲል አክሏል። የሽልማት ሥነ ሥርዓቱ እ.ኤ.አ. እሑድ ግንቦት 21 ቀን 2023 በጄኔቫ የሚካሄደው 76ኛው የዓለም ጤና ጉባኤ ከፍተኛ ደረጃ የመክፈቻ ስብሰባ አካል ነው።
translations.reference: Our awardees today personify that passion.” “Their combined stories and successes show how much the world has progressed in terms of collaboration at the global, regional and country levels to strengthen health emergency preparedness, response and resilience. These outstanding scientists will continue to inspire us every day,” he added The ceremony for the awards form part of the high-level opening session of the 76th World Health Assembly being held on Sunday 21 May 2023 in Geneva.
translations.hypothesis: "Our awardees are demonstrating that today. Their shared experiences and successes show how collaboration at the international, regional, and national levels can improve preparedness and response to global health emergencies. These remarkable individuals will continue to inspire us with their work." he added. The award ceremony is part of the 76th World Health Assembly, which is being held in Geneva on June 21, 2023.

translations.source: የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ቤኒን እና ማሊ ትራኮማን (በባክቴሪያ የሚመጣ የዓይን በሽታ) ከህዝብ ጤና ስጋት ስላጠፉት አድንቋል። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ቤኒን እና ማሊ ትራኮማን (በባክቴሪያ የሚመጣ የዓይን በሽታ) ከህዝብ ጤና ስጋት ማጥፋታቸውን ያረጋገጠ ሲሆን ይህም በአለም ጤና ድርጅት የአፍሪካ ክልል አምስተኛ እና ስድስተኛ ሃገራት አድርጓቸዋል ። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ትራኮማ እንደሌላቸው የገለጸባቸው አገሮች ጋና (እ.ኤ.አ. ሰኔ 2018)፣ ጋምቢያ (እ.ኤ.አ. ሚያዝያ 2021)፣ ቶጎ (እ.ኤ.አ. ግንቦት 2022) እና ማላዊ (እ.ኤ.አ. መስከረም 2022) ናቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ኃላፊ ዶ/ር ቴዎድሮስ አድሃኖም ገብረእየሱስ "ለቤኒን እና ማሊ እንዲሁም ከትራኮማ በሽታ እንዲላቀቁ የረዷቸውን አጋሮች ሁሉ እንኳን ደስ አላችሁ ብለዋል። "ከቤኒን እና ማሊ ስኬት በኋላ አሁንም በአፍሪካ 23 የትራኮማ ችግር ያለባቸው ሀገራት አሉ፤ ነገርግን እ.ኤ.አ. ከ2021 እስከ 2030 ድረስ ችላ የተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ዕቅድ መሰረት በሽታውን ለማስወገድ እየተቃረብን ነው።
translations.reference: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.”
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared Benin and Mali free of trachoma, a bacterial eye disease that poses a significant public health threat. The WHO confirmed that both countries have eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in Africa, respectively, to achieve this milestone. The WHO also listed other countries that have been declared free of trachoma, including Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022), and Malawi (September 2022). WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said, "I congratulate Benin and Mali on their achievements in eliminating trachoma. With the success of Benin and Mali, there are still 23 countries in Africa where trachoma remains a public health problem. However, we are on track to eliminate trachoma as part of the Global Elimination of Reversible Blindness (GERB) program, which aims to eliminate preventable blindness by 2030."

translations.source: በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ቤኒን እና ማሊ አሁን ከ15 ትራኮማን ከህብረተሰብ የጤና ችግር ካጠፍ ሃገራት ጋር ተቀላቅለዋል። እነዚህም ካምቦዲያ፣ ቻይና፣ ጋምቢያ፣ ጋና፣ ኢራን፣ ላኦስ፣ ማላዊ፣ ሜክሲኮ፣ ሞሮኮ፣ ምያንማር፣ ኔፓል፣ ኦማን፣ ሳዑዲ አረቢያ፣ ቶጎ እና ቫኑዋቱ ናቸው። የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት የ”ሴፍ” SAFE ስልት፤ ቤኒን እና ማሊ ትራኮማን ለመግታት የሴፍ (SAFE) ስልትን ተግባራዊ አድርገዋል። የ”ሴፍ” ስልት ማለት በትራኮማ የሚመጡ ችግሮችን ለማስተካከል ቀዶ ጥገና፣ በሽታውን ለማስቆም የመድሃኒት ህክምና፣ የፊት ንጽህና መጠበቅ እና አካባቢን የተሻለ ማድረግን የሚይዝ ምህጻረ ቃል ነው። (SAFE- Surgery for trichiasis, Antibiotic therapy, Facial cleanliness and Environmental change)
translations.reference: Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.
translations.hypothesis: Benin and Mali are now among the countries working to eliminate trachoma, a leading cause of preventable blindness, from the ranks of countries with the highest rates of the disease. Other countries with high rates of trachoma include Cambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, Iran, Laos, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo, and Vanuatu. The World Health Organization's "SAFE" strategy – Surgery for trichiasis, Antibiotic therapy, Facial cleanliness and Environmental change – is being implemented in Benin and Mali to combat trachoma. SAFE refers to a comprehensive approach that includes surgical correction of the affected eyelids, antibiotic treatment to prevent the spread of the infection, maintaining good hygiene, and improving the environment.

translations.source: ፋይዘር (Pfizer ) የተባለው የመድሀኒት አምራች ድርጅት ትራኮማን ለማጥፋት ለሚሰሩ ቡድኖች በነጻ azithromycin (የትራኮማ መድሃኒት) ይሰጣል። በብሔራዊ የበሽታ መከላከል ፕሮግራም ስር ቤኒን ትራኮማን ለማስወገድ ጥረቶችን ከሌሎች ችላ የተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ጋር እንድ ላይ ለመዋጋት ጥረቶችን አድርጋለቸ። ቤኒን ትራኮማን ከማጥፋቷ በፊት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2009 ድራኩንኩላይስስ (ወይም የጊኒ ወርም በመባል የሚታወቀውን የተበከለ ውሃ በመጠጣት የሚተላለፍ በሽት) እና እ.ኤ.አ. በ2021 ጋምቢይንዝ ሂውማን አፍሪካን ትራይፓኖሶሚሲስ (ወይም የእንቅልፍ በሽታ በመባል የሚታወቀውን በነፍሳት አማካኝነት የሚተላለፍ በሽት) ማጥፋት ችላለች። ማሊ በአገሪቷ አንዳንድ አካባቢዎች በሚከሰቱ ግጭቶች እና በመንግስት ላይ በተደረጉ ለውጦች ብዙ ፈተናዎች ቢኖሩባትም ትራኮማ የሰዎችን ጤና ምን ያህል እንደሚጎዳ ተመልክታ ጉዳዩን ለማስቆም ችላለች። ለማሊ ትራኮማ ችላ ከተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ማጥፋት የቻለችው የመጀመሪያ በሽታ ነው፤ በዚህም ቢያንስ አንድ ችላ የተባለ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ማስቆም ከቻሉ 47 አገሮች ጋር ተቀላቅላለች ።
translations.reference: Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD.
translations.hypothesis: Pfizer, a pharmaceutical company, is providing azithromycin (a medication used to treat trachoma) for free to groups working to eliminate the disease. As part of the national disease control program, they are working to combat trachoma alongside other neglected tropical diseases. Before trachoma was eradicated, it was a major cause of blindness in Benin, along with diseases like dracunculiasis (also known as guinea worm) and lymphatic filariasis (also known as elephantiasis). Despite facing challenges such as conflicts and political instability in some parts of the country, Benin has been successful in eliminating trachoma. It is the first country in Africa to eradicate a neglected tropical disease, and it is working with at least 47 other countries to eliminate at least one neglected tropical disease.

translations.source: "ይህ ለህብረተሰብ ጤና መሻሻል ትልቅ ስኬት ነው።" ብለዋል ዶ/ር ኢብራሂማ ሶሴ ፎል የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ችላ የተባሉ የሐሩር ክልል በሽታዎች ፕሮግራም ኃላፊ። "የቤኒን እና የማሊ ስኬት መሪዎች፣ የተለያዩ ዘርፎች እና ማህበረሰቡ ተባብረው ከሰሩ በሽታዎችን ለማስቆም እንዴት ጠቃሚ እንደሆነ ያሳያሉ።" ትራኮማን በመዋጋት ረገድ በአለፉት ዓመታት ትልቅ መሻሻሎች እየታዩ ነው። በአፍሪካ ለትራኮማ መድኃኒት የሚያስፈልጋቸው ሰዎች ቁጥር እ.ኤ.አ. በ2014 ከነበረው 189 ሚሊዮን እ.ኤ.አ. በሰኔ 2022 ወደ 105 ሚሊዮን፤ ይህም ማለት በ84 ሚሊዮን ቀንሷል። ለአዘጋጁ ማስታወሻ፦ እ.ኤ.አ. እስከ ሰኔ 2022 ድረስ ትራኮማ በ41 ሀገራት እንደ ትልቅ የማህበረሰብ የጤና ችግር ሆኖ ቀጥሏል፤ በእነዚያ ቦታዎች ወደ 125 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ ሰዎች እርዳታ ያስፈልጋቸዋል።
translations.reference: “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease.
translations.hypothesis: "This is a major achievement for public health," said Dr. Ibrahim Sose, head of the trachoma control program at the World Health Organization's office in Harar. "The success stories of Benin and Mali demonstrate the importance of collaboration between different sectors and communities in the fight against trachoma." Significant progress has been made in trachoma control in recent years. The number of people in Africa in need of trachoma treatment decreased from 18.9 million in June 2014 to 10.5 million in June 2022, a decrease of 8.4 million. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a major public health problem in 41 countries in Africa as of June 2022, with an estimated 12.5 million people in need of treatment in those areas.

translations.source: ትራኮማ በአብዛኛው በአፍሪካ፣ በመካከለኛው እና በደቡብ አሜሪካ፣ በእስያ፣ በምዕራብ ፓሲፊክ እና በመካከለኛው ምስራቅ በሚገኙ ዝቀተኛ ገቢ ባላቸው እና ሩቅ በሆኑ አካባቢዎች በብዛት ይገኛል። ከነዚህም አፍሪካ ትልቁን ድርሻ ትይዛለች፤ ወደ 105 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉ የትራኮማ ተጠቂዎች ሲኖሩ ይህም በዓለም ላይ ካሉት የትራኮማ ተጠቂዎች 84% ይይዛል። በዓለም አቀፍ ደረጃ በበሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን ምክንያት የሚመጣ አይነ ስውርነት ቀዳሚው ምክኒያት ትራኮማ ነው። “ክላሚዲያ ትራኮማቲስ” የተባለ በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያን በሽታውን ያመጣል። በሽታ አምጪ ተህዋሲያኑ ከሰው ወደ ሰው በንክኪ (አይን ወይም አፍንጫቸውን ከነኩ) ይተላለፋል፤ ከሰው ንክኪም በተጨማሪ ዝንብ ሊሰራጭው ይችላል።
translations.reference: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person.
translations.hypothesis: Chlamydia is most commonly found in low-income and remote areas of Africa, the Middle East, and South America, as well as in parts of Asia, the Pacific, and the Middle East. Africa accounts for the largest proportion of chlamydia cases, with an estimated 10.5 million people infected, representing 84% of the global chlamydia population. Globally, chlamydia is the most common sexually transmitted infection (STI). It is caused by a bacterium called "Chlamydia trachomatis." The bacteria are typically transmitted from person to person through sexual contact (by touching the eyes or genitals), but can also be spread from an infected animal to a person.

translations.source: እንደ እጅን በደንብ አለመታጠብ፣ በተጨናነቁ ቦታዎች መኖር እና ንጹህ ውሃ ወይም መጸዳጃ ቤት አለማግኘት ያሉ ሁኔታዎች በሽታው እንዲዛመት ያደርጋሉ። እ.ኤ.አ. በ1996 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እ.ኤ.አ. በ2020 ትራኮማን ለማጥፋት የሚሰራ ቡድን አቋቋሞ ነበር። ቡድኑን ከሚደግፉ ሌሎች አጋሮች ጋር በመሆን የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ትራኮማን ለማስቆም ”ሴፍ” (SAFE) የተሰኘ ስልት ሀገራት እንዲተገብሩ ይረዳል፤ በተጨማሪም የትራኮማን ስርጭት እንዲፈትሹ፣ እንዲከታተሉ እና የገንዘብ ድጋፍ እንዲያገኙ ያግዟቸዋል። እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2020 የዓለም ጤና ድርጅት ፍኖተ ካርታ እንደ አውሮፓውያኑ አቆጣጠር በ2030 ትራኮማን ለማጥፋት አዲስ እቅድ ያዟል።
translations.reference: Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.hypothesis: Factors such as poor hygiene, living in unsanitary conditions, and lack of access to clean water or sanitation facilities can contribute to the spread of the disease. In 1996, the World Health Organization established a task force to combat trachoma. Working with other partners, the task force helps countries implement a "SAFE" strategy, which aims to eliminate trachoma through surgery, antibiotics, and environmental improvements. In 2020, the World Health Organization announced a new goal to eliminate trachoma by 2030, according to the European calendar.

translations.source: 152 ሚሊዮን ሕፃናት ባለፉት አስር አመታት ውስጥ ከጊዜአቸው በፊት ነው የተወለዱት ። ከሚወለዱም 10 ሕፃናት፣ 1ዱ ያለጊዜው የሚወለድ ነው - እናም በየ 40 ሰከንዱ ከእነዚህ ህጻናት ውስጥ 1ዱ ይሞታል ። ያለጊዜ ወሊድ መጠን ባለፉት አስር አመታት በየትኛውም የአለም ክልል ውስጥ ለውጥ አላሳየም። የግጭት፣ የአየር ንብረት ለውጥ፣ የኮቪድ-19 ተጽእኖ በየቦታው ለሴቶች እና ህጻናት ስጋቶች እየጨመረ ነው። በዚህ ለተጎዱት ሕፃናት እና ቤተሰባቸን የተሻለ እንክብካቤ ከማድረግ ጎን ለጎን ያለጊዜ ወሊድን መከላከልን ለማሻሻል አስቸኳይ እርምጃ ያስፈልጋል።
translations.reference: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families
translations.hypothesis: Over the past decade, 152 million children have been born preterm. Of the 10 million children born each year, approximately 1 million are born preterm – and about 1 in 40 of these infants will die. The rate of preterm birth has not changed significantly in any region of the world over the past decade. Factors such as conflict, climate change, and the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic are increasingly raising concerns about the health of mothers and infants. Urgent action is needed to improve the care of preterm infants and their families, as well as to reduce the incidence of preterm birth.

translations.source: እ.ኤ.አ በ2020 ወደ 13.4 ሚሊዮን የሚገመቱ ሕፃናት ከጊዜያቸው በፊት የተወለዱ ሲሆን፣ ወደ 1 ሚሊዮን የሚጠጉት ደግሞ ከወሊድ በፊት በሚከሰቱ ችግሮች ህይወታቸውን አጥተዋል ሲል የተባበሩት መንግስታት ኤጀንሲዎች እና አጋሮች ዛሬ ይፋ ባደረጉት አዲስ ዘገባ አሳውቀዋል ። ይህም በአለም አቀፍ ደረጃ ከጊዜያቸው በፊት ከሚወለዱት (ማለትም ከ37 ሳምንታት እርግዝና በፊት) ከ10 ህጻናት 1ዱ ጋር በአንፃራዊው እኩል ነው። ያለጊዜ መወለድ፡- የአለም ጤና ድርጅት እና የተባበሩት መንግስታት የህጻናት ገንዘብ ለጋሽ ድርጅት ከእናቶች፣ ጨቅላ ህጻናትና ልጆች ጤና ማህበር ጋር በመተባበር በሴቶች፣ ህጻናት እና ታዳጊዎች ላይ በአለም ለአስር አመታት ያለጊዜ መወለድ ላይ ሲሰራ ቆይቷል ፣ ከጊዜ በፊት መወለድ “ ትኩረት ያላገኘ አሳሳቢ አደጋ” ነው በሚል የማንቂያ ጥሪን አድርገዋል ፣ በመጠኑ እና በአሳሳቢነቱ ለረጅም ጊዜ ትኩረት ሳያገኝ የቆየ ሲሆን ፣ ይህም የልጆችን ጤና እና ሕልውና የመሻሻል እድገትን እያደናቀፈ ነው። ዘገባው ከዓለም ጤና ድርጅት እና ዩኒሴፍ የተሻሻለውን ጥናታዊ ግምቶች ያካትታል፣ አስቀድሞ ያለጊዜ መወለድ ስርጭት ላይ የተሰራ ሲሆን፣ ከለንደን የንጽህና እና የሀሩር ክልል ህክምና ትምህርት ቤት ጋር በመተባበር የተዘጋጀ ነው። ባጠቃላይም፣ እ.ኤ.አ ከ2010 እስከ 2020 ድረስ 152 ሚሊየን ተጋላጭ ህጻናት ያለጊዜያቸው መወለዳቸውን እና፣ ባለፉት አስርት አመታት በአለም ላይ በየትኛውም ክልል ውስጥ ያለ ያለጊዜ ወሊድ መጠን ምንም ለውጥ እንዳላመጣ አረጋግጧል።
translations.reference: An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.hypothesis: According to a new report released today by the United Nations and its partners, an estimated 13.4 million babies were born preterm in 2020, and over 1 million died as a result of complications related to preterm birth. This represents approximately 10% of all births worldwide, which is similar to the global average. Preterm birth is considered a significant and preventable risk, and the World Health Organization (WHO) and its partners have been working for over a decade to raise awareness of the issue and to improve the health and well-being of mothers, newborns, and children. The report includes data from the WHO and UNICEF, as well as from the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, which has been conducting research on preterm birth for over 20 years. The report also highlights the fact that preterm birth is a leading cause of infant mortality worldwide, and that there has been little progress in reducing the rate of preterm birth in any region of the world over the past decade.

translations.source: ያለጊዜ መወለድ በአሁኑ ጊዜ የህጻናት ሞት ዋነኛ መንስኤ ሲሆን ይህም ከ 5 ኛ የልደት በዓላቸው በፊት ከሚሞቱት ህጻናት ከ5ቱ ከ 1 በላይ የሚሆኑት እነዚህ ህጻናት ናቸው። ያለጊዜ ወሊድ ተጠቂ ሆነው የተረፉ ሰዎች የዕድሜ ልክ የጤና መዘዝ ፣ የአካል ጉዳት ሰለባ የመሆን እድላቸው ከፍ ያለ እና የእድገት መዘግየት ሊያጋጥሟቸው ይችላል። በክልል፣ በገቢ፣ በዘር አኳያ ያሉ የመዳን ክፍተቶች እ.ኤ.አ. በ 2012 በርዕሱ ላይ ካለው ጉልህ ዘገባ በመነሳት ፣ ይህ 'ከጊዜው በፊት አዲስ የተወለደው “አስርት ዓመታት ”' የተሰኘው ዘገባ ያለጊዜ መወለድን ስርጭት እና በሴቶች ፣ በቤተሰቦች ፣ በማህበረሰቦች እና በኢኮኖሚዎች ላይ ስላለው ከፍተኛ ተፅእኖ አጠቃላይ እይታን ይሰጣል ። በጣም ብዙ ጊዜ፣ ህጻናት የሚወለዱበት ቦታ በሕይወት የመትረፋቸውን እጣ ይወስናል።
